Title: Data & Platform Analyst (m/w/d)
Description:
NeXtWind betreibt und entwickelt Windenergieanlagen in Deutschland mit dem Ziel, bis 2028 auf 3 GW zu wachsen. Unser Data & IT-Team baut die interne Dateninfrastruktur als Produkt: Airtable als operativer Kern, automatisierte Prozesse und AI-Use-Cases in der Pipeline. Du übernimmst Verantwortung für unsere Datenplattform — von der Architektur bis zur Adoption.




Deine Aufgaben




Datenplattform

Airtable-Architektur weiterentwickeln, pflegen und dokumentieren (Bases, Interfaces, Automations, Skripte)
Datenmodelle designen: Feldstandards, Naming Conventions, Tabellenbeziehungen — konsistent und zukunftsfähig
Schnittstellenmanagement ausbauen: Interfaces für Fachabteilungen bauen und iterieren
Automatisierte Reports aufsetzen und betreiben
Datenqualitätsprozesse definieren und gemeinsam mit den Teams einführen
AI- und Automatisierungs-Use-Cases identifizieren und mitumsetzen
Self-Service-Zugang für Fachabteilungen ausbauen




Dein Profil




Muss

Airtable: tiefes Verständnis von Bases, Interfaces, Automations und Scripting
Datenarchitektur: strukturiertes Denken in Datenmodellen, Abhängigkeiten und Standards
Sehr gutes Deutsch (Unternehmenssprache, schriftlich und mündlich)
Gutes Englisch (Teamkommunikation, externe Partner)
Eigenverantwortung: du bringst Dinge zu Ende, auch ohne Micromanagement

Darüber hinaus ist unser Unternehmen auf einem Fundament stabiler Kernwerte gebaut, die unsere Kultur definieren und prägen.




Transparenz und Eigeninitiative stehen bei uns hoch im Kurs. Der respektvolle Umgang miteinander - intern wie extern - ist die unverzichtbare Grundlage unseres Handelns. In unserer Zusammenarbeit sind wir schnell und offen für unvermeidbare Fehler auf dem Weg zur Verbesserung. Kollegialität und gegenseitige Unterstützung sind bei uns selbstverständlich.




Wir bieten unseren Mitarbeitenden jederzeit Möglichkeiten zur Entwicklung und zum Wachstum, basierend auf ihren individuellen Fähigkeiten und Neigungen. Flexibilität, so zu arbeiten, wie es optimale Produktivität und Effizienz ermöglicht, ist für uns selbstverständlich.

Die Möglichkeit, aktiv an der Energiewende mitzuwirken und einen positiven Einfluss auf die Umwelt zu nehmen




Bringe frischen Wind in Deine Karriere.

Title: Working Student – GIS (m/f/d)
Description:
Unternehmensbeschreibung

Established in 2002, ib vogt GmbH is specialized in the Development, Design and Engineering, Financing, EPC and Operation of solar power and provides turnkey solar power plant solutions to investors internationally.

As a manufacturer-independent integrated developer, the company focuses on tailor-made solar power plant solutions that maximize lifecycle performance and returns.

Since 2009, ib vogt has commissioned plants with a capacity of almost 2 Gigawatt. We employ over 840 specialists, in all areas of the solar power plant value chain in over 40 countries. The company operates internationally from offices in Spain Germany, Italy, Netherlands, France, Poland, USA, Australia, United Kingdom, Panama, India and South East Asia.

Stellenbeschreibung

The working student inside the GIS-team is supporting the sourcing, maintenance, and provision of geographical data required within BD Germany. The role also includes active involvement in identifying suitable areas for large-scale PV and BESS projects, obtaining landowner information, and creating maps for acquisition and contractual purposes.


Support GIS-based screenings for large-scale hybrid PV+BESS projects using QGIS, ArcGIS and ArcGIS Online
Assist with sourcing, maintaining and preparing geographical data
Support the ordering, processing and provision of landowner and parcel information
Create and update maps for acquisition, planning and contractual purposes
Help maintain GIS databases and structure project-related geodata
Support the preparation of reliable and traceable map material
Assist with online tools such as ArcGIS Online and the internal CRM system
Support the GIS team in day-to-day tasks and further GIS-related activities
Conduct research on publicly available geodata and support data requests from public authorities where required
take part in assessing and deepening the implementation of AI features for GIS purposes


Qualifikationen


Enrolled Bachelor’s or Master’s student in geography, geoinformatics, spatial planning, environmental planning, surveying, renewable energies or a related GIS field
Good basic understanding of geographical information systems, geodata and databases
First practical experience with GIS software such as QGIS, ArcGIS and/or ArcGIS Online
Initial experience with database maintenance, structuring or analysis is preferred
Basic knowledge of query or programming languages such as SQL and/or Python is desirable
First experience with automation processes in GIS or data workflows is advantageous
Conscientious, structured and reliable working style, especially when handling sensitive parcel, landowner and project data
Certifications or completed courses in QGIS, ArcGIS, SQL or Python are a plus, but not mandatory
Very good German skills, both written and spoken; English skills are desirable
Interest in renewable energies, spatial analysis and the development of large-scale PV and BESS projects
Availability of approximately 20 hours per week


Zusätzliche Informationen


A truly international working environment with colleagues from all over the world
An open-minded, friendly, dynamic, and highly motivated team
Interesting and challenging tasks, and thus a scope of professional growth and development
We encourage both team work and personal responsibility
Very good opportunities for professional and personal development
Competitive remuneration, and other exciting benefits


At ib vogt, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and respectful workplace where everyone can thrive. We believe that embracing diverse perspectives drives innovation and helps us better serve the global communities we operate in. As an equal opportunity employer, we provide employment opportunities without regard to age, ethnicity, religion or belief, gender identity, sex, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy or maternity, marriage and civil partnership, or any other protected status.

We strive to create an accessible recruitment process for all candidates and are happy to discuss reasonable accommodations to support your application. As a global company, we acknowledge that accessibility provisions may vary by location depending on local infrastructure and legal requirements. If you have a disability or require any adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know - we will do our best to support you.

Title: Senior Data Engineer
Description:
We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer at GLS/NXT to ensure the reliability and integrity of our growing data infrastructure and analytics platform.




In this role, you will work at the intersection of data engineering, product, and operations - driving quality across our data pipelines, models, and warehouse architecture. You’ll design and implement testing strategies, proactively identify data quality issues, and collaborate with teams to build scalable, well-documented solutions. Your focus will be on enabling accurate, trustworthy data for analytics and reporting while supporting the broader mission of GLS/NXT to deliver innovative, data-driven digital products.


Tasks



Collaborate across functions to scale GLS/NXT data infrastructure.


Contribute to the roadmap and development of GLS/NXT data products.


Design, build, monitor, and scale data pipelines for Data Warehousing, integrating various data sources and destinations.


Develop data models and schemas supporting analytical requirements.


Work with other engineering teams to resolve architecture and infrastructure challenges, designing effective solutions.


Participate in cross-functional projects supporting data applications and reporting.


Address data quality issues, implementing best practices.


Create and maintain comprehensive systems documentation.



Requirements



Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related engineering field.


5+ years of proven experience in data engineering roles.


Expertise in designing, building, monitoring, and scaling data engineering pipelines.


Hands-on experience with workflow orchestration tools such as Apache Airflow for designing, scheduling, and managing complex data pipelines.


Proficient in modern data programming languages, including Python and Go.


Extensive hands-on experience with cloud platforms, specifically AWS and GCP.


Proficient with data warehousing technologies, including Google BigQuery and Snowflake.


Proficient in SQL for diverse data sources, leveraging tools such as DBT for data modeling, schema design, incremental strategies, and query optimization.


Looker and LookML expertise, with a focus on data preparation and modeling to support analysis and efficient visualizations.


Software development operations management experience, including CI/CD pipelines, grounded in agile methodologies


Strong English communication skills for effective cross-functional collaboration


Highly organized with the ability to prioritize tasks and manage projects aligned to business goals


Skilled at setting clear priorities to drive project success



Benefits



Dynamic workplace: A digital environment with international projects and space to learn and grow


Flexible work conditions: Hybrid working mode with a tailored remote policy


Focus on learning: A personal budget (1.000€) for learning and development initiatives


Physical space for ideas: A great office space in Berlin Mitte with top-tier equipment


Transparent and competitive compensation: A salary package that reflects your expertise and experience.


Active participation: A clear bonus system enabling participation in your and the team’s performance


Engaging team culture: Monthly team cooking, regular events and offsites


Vacation policy: 30 days of yearly holiday


Reward system: Rewards for the recommendation of additional team members





Even if you do not meet every single requirement, we encourage you to apply if you believe you are well-suited for the role and can contribute meaningfully to our team.




About GLS/NXT




GLS/NXT was founded with the mission to build the next generation of logistic experiences driven by technology - core, adjacent & beyond. More specifically, our sphere of interest is the Logistics and eCommerce sector. We are entrepreneurs and hence always start with the user's needs. We are located between Corporate and Startups and leverage the benefits from both (startup speed and corporate muscle). GLS/NXT inhabits different project teams, acting as startups with business responsibility and setting their own vision and mission. GLS/NXT is part of the GLS IT Services GmbH.




Commitment to Diversity




GLS/NXT is an equal opportunity employer, which means we do not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Title: Web Developer Intern
Description:
About Alpine Business Consulting
Alpine Business Consulting is a specialized team that steps in when enterprise reporting systems fail to deliver. Many companies invest heavily in complex planning tools only to find that poor implementation has left them with mismatched numbers and long month-end closes. We fix these systems. Our consultants use their real-world experience to configure tools properly, providing clients with a single source of truth that their finance teams can actually trust.
Currently, a major German logistics provider is expanding its tracking and financial reporting infrastructure. We are assisting them in building out advanced performance management modules to handle their growing data needs. We need a Web Developer Intern who can help us translate complex datasets into functional, clear web interfaces and internal tools for this project and others.

About the role
This is a focused, technical internship for someone who wants to see how web technology supports large-scale business operations. This position is fully remote, meaning you will work from your own environment while staying in constant contact with our senior team. You will not be building marketing sites or flashy animations. Instead, you will help create the dashboards and interfaces that allow executives to make decisions based on clean data.
You will be paired with senior consultants who prioritize working systems over corporate jargon. We value documentation that is easy to read and code that is easy to maintain. This role is about learning how to build tools that last and ensuring that every project ends with a smooth handover to the client team.

What you will do
Assist in building and maintaining web-based dashboards using HTML, CSS, and modern JavaScript frameworks.
Collaborate with our data specialists to ensure that front end components accurately reflect the underlying business logic.
Draft clear documentation and configuration guides that explain how our custom tools are structured.
Participate in internal reviews to ensure all web components meet our standards for reliability and clarity.
Help test user interfaces across different devices to ensure consistent performance for our clients.
Support the team in setting up data visualizations that simplify complex logistical information.
Create simple, functional internal tools to help our consultants manage project workflows more effectively.



What we need from you
Basic proficiency in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. You should be able to build a functional webpage from a provided design or set of requirements.
Current enrollment in a computer science, web development, or related degree program, or recent completion of similar studies.
A preference for clean, predictable code over complex or experimental solutions.
Strong written communication skills, especially the ability to write instructions and documentation clearly.
Interest in how business data is structured and presented for professional use.
The ability to work independently in a remote setting while staying disciplined with your tasks and deadlines.
Familiarity with Git or other version control systems is helpful but not required.



What the role offers
This is a paid internship with a compensation package that is competitive for the contemporary market. We offer a direct look into the world of high-level business consulting without the usual corporate fluff. You will work on real projects, including the expansion for the logistics provider mentioned earlier, and your contributions will go directly into the tools our clients use every day.
Since the role is fully remote, you will have the flexibility to manage your workspace and avoid any commute. You will benefit from the mentorship of senior staff who have years of experience with Oracle EPM, SAP BPC, and modern analytics stacks. We believe in providing our interns with substantive work that builds actual skills in configuration and software development.

How we work
At Alpine Business Consulting, we value transparency and predictability. We provide fixed-price quotes to our clients and we keep our internal processes just as direct. We do not use buzzwords like synergy or disruption. We talk about clean data, reliable reporting, and thorough documentation. We want to finish a project knowing the client is well-trained and capable of running their new system independently. As an intern, you will be expected to adopt this same honest, helpful approach to every task you take on.

Title: Assistant Manager (m/w/d)
Description:
Du willst Teil der LA FAMIGLIA werden?
Dann definiere gemeinsam mit uns Gastfreundschaft und Geschmack neu! In mittlerweile elf europäischen Ländern sind wir ständig auf der Suche nach Talenten!



DAS SIND UNSERE EXTRA-ZUTATEN FÜR DICH

L’Osteria Speedway: vom Schichtleiter zum Betriebsleiter in nur 2,5 Jahren 🚀
Attraktive Bezahlung nach Tarif, sowie zusätzliche steuerfreie Zuschläge für deinen Geldbeutel
Kostenlose Mitarbeiterverpflegung, unser Rundum-sorglos-Paket für deine Gaumen
Unbefristeter Arbeitsvertrag für maximale Planungssicherheit 📝
Dein krisensicherer Arbeitsplatz sowie attraktive internationale Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten in einem schnell wachsenden Unternehmen
Mit Job-Rad sorgen wir für deine Mobilität 
Umfassende Aus- und Weiterbildung: Nimm an internen Schulungen in unserer L‘Accademia teil 📚
Work-Life-Balance: Planungssicherheit durch mind. 2 Wochen Vorlauf in deinem Schichtplan
Mental Health: Mit L’Osteria Cura tragen wir Sorge für Dein mentales Wohlbefinden 💪


SO TRÄGST DU ZU UNSERER LA FAMIGLIA BEI


• Operative Schichtleitung – Du sorgst für einen reibungslosen Ablauf und effizientes Arbeiten im Team
• Führung & Motivation – Du führst Dein Team mit Begeisterung und entwickelst es weiter 💥
• Support des Betriebsleiters – Du packst mit an und behältst das große Ganze im Blick
• Produktfokus & Kontrolle – Du überwachst Wareneinsatz, Inventuren, Lagerhaltung und Bestellungen 📋
• Personal & Schulung – Du managst Schichten und führst Schulungen zu People-Themen durch 👥



DAMIT BRINGST DU UNS AUF DEN GESCHMACK

Title: Office Manager (w/m/d)
Description:
Als Office Manager (m/w/d) bist du verantwortlich für das Management unseres Büros in Berlin-Kreuzberg. Dies ist eine Teilzeit-Stelle und nicht hybrid, da wir dich vor Ort als Support brauchen.




Deine Aufgaben:

Büroorganisation & Infrastruktur: Du bist verantwortlich für den reibungslosen Ablauf des täglichen Bürobetriebs, verwaltest das Büro- und Verbrauchsmaterial und koordinierst externe Dienstleister (z.B. Reinigung, IT-Support, Handwerker).
Empfang & Hospitality: Als erstes Gesicht des Unternehmens empfängst du Kunden, Bewerber und Partner mit einer herzlichen und professionellen Ausstrahlung.
Event- & Reisemanagement: Du übernimmst die Planung, Organisation und Buchung von Geschäftsreisen sowie die Vorbereitung von internen Meetings, Workshops und regelmäßigen Team-Events.
Vorbereitende Buchhaltung: Du unterstützt unser Finanzteam bei der Rechnungsprüfung, dem Belegmanagement sowie der vorbereitenden Buchhaltung und stehst im Austausch mit unserem Steuerbüro.
Feel Good Management: Du sorgst für eine Wohlfühlatmosphäre im Office, gestaltest die Räumlichkeiten attraktiv und kümmerst dich um das wöchentliche Wohlbefinden des Teams (Obst, Getränke, kleine Überraschungen).




Was du mitbringst:

Erfahrung: Mindestens 3 Jahre Berufserfahrung im Bereich Office Management, Assistenz, Front Desk oder in einer vergleichbaren Schnittstellenfunktion, idealerweise in einem agilen Umfeld oder Startup oder Agentur.
Organisationstalent: Eine strukturierte, proaktive und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise – du behältst auch in turbulenten Phasen stets den Überblick und die Ruhe.
Kommunikation & IT: Exzellente Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift sowie gute Englischkenntnisse für die internationale Kommunikation. Sicherer Umgang mit MS Office bzw. der Google Workspace.
Persönlichkeit: Ein hohes Maß an Empathie, Diskretion, Zuverlässigkeit und eine ausgeprägte Serviceorientierung.




Was wir dir bieten:

Eine anspruchsvolle und abwechslungsreiche Tätigkeit in einem jungen und dynamischen Team
Flexible Arbeitszeiten
Eine faire Vergütung
Eine Teilzeitstelle von 20-30h pro Woche (nach Absprache), familienfreundliche Modelle sind möglich
Überstunden- und Wochenendausgleich
Ein Office direkt um die Ecke von La Maison
Flache Hierarchien, kurze Entscheidungswege, eine offene Feedbackkultur und ein dynamisches Team, das sich auf Augenhöhe begegnet.
Regelmäßige Teamevents, ÖPNV-Zuschuss (Deutschlandticket), kostenlose Getränke und Snacks.




Wir legen großen Wert auf ein positives und diverses Arbeitsumfeld.




Wenn Du Dich angesprochen fühlst, sende uns Deinen Lebenslauf, Referenzen, Beispiele deiner Arbeit und alles weitere, von dem du glaubst, dass es uns überzeugen könnte.




Auch, wenn du nicht alle Anforderungen erfüllst: Wir freuen uns auf Deine Bewerbung!




Schick deine Bewerbung bitte an:

jobs@peopleperson.xyz




Über Uns:

Als peopleperson, Agentur für digitale Kultur und Creator Marketing, legen wir unseren Fokus darauf, gute Marken noch besser zu machen, Online-Communities aufzubauen und fesselnde Geschichten durch Influencer und kreativen Content zu erzählen. Unsere Kundinnen kommen aus den Bereichen Tech, NGO/Non-Profit sowie Kunst & Kultur.

Title: Founder's Associate
Description:
Berlin, Germany | Full-time | Based in Berlin, Germany




Rapid Pioneers is the investment group behind one of the most decorated and successful company builders and investors in Europe in the consumer space — with a track record spanning some of the most exciting brands in beauty, food & beverages, and FMCG this decade.




We are constantly building and investing — and we're looking for a high-potential generalist to be involved in everything: from evaluating business ideas and investments to researching trends and developing original ideas for companies to build.




As Founder's Associate, you'll work directly alongside our founder across all three core activities: building new consumer companies, making investment decisions, and shaping the ecosystem around us. This is not a support role — it's a seat at the table from day one.


Tasks


What You'll Work On




1. Building Companies





Identify emerging market trends and white spaces in the consumer landscape


Spot and evaluate opportunities for new companies to build


Develop original business ideas — and defend them with structured thinking and data


Support the end-to-end process of taking a company from concept to launch





2. Investments





Engage directly with founders — sourcing conversations, evaluating pitches, and building relationships across the ecosystem


Scope and assess new investment opportunities from first look to final decision


Lead and coordinate due diligence processes across market, commercial, and competitive dimensions


Synthesize findings and formulate clear investment recommendations





3. Community Building & Events





Help build and maintain a thriving community of founders, investors, and brand builders


Plan, coordinate, and co-host events, dinners, and gatherings within the consumer and startup ecosystem


Actively represent Rapid Pioneers in the ecosystem — online and in person


Cultivate long-term relationships with key stakeholders, partners, and emerging talent



Requirements


Must-Haves





At least a Bachelor's degree from a top-tier university


Minimum 1 year of experience in a fast-paced environment (top-tier consulting, startup, scale-up, VC, finance, brand building, etc.) — high-impact internships will also be considered


Based in Germany or willing to relocate


Excellent written and verbal communication in English


Experience & interest in AI tools (Claude Co-Work, Lovable, etc.)





Mindset & Soft Skills





Genuine passion for consumer brands, company building, and the startup ecosystem


Problem-solver with strong analytical thinking — able to structure ambiguous problems quickly


Proactive, resourceful, and execution-focused — you don't wait to be told what to do


Entrepreneurial mindset: you come with ideas, not just answers


High ownership and willingness to go the extra mile


Natural connector who enjoys bringing people together



Benefits



A seat at the table with one of Europe's most experienced consumer company builders and investors


Deep exposure to the full cycle of company building and investing — from idea to execution to exit


High learning curve, fast execution cycles, and early responsibility


Option to evolve into a leadership role





Interview Journey





Initial Conversation (20 min) — A first chat to get to know each other, introduce the role and setup, and ensure mutual alignment on expectations and goals.


Deep-Dive Interview (45 min) — We'll explore your experience in more detail, focusing on how your background, mindset, and ambitions align with what we're building.


Case Interview (45 min) — You'll work through a practical case to show how you approach problems, structure thinking, and communicate results.


Feedback Call

Title: IT Desktop Support Technician (m/w/d) – Gigafactory Berlin-Brandenburg
Description:
What To Expect
Tesla is seeking a Desktop Support Technician to join our team at the Gigafactory Berlin-Brandenburg, Germany. Troubleshoot and work on a wide range of queries and incidents, this position will be on the front lines of Tesla Motors’ IT systems support. IT Desktop Technicians are the face of IT for internal customers. The primary task of this position will be to troubleshoot and resolve endpoint issues, install and support the systems, and assist with the day-to-day operations for Tesla’s global IT infrastructure to provide for the growth of Tesla’s initiatives. These duties will be performed in compliance with policy guidelines and within timeframes as defined in the team’s SLAs and/or project requirements.

What You'll Do


Provide onsite and remote support for Tesla organization. Daily technical support of laptops and mobile device hardware and software issues, telephony, printers, and networking and other computer hardware
In the course of performing assigned duties, effective communication both orally and in writing to establish and maintain cooperative working relationships, including company management and outside business partners
Exercise independent judgment consistent with department guidelines\standards
Organize and prioritize workflow and to meet established timeframes and minimal disruption to users/operations
Maintain and update knowledge of policies, procedures, products, IT Stock, and activities of assigned areas
Maintain IT stock, ensuring adequate supplies are available, tracking inventory levels, keeping accurate records
Occasionally work weekends, after hours, periodic on-call rotations, and travel within EMEA up to 10%



What You'll Bring


Requires 2+ years of experience in Desktop Support or 3+ years in similar support role, ideally in supporting international and/or global organizations
Requires equivalent training/education in Computer Science
Excellent customer-oriented service skills, attention to details with strong oral/written communication skills
English is a must; German speaking a big plus
Must have - working with ticket-based incident and request systems
Advanced experience troubleshooting Windows 10&11, MacOS, iOS & Android together with proficiency in using Office 365 desktop apps (e.g. Outlook, Excel) Linux is a plus
Work experience with enterprise remote imaging and administrative tools including SCCM, JAMF, Intune



Candidates are expected to uphold and actively promote sustainability principles in their daily work, operating in line with Tesla Global Environmental, Health, Safety & Security (EHS&S) Policy and EMAS requirements, fostering a culture of continuous environmental improvement.

Title: Data Engineer - Full Stack (m/f/d)
Description:
Berlin Mitte


Hybrid (2 days/week remote)
€60,000-75,000 + VSOP equity


Help us decarbonize cement and concrete - not tomorrow, today.

alcemy is on a mission to keep 100 million tons of CO₂ out of the atmosphere every year by 2030. Making concrete accounts for around 8% of global CO₂ emissions, and our machine-learning software runs inside cement and concrete plants 24/7 to cut it - without sacrificing quality or driving up costs. We're already live in a third of Germany's cement plants and 30+ ready-mix concrete plants.

The role

As our Full Stack Data Engineer, you'll own the data that powers everything we do - the flow of cement and concrete plant data from raw, messy ingestion through cleaning, to the pipelines our machine-learning recommendations run on. The split is roughly 50% data engineering, 50% backend, and it's refreshingly hands-on with customers: you'll dig into real-world data, explain what you find to non-technical people, and make getting data into alcemy dramatically easier.

What You'll Do


Build and own the pipelines that ingest and clean messy plant data into something our ML can use
Help us move from strict, structured data requirements toward flexible, AI-powered ingestion - so customers onboard with far less friction
Track down data anomalies and explain your findings to customers in plain language
Build and maintain the APIs and PostgreSQL databases behind our products, and keep production running smoothly
Work shoulder to shoulder with customers, sales, and customer success - translating between the technical and the non-technical


Requirements

You'll thrive here if...

Above all, you care about climate change and want your work to count.

Must-haves


You've deployed production-grade Python applications
You love getting to grips with an unfamiliar codebase quickly, and you're eager to start delivering work that matters from the start
You're comfortable working directly with customers, sales, and customer success - patient, plain-spoken, and at ease with non-technical enterprise teams
You're open to using AI tools to augment how you work


Nice-to-haves


Experience with Flask or Django and Fast API
PostgreSQL or similar SQL databases
Some exposure to machine learning


Don't tick every box? We'd still love to hear from you.

How We Work


No lone warriors - you'll work collaboratively alongside senior and junior engineers, owning features end to end: build, test, review, deploy
Small team, direct customer exposure, fast startup pace - so async communication, sharp prioritisation, transparency, and direct feedback are the norm, and how we work is something we shape together, and keep improving
We use Claude Code responsibly - AI to move fast, engineers always in charge and accountable, no AI-slop merge requests
Hybrid by design (Berlin Mitte office, up to 2 days remote, plus offsites and boat trips), with real room to grow along technical-expert or management tracks - and the chance to lead a product area within a couple of years


Benefits


25 days' vacation - plus an extra day for every 2 years here, and the days between Christmas and New Year off
€1,500/year and 3 days/quarter for your own development
The Mac of your choice
An office in the heart of Berlin Mitte


Our Tech Stack


Backend & data: Python throughout - FastAPI (auto-generated Swagger/OpenAPI), SQLAlchemy, pydantic, pytest, mypy; pandas, polars, numpy, sklearn; uv for dependencies
Infra: PostgreSQL, AWS on Kubernetes managed with Terraform, ArgoWorkflows for scheduled jobs, GitLab for code
Frontend: React & Next.js in TypeScript with TailwindCSS; Blueprint.js / Radix UI; TanStack Query or axios; Streamlit for internal tools


Our Hiring Process is fully remote


Intro call with our Recruiter
Meet our Hiring Manager
A take-home task that mirrors the real work you'd be doing
Technical interview (90 min) with two engineers, including 30 min of live coding
Team-fit interview (45 min) with two future teammates


At alcemy, we're building a team as diverse as the industry we're transforming. We welcome applications from everyone and assess every candidate on merit alone - regardless of gender, age, ethnic or national origin, religion or worldview, disability, sexual orientation, or gender identity. We especially encourage people who are underrepresented in tech to apply. If you need any adjustments to take part fully in our hiring process, just let us know and we'll make them.

Note: We don't currently offer visa sponsorship, so you'll need a valid EU work visa.

We welcome applications from everyone and assess every candidate on merit alone - regardless of gender, age, ethnic or national origin, religion or worldview, disability, sexual orientation, or gender identity. We especially encourage people who are underrepresented in tech to apply. If you need any adjustments to take part fully in our hiring process, just let us know and we'll make them.

Note: We don't currently offer visa sponsorship, so you'll need a valid EU work visa.

Title: Projektassistenz (w/m/d)
Description:
Unternehmensbeschreibung

Wir stehen jeden Morgen dafür auf, eine lebenswerte Zukunft für nachfolgende Generationen zu schaffen. Je nach Projekt sind wir Berater, Umsetzer – oder beides – nachhaltiger, innovativer und wirtschaftlicher Lösungen für Immobilien, Industrie, Energie und Infrastruktur. In interdisziplinären Teams unterstützen unsere über 6.500 Mitarbeitenden an mehr als 80 Standorten weltweit unsere Kunden. Wir denken visionär und realistisch. Wir arbeiten eigenständig und im Team. Mit Leidenschaft und modernsten Technologien. We unite. Join us at Dreso and let’s create a world we want to live in.

Stellenbeschreibung

Sie haben Organisationstalent, behalten auch bei vielfältigen Projektaufgaben den Überblick und möchten ein Team unterstützen? Bei uns bringen Sie Ihre Stärken in der Projektorganisation, Kommunikation und Administration ein und tragen dazu bei, reibungslose Abläufe in anspruchsvollen Beratungs- und Vergabeprojekten sicherzustellen. Bewerben Sie sich jetzt für einen unserer Standorte


Professionelles Unterstützen der Projektteams für die Planung und Realisierung von Bauprojekten
Erstellen Sie projektbezogene Berichte, Präsentationen, Angebote, Auswertungen und Protokolle
Koordinieren Sie Termine, Besprechungen sowie interne und externe Abstimmungen und bereiten Sie diese vor und nach
Pflegen Sie Projekt- und Datenbanken sowie interne Systeme und digitale Tools
Wirken Sie beim Projektcontrolling mit, etwa durch Reports, Rechnungsverfolgung und Vorprüfung projektbezogener Unterlagen
Kommunizieren Sie souverän mit internen und externen Projektbeteiligten sowie Ansprechpersonen


Qualifikationen


Abgeschlossene kaufmännische oder immobilienwirtschaftliche Ausbildung oder vergleichbare Qualifikation
Erste Berufserfahrung in vergleichbarer Position, erste Erfahrung in der Bau- und Immobilienbranche und idealerweise Erfahrung mit öffentlicher Bauherrenschaft
Sicherer Umgang mit MS-Office
Verhandlungssichere Deutschkenntnisse
Selbstsicheres, freundliches, offenes und interessiertes Auftreten gegenüber allen Ansprechpartnern
Ausgeprägtes Organisationstalent mit guter Strukturfähigkeit und Teamgeist


Zusätzliche Informationen


Zur Sicherstellung Ihrer Work-Life-Balance bieten wir die Möglichkeit zum mobilen Arbeiten
Ihre berufliche und persönliche Entwicklung fördern wir durch individuelle Aus- und Weiterbildungen in der Drees & Sommer - Academy
Ihre Gesundheit unterstützen wir mit einem Bonus für sportlich Aktive oder einem Zuschuss zur Mitgliedschaft bei EGYM-Wellpass
Eine nachhaltige Mobilität fördern wir mit Jobrad-Leasing, ÖPNV-Zuschuss oder Firmenwagen (positionsabhängig)
Zusätzliche attraktive Angebote mit Rabatten im Corporate-Benefits-Portal. Mitarbeitenden-Empfehlungsprogramm mit attraktiver Prämienregelung
Zur Vereinbarkeit von Beruf und Familie bieten wir einen KITA-Zuschuss an. Darüber hinaus offerieren wir Zusatzleistungen für besondere private Ereignisse

Title: Werkstudent:in Data Science
Description:
Über nerou




Die Datenanalyse-Software von nerou unterstützt Betreiber von Kläranlagen bei täglichen Entscheidungen in der Anlagensteuerung. In Kläranlagen müssen komplexe Entscheidungen getroffen werden, um die Anlage auf sich täglich ändernde Einflüsse einzustellen. Alle relevanten Daten werden von Machine Learning Algorithmen analysiert und ausgewertet. Die gewonnen Erkenntnisse werden in Form von Entscheidungshilfen an den Kunden weitergeleitet. Dies reduziert nicht nur Kosten für die Betreiber, sondern schont so auch Ressourcen und Umwelt. Zur Verstärkung unseres Data Science-Bereichs suchen wir nun Dich!







Deine Tätigkeiten



Du extrahierst und validierst große Datenbestände auf Grundlage verschiedener Fragestellungen
Du analysierst Datensätze mit geeigneten statistischen Methoden
Du ergänzt unser motiviertes Team mit Machine Learning- und Data Science-Expertise






Deine Erfahrung



Du studierst (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Mathematik oder etwas vergleichbares
Du hast Erfahrung im Umgang mit Data Science und Machine Learning
Du hast grundliegende Kenntnisse mit Python
Du bist in der Lage, deine Arbeit selbstständig zu strukturieren






Was wir dir bieten




Eine Menge Spaß, versprochen! Du wirst als Werkstudent Teil eines jungen Start-ups und kannst dich mit deinen eigenen Ideen austoben. Du sammelst wertvolle Erfahrungen und hast dabei die Möglichkeit, eigene Projekte zu verwirklichen. Hierbei erhältst du zudem wertvolle Einblicke in das Thema Unternehmensgründung und Start-up. Bei uns kannst du dir deine Arbeitszeit frei einteilen und hast die Wahl zwischen Büro und Home Office - somit bist du auch neben deinem Studium flexibel.




Wenn es dir bei uns gefällt, freuen wir uns natürlich, wenn du lange bei uns bleibst - auch nach deinem Studium in einer Festanstellung! Interessiert? Dann ran an die Tasten! Sende uns deine Mail mit Anschreiben, Zeugnissen und Lebenslauf an jobs@nerou.de Wir freuen uns von dir zu hören und hoffen, dich schon bald in unserem Team begrüßen zu dürfen!

Title: Datenanalyst / Data Engineer (m/w/d)
Description:
Wir sind Bertrandt.

Ein eigenständiger und internationaler Engineering Dienstleister mit langjähriger Expertise. Mit branchenübergreifendem Know-how und einem ganzheitlichen System- und Produktverständnis schaffen wir technologische Lösungen entlang der gesamten Wertschöpfungskette. Wir beschäftigen uns mit den Trendthemen Digitalisierung, E-Mobilität und Autonome Systeme hauptsächlich für die Branchen Automotive, Luftfahrt, Defence und Maschinenbau und ermöglichen hier konsequent die Entstehung passgenauer Lösungen. Unser Ziel: den technologischen Fortschritt beschleunigen und einen relevanten Beitrag zu einer nachhaltigen Zukunft leisten. Daran arbeiten wir jeden Tag – mit rund 12.000 Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeitern an über 50 Standorten weltweit.

Was Sie Erwartet


Verantwortlich für die Entwicklung von Softwaretools zur Erfassung von Daten verschiedener Datenlieferanten aus unterschiedlichen Quellen, darunter Datenbanken, Tabellenkalkulationen und Unternehmenssysteme
Bereinigung, Validierung, Kontrolle und Organisation von Datensätzen für Berichts- und Analysezwecke
Pflege von Datensätzen und Unterstützung von Data-Governance-Maßnahmen
Analyse großer Datensätze zur Identifizierung von Trends, Mustern und Anomalien
Durchführung von Analysen zur Bewertung der Qualität der Datenlieferanten vor der Freigabe an den Kunden
Unterstützung bei Ad-hoc-Anfragen verschiedener Abteilungen zur Datenanalyse
Entwicklung und Pflege regelmäßiger Berichte, Dashboards und Leistungs-Scorecards
Präsentation von Daten auf klare und visuell ansprechende Weise mithilfe von Diagrammen, Tabellen und Dashboards
Verfolgung von Leistungskennzahlen (KPIs)
Identifizierung von Möglichkeiten zur Verbesserung von Berichtsprozessen, Datenqualität und Analysemethoden
Mitwirkung an der Entwicklung von Datenstandards, Vorlagen und Best Practices


Was Sie Mitbringen


Abgeschlossenes Studium oder vergleichbare Qualifikation im Bereich Datenanalyse, Statistik, Informatik oder einer verwandten Fachrichtung
Fundierte Kenntnisse in der Analyse und Aufbereitung von Daten
Grundverständnis im Bereich Informationssysteme von Vorteil
Sicherer Umgang mit Excel bzw. Google Sheets
Gute Kenntnisse in SQL zur Datenabfrage und -verarbeitung
Erfahrung mit BI-Tools wie Power BI oder Tableau
Programmierkenntnisse in Python oder R zur Datenanalyse und Automatisierung
Sehr gute Englisch- und Deutschkenntnisse, Französisch ist von Vorteil
Flexibilität und Reisebereitschaft für gelegentliche kurzfristige Dienstreisen


Bertrandt legt großen Wert auf Gleichbehandlung und Chancengleichheit. Unsere Recruiting-Entscheidungen basieren auf den Erfahrungen und Fähigkeiten der Bewerber*innen. Schwerbehinderte Menschen bzw. gleichgestellte Menschen im Sinne des SGB IX sind herzlich willkommen und werden bei gleicher Eignung bevorzugt eingestellt.

Sie sind interessiert?

Wir freuen uns über Ihre Bewerbung!

Title: Projektassistenz (m/w/d)
Description:
WERDEN SIE TEIL UNSERES TEAMS!

Für unseren Mandanten, eine innovative Umwelt- und Ingenieurberatung, suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt eine engagierte Projektassistenz (m/w/d) für die Bereiche Gebäudeschadstoffe und Geologie, die Organisationstalent mit kaufmännischem Know-how verbindet. Hier erwartet Sie ein dynamisches Team, in dem Sie die Projektleitung entlasten, Prozesse optimieren und einen echten Beitrag zum Unternehmenserfolg leisten.

WAS SIE BEI UNS LEISTEN


Vorbereitung von Angeboten, Berichten, Gutachten und sonstigen Dokumenten in Zusammenarbeit mit den Projektleitungen und weiteren Schnittstellen
Überwachung und Priorisierung von Aufträgen, Projekten und Prozessen
Erstellung von Rechnungen, Verfolgung der Zahlungseingänge und Koordination des Mahnwesens
Erfolgskontrolle sowie das Controlling von Projekten in Abstimmung mit der Abteilungsleitung und ggf. weiteren Verantwortlichen
Steuerung des Vertriebs- und Kalkulationswesens sowie der Öffentlichkeitsarbeit
Mitwirken bei der Prozessoptimierung


WAS SIE MITBRINGEN


Erfolgreicher Abschluss eines betriebswirtschaftlichen Studiums oder eine abgeschlossene Ausbildung im kaufmännischen Bereich
Erste Erfahrungen in der Projektunterstützung
Zuverlässige, selbstständige und sorgfältige Arbeitsweise gepaart mit ausgeprägter Teamfähigkeit
Kommunikations- und Organisationsfähigkeit sowie Interesse an der Einarbeitung in neue Sachverhalte
Lösungs- und kundenorientiertes Denken und Handeln
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift (mind. C1-Niveau)


WAS WIR IHNEN BIETEN


Verantwortungsvoller Aufgabenbereich mit Gestaltungsfreiheit
Kollegiales, dynamisches Team und flache Hierarchien
Strukturierte Einarbeitung und individuelle Entwicklungswege für Ihre Karriere
30 Urlaubstage, flexible Arbeitszeiten und Möglichkeit des mobilen Arbeitens
Firmenfitness


KONTAKT

Wenn Sie sich in diesem Profil wiedererkennen, freuen wir uns auf Ihre Bewerbung unter Angabe Ihres Gehaltswunsches und Ihres möglichen Starttermins.

Bei Fragen steht Ihnen Frau Kira Bukin unter recruiting@aem.email zur Verfügung.

Title: Data Analysis Werkstudent:in (w/m/d)
Description:
Unser Ziel ist eine Solaranlage auf jedem Dach, ein Speicher in jedem Haus und ein E-Auto in jeder Garage. Enpal macht das möglich mit einer integrierten Gesamtlösung für dezentrale Energie – von Solaranlage und Batteriespeicher über Wallbox, Smart Meter bis zur Wärmepumpe. Herzstück ist unsere KI-gestützte Plattform Enpal.One+, die tausende Systeme intelligent vernetzt und Strombezug sowie -einspeisung effizient am Energiemarkt optimiert.

Bist Du bereit für Lösungen, die mehr als nur ein Versprechen sind und täglich echte Lebensqualität in tausende Haushalte bringen? Was du bei Enpal gestaltest, liefert morgen sauberen Strom und verändert nachhaltig, wie wir Energie nutzen.

Deine Aufgaben


Beantwortung einfacher Use Cases auf Basis bereits vorhandener Data Views (mit Fokus auf Sales-Daten)
Erstellung und Pflege von Dashboards zur Visualisierung von Vertriebskennzahlen
Wartung bestehender KPIs sowie Integration neuer Kennzahlen im Sales Kontext
Entwicklung und Anpassung einfacher Kalkulationen (z.B. Forecast, Performance-Metriken)
Unterstützung bei der Strukturierung und Pflege von Data-Warehouse-Lösungen


Dein Profil


Laufendes Studium im Bereich Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science, BWL mit Analytics-Schwerpunkt oder vergleichbar
Erste praktische Erfahrung oder starkes Interesse an Datenanalysen
Sicherer Umgang mit SQL
Erfahrung im Dashboarding (z.B. mit Power BI, Tableau oder ähnlichen Tools) von Vorteil
Grundkenntnisse im Data Warehousing
Erste Erfahrung mit Snowflake von Vorteil
Grundkenntnisse in Sales-Prozessen wünschenswert
Erfahrung mit Salesforce von Vorteil
Analytisches Denkvermögen und strukturierte Arbeitsweise
Fähigkeit, einfache Anforderungen effizient und pragmatisch umzusetzen


Du bist interessiert, auch wenn du nicht alle Anforderungen erfüllst? Bewirb dich trotzdem! Wir freuen uns darauf, dein Potenzial zu entdecken - unabhängig davon, ob deine Erfahrung jede einzelne Voraussetzung erfüllt. ☀️

Darauf kannst du dich freuen


Arbeite in Deutschlands erstem grünen Unicorn - Gestalte aktiv die solare Energiewende mit.
Die Sonne scheint auf der ganzen Welt - Bei Enpal arbeitest du mit einem hochmotivierten, diversen Team aus über 65 Nationalitäten.
Das ideale Setup für deinen Fokus - Wir sind überzeugt, dass exzellente Ergebnisse dort entstehen, wo die Bedingungen stimmen. Deshalb setzen wir auf ein hybrides Arbeitskonzept, das dir die Freiheit gibt, immer genau so zu arbeiten, wie es am besten zu deinen Aufgaben passt. Du brauchst neue Impulse auf internationalem Niveau? Mit Workflex bieten wir, abhängig von deiner Verantwortung, die Möglichkeit, deine Tätigkeit bis zu 30 Tage im Jahr ins Ausland zu verlegen.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Place: Brighton And Hove, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Legal & General (L&G) is a leading UK financial services group and major global investor.



We’ve been safeguarding people’s financial futures since 1836, and strive to build a better society, while improving the lives of our customers and creating value for shareholders.



We are one of the world’s largest asset managers and provide powerful asset origination capabilities. Together, these underpin our retirement and protection solutions: we are an international player in pension risk transfer, in UK and US life insurance, and in UK workplace pensions and retirement income.



Our Group Functions provide the services that all areas of the business need. This requires a talented and diverse team behind the scenes, who enable everyone at L&G to do what they do best. 



Joining us means helping to improve the lives of our customers and contributing to the success of the business every day.





Job Description



We’re looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our Institutional Retirement Client Services team.



This role is all about using data carefully and confidently to support Payroll & Reinsurance activity, Pension Risk Transfer transactions and wider operational decision-making. You’ll be working with data from end to end, helping to cleanse, validate, transform, compare and document information so that it can be used accurately by the right teams and systems.



You’ll be joining a supportive team that works closely together, with opportunities to collaborate with payroll consultants, internal stakeholders and wider data teams across Legal & General. We’re looking for someone who enjoys detail, takes pride in accuracy, and is curious about finding better ways to do things.



What you’ll be doing:



Managing operational data across cleansing, validation, transformation, integration and mining activities
Supporting Pension Risk Transfer transaction data from end to end
Gathering requirements from payroll consultants and internal stakeholders
Improving data quality so teams can make accurate and timely decisions
Testing and documenting processes to support consistent client delivery
Maintaining clear audit trails across the full data lifecycle
Supporting and upskilling less experienced team members in new processes
Sharing best practice with wider data teams and identifying better ways of working

Who we’re looking for:



Advanced Excel skills and confidence working with detailed data
Experience in data analysis, operational data management or data process improvement
Strong attention to detail and a right-first-time mindset
Confidence communicating with stakeholders and gathering requirements
Ability to manage tight timescales, prioritise work and escalate when needed
Curiosity, resilience and confidence challenging where appropriate
Familiarity with Alteryx, Python, SQL or SAS would be beneficial
Understanding of defined benefit pensions, Pension Risk Transfer or pension-related data would be beneficial

Whatever your role, we reward performance and behaviour with a package that looks after all the things that are important to you. Here are some of the benefits we offer:



The opportunity to participate in our annual, performance -related bonus plan and valuable share schemes
Generous pension contribution
Life assurance
Healthcare Plan (permanent employees only)
At least 25 days holiday, plus public holidays, 26 days after 2 years’ service. There’s also the option to buy and sell holiday
Competitive family leave
Participate in our electric car scheme, which offers employees the option to hire a brand-new electric car through tax efficient salary sacrifice (permanent employees only)
There are many discounts we offer – both for our own products and at a range of high street stores and online
We’re creating net‑zero carbon workplaces by 2030 by investing in our sustainable, modern offices across the UK, all designed to bring people together and elevate the in‑person experience


Additional Information



At L&G, we believe it's possible to generate positive returns today while helping to build a better future for all.



If you join us, you’ll be part of a welcoming, inclusive culture, with opportunities to collaborate with people of diverse backgrounds, views, and experiences. Guided by leaders with integrity who care about your future and wellbeing. Empowered through initiatives which support people to develop their careers and excel.



We care passionately about outcomes rather than attendance and are therefore open to discussing all kinds of flexible working options including part-time, term-time and job shares. Although some roles have limited flexibility due to customer demand, we accommodate requests when we can.



It doesn’t matter if you don’t meet every single criterion in this advert. Instead, think about what you excel at and what else you can bring in terms of strengths, potential and connection to our purpose.

Title: Data Coordinator – Adult Learning
Description:
Job Description

Do you have experience of managing and analysing data to support service delivery and are you keen to play a key role in supporting Adult Learning programmes that help residents develop new skills and progress in work and learning? If so, you could be the perfect fit for our Adult Learning Data Coordinator role in the Employment and Skills Team at East Sussex County Council.

As Data Coordinator, you will play a central role in maintaining high standards of data quality across Adult Learning programmes. You will support the preparation, validation and submission of learner and programme data, ensuring information is accurate, complete and compliant. Through reporting, analysis and forecasting, you will support performance monitoring and informed decision-making, while identifying trends, risks and opportunities for improvement. Working closely with colleagues and delivery partners, you will support audit and assurance activities and provide line management and support to the Adult Learning Data Officer.

This role is a one-year fixed term contract with the potential for extension, subject to funding.

We Are Looking For Candidates Who


Have experience of working in a data-focussed role involving reporting, data quality and performance monitoring
Can analyse and interpret data to produce accurate information that supports decision-making
Have strong organisational skills and can manage a varied workload to meet deadlines
Can communicate complex information clearly and build positive working relationships with a range of stakeholders.


Please download the job description and person specification for more information.

To apply for this role, please follow the ‘Apply Now’ button above and upload your CV and a Supporting Statement. We will be using your Supporting Statement to shortlist so please be sure to address how you meet all the criteria in the person specification, drawing on any relevant work/life experience, education, or personal interests.

For guidance on how to write an application see our how to apply page and our statement on the use of AI .

Interviews will take place at County Hall in Lewes on Tuesday 11 August 2026. If you are unavailable on this date, please let us know, and we may be able to schedule another time.

Candidates who meet certain criteria are given priority when we shortlist. See our priority status page for details.

This is an excellent opportunity to use your analytical and reporting skills to make a real impact on Adult Learning programmes across East Sussex. You'll join a supportive team, contribute to service improvement and have access to training and development opportunities.

For an informal discussion about this opportunity contact Rebecca Jones, Adult Learning Manager, Rebecca.jonesCS@eastsussex.gov.uk

We offer a diverse range of benefits to help and support you during your employment. More information can be found on our benefits page.

Choose East Sussex!

About Us

Additional Information

Your starting salary will be pro rata if the above position is part-time or term-time only.

If you require sponsorship to work in the UK please let the hiring manager know this in your application so we can check eligibility for sponsorship for the role. For further information please go to Work in the UK - GOV.UK (www.gov.uk) .

If you require any reasonable adjustments, due to disability, to enable you to participate fully in the process please contact the hiring manager on the contact details above.

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
Role: Marketing Data Analyst

Salary: Dependant on skills and experience

Hours: 35 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Matchday working included in role too.

Location: American Express Stadium, Brighton

Contract Type: Permanent

Deadline Day: 22nd July 2026




About Brighton & Hove Albion FC

We compete at the highest levels of football on a global stage while embracing our Sussex community spirit. At the heart of everything we do is our commitment to high performance, professional excellence, and making a positive impact. We are proud to have placed in the Sunday Times Best Place to Work 2026.




Turn raw data into match-winning commercial strategy.

Are you ready to be the "single point of insight" at a forward-thinking football club? We are looking for a highly strategic data professional to own our metrics, rules, and logic. In this role, you won’t just be crunching numbers, you will be translating complex datasets into the deep context and actionable recommendations that drive our global growth. This role will work across our marketing and ticketing team.




The role will also include matchday working to run and automate the tout identification models and review post-match accounts, therefore there will be an element of evening and weekend working involved.




To find out more about this role, please click here to view the job description.




Ready to make your mark off the pitch?

If you are a proactive problem solver with a passion for driving commercial success through data, we want you on our team. We are looking for someone with strong skills in Google Cloud Platforms, advanced SQL skills for complex data queries and strong proficiency in Python for building, maintaining and automating data models.




Our values are essential to our success

Our values serve as guiding principles for how we connect with one another, our fans, partners and sponsors. As a club, we embody these values in everything we do:




🔵 Treat People Well

⚪ Exceed Expectations

🔵 Aim High. Never Give Up

⚪ Act with Integrity

🔵 Make it Special

⚪ Be Fan Focused







How we say thank you

In appreciation for your hard work and dedication, we are pleased to offer you a range of additional benefits, including but not limited to:




🥗 Complimentary breakfast and lunch at both sites

🏝️23 days holiday rising with length of service (pro rata for part time staff)

⚡Rewards platform, which includes access to 900+ retailers, a wellbeing centre and more!

👶 Enhanced family policies

🎾 Access to a gym and padel court at our training ground

🎫 Priority access to match tickets and access to free WSL tickets for 2026/27 season

🎓 In-house training programme and CPD opportunities

💰 Exclusive discounts and benefits from our partners and local businesses




Our commitment to EDI

We take pride in being an equal opportunity workplace that reflects the diversity of our society and the world around us. Our core values foster a strong ethical culture, enabling us to meet the needs of our diverse audience, both on and off the pitch. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age, disability, sex, gender reassignment, sexual orientation, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, and marriage or civil partnership. If you require any reasonable adjustments to support your application, please contact teamtalent@brightonandhovealbion.com




Safeguarding is part of everything we do

We are committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children, young people and adults at risk. We expect all colleagues and volunteers to share this commitment.




This position is UK-based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All job offers are conditional upon background checks, including a full 3-year reference history, DBS checks, and verification of your eligibility to work. Please note, we do not sponsor applicants without the right to work in the UK.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Kantar is the world’s leading AI-native marketing data and analytics business and an indispensable brand partner to the world’s top companies. We combine the most meaningful attitudinal and behavioural data with deep expertise and advanced analytics to uncover how people think and act. We help clients understand what has happened and why and how to shape the marketing strategies that shape their future.




Why this job is important




We’re known for our data. Help us turn it into insight that makes a real difference.




At Kantar, you’ll join a team that sits at the heart of how data becomes decisions. As a Data Analyst in our Panels Performance & Business Intelligence team, you’ll strengthen the analytical foundations that underpin our panel solutions. Working closely with colleagues across data science, engineering, technology and our Global Delivery Centres, you’ll help ensure our models are robust, scalable and genuinely useful for clients and internal teams alike.




This role is ideal if you enjoy combining analytical thinking with practical delivery – taking models from idea to production, spotting opportunities for improvement, and explaining complex findings in a way that others can act on.




What you’ll be doing

Maintaining and improving analytical models used in day-to-day panel operations
Analysing model outputs to identify performance issues and opportunities to enhance results
Working with global delivery and technology teams to support production-ready data models and BI solutions
Running statistical and exploratory analyses to uncover actionable insights
Communicating analytical findings clearly to both technical and non-technical colleagues







The ideal skills & experience

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a natural curiosity for data
Experience using SQL and at least one analytical language such as Python or R
A solid understanding of statistics and machine learning fundamentals
Experience working with data transformation or ETL tools (DBT is a plus)
Confidence explaining technical concepts to non-technical audiences
A collaborative mindset and comfort working with distributed, cross-functional teams




What’s in it for you

Flexible hybrid working (two to three days in the office)
25 days leave, 2 days paid for volunteering and life event leave
Competitive salary and bonus (bonus dependent on role)
Company pension
Enhanced parental leave
Healthcare options
Wide range of flexible benefits




Please note

We are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this position, either at the point of hire or at any point in the future. Applicants should only apply if they have the permanent right to live and work in the UK and will not require employer sponsorship during their employment.




Why join Kantar?

You’ll be joining our Profiles division, home to specialists in survey design, sampling and data science. With the world’s largest audience network (over 170 million people), we’re trusted by many of the world’s leading brands to provide amazing insights from real people.




What about Kantar itself? Well, we shape the brands of tomorrow by better understanding people everywhere. By understanding people, we can understand what drives their decisions, actions, and aspirations on a global scale. And if we combine the expertise of our people with the latest AI technology, we can really help brands discover some amazing insights.




And because we know people, we like to make sure our own people are being looked after as well. Title: CRM Analyst
Description:
Because your new ideas are our way new ways of working.

Evolve, your way.

Digital at Primark

Our digital teams are right at the heart of what we do. Surrounded by some of the best digital specialists in the industry, you’ll bring your own ideas to the business to shape the experience of our customers as part of a forward-thinking team.

Are you a data-driven CRM professional with a passion for customer insights and analytics? Join Primark’s growing Digital Team as a CRM Customer Insight Analyst, where you'll transform customer data into actionable insights that drive CRM performance and business growth. Working closely with CRM and key business stakeholders, you will deliver reporting, analysis, dashboards, and campaign performance evaluations to support data-driven decision-making. You will also identify opportunities to enhance customer engagement through segmentation and customer insights. This is an exciting opportunity to play a key role in the continued evolution of Primark’s CRM programme while developing your career within a dynamic and fast-paced digital environment.

What You’ll Do as a CRM Analyst

We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities:


Demonstrate CRM impact and effectiveness through analysis of campaign, customer, transactional, GA4, and app data.
Deliver campaign reporting, post-campaign analysis, and recommendations to improve CRM performance and support planning decisions.
Develop and automate reporting on acquisition performance, enabling ROI tracking and activity prioritisation
Lead the delivery of CRM reporting initiatives, working cross-functionally to ensure accurate, accessible CRM data within Tableau.
Design and maintain KPI dashboards across the CRM funnel, providing actionable insights and clear performance visibility for senior stakeholders.
Analyse customer data to build audience understanding, inform segmentation strategies, and optimise campaign targeting.
Evaluate A/B test results, including target vs. control group analysis, to inform the CRM testing roadmap.
Support ADHOC requests from across Digital, to demonstrate the impact CRM has had on the wider business and commercial objectives
Present analytical findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Promote data literacy by upskilling CRM teams and stakeholders in reporting, insights, and performance measurement.


What You’ll Bring

We want every person in our organisation to feel like they’re making a key contribution. Here are some of the experiences and skills you’ll need for the role:


Proven experience in CRM, customer insights, digital analytics, or marketing analytics, with strong analytical skills and the ability to translate complex data into actionable recommendations that drive business outcomes.
Strong CRM and customer analytics expertise, including experience analysing campaign performance, customer behaviour, segmentation, lifecycle analysis, CRM performance metrics, and identifying optimisation opportunities.
Advanced technical skills, including proficiency in SQL for data extraction, manipulation, and analysis, along with experience working with large customer databases and multiple data sources such as transactional, behavioural, and campaign data.
Experience with reporting and analytics tools, including data visualisation platforms such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar tools, as well as digital analytics platforms such as Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and app analytics data.
Knowledge of testing and measurement methodologies, including A/B test design, performance measurement, results evaluation, and applying insights to improve customer engagement and business performance.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to present insights effectively to both technical and non-technical audiences and work cross-functionally with CRM, Marketing, Digital, Data, and Technology teams.
Highly organised, detail-oriented, and proactive, with a passion for customer insight, continuous improvement, data accuracy, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Experience within retail, e-commerce, or other customer-focused industries is desirable.


Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future - and we’re excited to develop yours.

What You’ll Get

People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best.

Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, potential bonus. You will also get 27days of annual leave and bank holidays and you can also buy 5 more. Because primark is all about tailoring to you we offer tax saver ticket, fitness centre and a subsidised cafeteria.

About Primark

At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together.

If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist.

All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss.

REQ ID: JR-11447

Title: Product Analyst - Anti-Cheating
Description:
Office location: Cambridge, UK - This position offers flexible working options, including predominantly remote work. However, applicants should be based (or be willing to relocate) within a comfortable commuting distance of our office to attend onsite as required.

Are you a data-driven problem solver who is keen on protecting fair play and improving the player experience in MMO games?

As a Product Analyst focused on anti-cheating at Jagex, you'll play a vital role in safeguarding the integrity of our games by helping us understand and combat cheating, botting, and gold farming. Working as part of our Anti-Cheat team, you'll use data to uncover player behaviours, identify emerging threats, and develop insights that help shape detection systems and preventative measures.

This is an exciting opportunity to combine analytical expertise with game knowledge, working alongside Product, Data Science, Game Analytics, and Engineering teams to protect millions of players and ensure our game worlds remain healthy, balanced, and fair.

What you'll be doing:


Support the day-to-day operation of the Anti-Cheat team, helping ensure key KPIs, SLAs, and player sentiment remain on track.
Analyse large datasets to identify suspicious behaviours, botting activity, and gold farming trends.
Work closely with Game Analytics, Data Science, and Product teams to develop effective anti-cheat strategies and deterrents.
Translate complex datasets into clear insights and practical recommendations that improve game integrity.
Support the development and enhancement of game telemetry by partnering with Engineering and Design teams.
Collaborate with central Data teams to define reporting requirements and improve data products.
Build a deep understanding of game systems, player behaviours, and telemetry to improve the effectiveness of anti-cheat initiatives.
Communicate findings clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders, helping drive informed decision-making across the business.


What we're looking for:


Experience querying and analysing data using SQL, with a strong understanding of data cleansing techniques.
A demonstrable understanding of video game player behaviours, particularly in MMORPGs and preferably in Old School RuneScape and/or RuneScape.
Familiarity with modern data platforms and architectures such as AWS, Snowflake, and OLAP technologies.
Experience creating reports and visualisations using tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies within large datasets.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to present technical findings in a clear, accessible way.
A proactive approach to problem-solving and a passion for using data to improve player experiences.


What we offer:

When you join Jagex you can look forward to a generous Perks & Benefits package including:


Private Healthcare, including Dental Plan.
Minimum 6% Pension contributions.
Employee Assistance Programme & onsite Counselling.
Life Insurance.
Discretionary annual performance bonus.
Enhanced family leave policies from day 1.
Flexible working hours.
25 days annual leave + Bank holidays & the option to buy/sell holidays + so much more!


Inclusion & Accessibility Statement

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

Right to Work Statement

This role is only open to applicants who have the permanent right to work in the UK. We are unable to provide or take over visa sponsorship for this position, either now or in the future. Applicants must therefore be able to demonstrate their ongoing eligibility to work in the UK without the need for employer sponsorship.

About Jagex:

We are Jagex: The RuneScape Company- home to one of gaming's most enduring success stories. We create deep, community-powered games with worlds that evolve, inspire, and endure. Our flagship MMORPG franchise, RuneScape has welcomed over 300 million player accounts, inspiring generations of players across PC and mobile for 25 years.

As we begin our 26th year, we are accelerating our strategy to expand RuneScape into a broader ecosystem of forever games and connected experiences, grounded in a renewed commitment to integrity and fair play. This includes the reduction of monetisation mechanics, as well as the launch of RuneScape: DragonWilds into Steam Early Access in 2025 representing a major step in building a multi-genre future for the RuneScape universe. We remain focused on building enduring worlds, empowering player-led innovation, and laying the foundations for the boldest era in RuneScape's history. For more information, visit www.jagex.com.

Headquartered in Cambridge, United Kingdom, We are home to passionate creators, storytellers, engineers, and even a few dreamers, all united by a single mission- to shape the future of online worlds and empower players to make them their own. We're looking for people to join us who share our ambitions & values - We foster an inclusive workplace built on trust, autonomy, and a shared passion for creating experiences which inspire our communities. Our values guide everything we do: We Embrace Fellowship by collaborating and sharing openly. We Hone our Craft by always learning and improving; We Honour our Vows by being honest and transparent; We are our Customers' Heroes by striving to understand our team & player needs, and We Seize The Day by making the most of every opportunity.

Join us. The next era of RuneScape - and the next chapter of Jagex: The RuneScape Company - starts now.

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

Title: People Insight Analyst
Description:
Role: People Insight AnalystWor...

Role: People Insight Analyst

Work Level: £35,000 - £40,000 + up to 15% bonus + bens

Contract Type: Permanent

Location: LU5 5XE, Dunstable, 3 days in the office, 2 days working from home

Role Overview

Are you passionate about turning data into compelling stories that shape real business decisions? Join our People Insight team as a People Insight Analyst and play a pivotal role in delivering meaningful reporting and analytics that directly influence how we understand and develop our people.

This is an exciting opportunity to be at the heart of a modern, data-led transformation, helping to evolve reporting into a highly automated, scalable and insight-driven model. You will work with cutting-edge tools, building dashboards and reporting solutions that empower stakeholders to make informed decisions quickly and confidently.

You will collaborate with teams across the business, translating complex data into clear, actionable insights, helping leaders spot trends, identify risks and unlock opportunities. Your work will have tangible impact, whether responding to ad hoc insight requests, supporting strategic analytics roadmaps, or enabling self-service reporting.

This role offers excellent development potential. You will strengthen your skills in data storytelling, analytics, and automation, while gaining exposure to strategic business partnering and statutory reporting requirements. You will also contribute to building capability across the People function, helping others unlock the power of data.

If you thrive on solving problems, enjoy working with stakeholders, and take pride in delivering accurate, trusted insights, this is a highly rewarding role where your work truly matters.

Minimum Criteria

To succeed in this role, you will demonstrate:


Experience producing automated dashboards or reports (e.g. using Excel formulas or Power BI) within a business environment
Proven ability to manipulate and analyse large datasets, including data cleansing, validation and reconciliation of outputs against source systems (e.g. HR systems such as Dayforce)
Hands-on experience managing and responding to ad hoc stakeholder insight requests, including prioritising workload through ticketing, backlog or SLA-driven processes
Evidence of translating complex data into clear, actionable insights, for example presenting trends, risks or KPI performance to non-technical audiences
Working knowledge of people metrics (e.g. attrition, hiring, absence, diversity data) or similar operational KPIs, and experience supporting regular or statutory reporting cycles


We believe that everyone is unique and there should be no barriers to entry and no limits to ambition. We are committed to being an inclusive organisation that values diversity and welcomes your application whatever your background or situation.

Under-represented groups such as women, ethnic minorities, people with disabilities & members of the LGBTQIA+ community (those who identify as lesbian, gay, bi, trans and non-binary or those who use a different LGBTQIA+ term), are strongly encouraged to build a career with us. Speak to us about workplace adjustments, part-time and flexible working. Where possible we will support this.
