Title: Ecommerce Data Analyst – EMEA
Description:
Ecommerce Data Analyst – EMEA




Basingstoke / Hybrid (3 days office 2 WFH)

£45,000–£50,000 DOE + Bonus + Excellent Benefits




About the Company

My client is a global consumer goods business with a portfolio of leading brands across beauty, personal care and lifestyle categories, sold through major retailers and ecommerce platforms across EMEA.




With Ecommerce being a key growth priority, with increasing focus on driving sell-out performance, conversion and execution across priority SKUs and retailers.




Continuing to build a lean, high-impact Ecommerce team focused on accelerating performance where it matters most, with clear ownership of outcomes and a strong bias for execution.




The Role

Reporting into the Head of Ecommerce EMEA, this role will serve as the analytical backbone of the eCommerce Acceleration team.

You will be responsible for identifying where growth opportunities exist, where performance is being lost, and what actions will have the greatest commercial impact.

Working closely with the Retail Growth Lead, Retail Media Lead and Digital Shelf Lead, you will translate data into clear priorities and actionable recommendations.

This role is focused on driving decisions, not just producing reports.







Key Responsibilities

Performance & Insight

Analyse performance across priority retailers and SKUs
Identify trends, opportunities and root causes of underperformance
Translate data into commercially relevant recommendations

SKU & Retailer Prioritisation

Help determine where the business should focus investment and effort
Develop prioritisation frameworks for SKUs, retailers and initiatives
Ensure resources are directed towards the highest growth opportunities

Retail Media & Digital Shelf Analytics

Identify links between content quality, media investment and conversion performance
Measure the impact of optimisation initiatives and test-and-learn programmes
Support ongoing improvement of media ROI and ecommerce conversion

Business Partnering

Act as a trusted analytical partner to the Ecommerce leadership team
Support weekly business reviews with insight and recommendations
Present findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

Dashboarding & Automation

High competence using Power BI dashboards and reporting tools
Improve visibility and accessibility of ecommerce performance data
Automate reporting and reduce manual analysis wherever possible




What We’re Looking For

FMCG and/or Lifestyle/Personal Care/Beauty category experience preferred
3+ years’ experience in ecommerce, commercial analytics, business intelligence or data analytics
Advanced Excel skills and experienced Power BI/Tableau user
Strong commercial mindset with the ability to turn data into action
Experience analysing sales, traffic, conversion or digital performance metrics
Ability to communicate complex analysis in a clear and practical way
Curious, proactive and comfortable challenging assumptions
Highly organised with strong attention to detail




Why Join?

This is an opportunity to become the analytical engine of a newly established eCommerce Acceleration team.

You will work directly with senior leadership and influence where the business invests, which opportunities it pursues and how success is measured.

The role offers exposure across ecommerce strategy, retail media, digital shelf and commercial performance, with a direct impact on growth across EMEA.




Benefits include-

Up to £50,000 salary DOE + bonus
Annual pay reviews
Hybrid working – 3 days office 2 WFH 35 hours per week during the hours of 9.00 to 17.00.
Holidays 25 per year– up to 28 days + Bank Holidays
Pension contributions
Private Medical Health cover including family

Beautiful contemporary office and wonderful flexible working culture!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Hey! We're Huel, and we're pleased to meet you 👋

Our culture thrives on high performance, and we set the bar high for new Hueligans who join us.

We invite you to read the advert below for our new Data Analyst position to understand the role, and explore our 7 Huel values before applying to ensure we have the right working environment for you!




The role

Huel is looking for a Data Analyst to turn raw behavioural and commercial data into clear, actionable insights for Marketing, Trading, Product, and Retail teams. You’ll build measurement frameworks, run causal analyses, and create self-serve dashboards, helping the business move fast and make confident, data-driven decisions.




Key responsibilities include:

Owning end-to-end analysis – translate open-ended questions from Marketing, Trading, Product, and Retail into clear analytical briefs, run the analysis, and land unambiguous recommendations.
Designing & evaluating experiments – design A/B tests and causal-inference studies (lift analysis, hold-out tests) and communicate results in plain language.
Defining measurement frameworks – set the right KPIs and establish how new initiatives will be measured, ensuring every launch has a clear success metric and data plan from day one.
Scale our data foundations – team up with Engineering to design efficient, accurate data sets and schemas, define requirements for new data feeds, and automate the checks that keep everything running smoothly.
Championing self-serve analytics – develop and maintain live dashboards, document datasets, and run training sessions so non-analysts can answer routine questions without waiting in a queue.
AI & Automation - Champion the adoption of AI-powered analytics, using AI tools to enhance analysis, automate routine activities, uncover deeper insights, and improve productivity, while enabling colleagues to self-serve trusted data and answer business questions independently.
Spotting growth opportunities – combine behavioural and marketing data with commercial metrics to size new products, markets, and campaigns, prioritising by impact and effort.




This role is based at our HQ in Tring, Hertfordshire. We operate a hybrid working model, with office days on Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays, and the flexibility to work from home on Wednesdays and Fridays.




Not yet sure if this is the right role for you? Click HERE to read a more detailed job description.




What we're looking for in you

We’re looking for a confident, curious analyst who’s comfortable working with statistics, behavioural data, and experiment design. You can move seamlessly between SQL, product analytics tools, and storytelling through slides. You’re proactive, independent, and always looking for high-impact opportunities to raise the bar on how we use data. In a fast-paced environment, you adapt quickly and communicate clearly - collaborating with both technical and non-technical teams to get things done.

SQL – you can write quick exploratory queries or optimise a multi-table join on Snowflake without breaking a sweat.
Product analytics – comfortable in platforms such as Heap, Amplitude or Mixpanel and happy to set up events, funnels and cohorts from scratch.
Statistical – solid on A/B testing, experiment design and the basics of causal inference; you know when a result is worth acting on.
Measurement mindset – you’ve built tracking plans and KPI frameworks that let teams see what “good” looks like before they ship.
Data - experience modelling data in dbt (or similar), automating checks and keeping feeds clean and accurate as they scale.




And as always, we’re on the lookout for new Hueligans who love a fast-paced environment and are always striving to be their best. We want people who aren’t afraid to think differently and push boundaries. If you’re all about setting big goals and believe that amazing things happen through creativity and teamwork, you’ll fit right in at Huel!




What do we offer in return?

We know that at times, our teams face demanding pressures, and exceptional effort deserves meaningful rewards. That's why we've created a world-class perks and benefits program designed to support our Hueligans in achieving their best, both professionally and personally, while celebrating the global impact they're making!




🕖 Hybrid working - We spend Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays in the office together. The remaining two days have the option to be worked from home.

🌴 30 days annual leave PLUS bank holidays

🥤 Free Huel to keep you going

🏖️ 2 weeks a year to work remotely from anywhere!

🐾 Dog friendly

🙋 Paid Volunteering Days

🏋️ Free on-site gym with free classes, and we will give you your own nutrition plan

🧘 Free on-site wellness area offering infrared saunas and an ice bath🧘

🧠 Free 1-on-1 therapy provided by Self-Space.

🏥 Private Medical and Health insurance for you and your loved ones, including free life insurance covering up to 4x your salary

⚡ Electric Car Scheme with onsite charging

🚴Cycle to Work Scheme

🤰 Enhanced Family Leave

👪 Workplace Nursery Scheme

🎓 Huel Academy

💸 Paid Employee Referral Scheme

🎉 Biannual events to celebrate success - Have you heard about Huelchella?




We are Hueligans

We know that diversity isn't just important; it’s essential, and it makes us stronger. We're all about embracing our differences, celebrating what makes us unique, and bringing together Hueligans from all walks of life.

Whilst we all share the 7 values of Huel, it’s our individual differences that truly enhance our culture of belonging. We seek out Hueligans from around the world, encouraging authenticity, diverse views, and fresh ideas to create products that our global customers love.

Meet our teams here.

Title: Data Reporting Analyst
Description:
/Position: Reporting Analyst

/Location: Farnborough HQ (Hybrid options available)




Are you a Reporting specialist with advanced knowledge of SSRS and SQL?




We have an immediate need for a Reporting Analyst to join our team! Reporting and timely data play a critical role in our customers' business operations. As a Reporting Analyst, you will focus on creating, updating, and refining SQL reports tailored to our customer environments. Additionally, you'll utilise Power BI to develop dynamic queries and visually engaging dashboards that provide insightful decision-making for both our organisation and clients




Your role is not only technical but integral in understanding how customers utilise scheduling software. Your expertise in SQL scripting, software report writing, and Power BI utilisation will empower our organisation and clients to make data-driven decisions. You'll be instrumental in crafting informative, visually engaging reports and dashboards using SSRS, Power BI, and SQL.




Your proficiency in software report writing, SQL scripting, and understanding customer software utilisation will be key. Being a self-directed worker is essential, persevering and growing as a technical reporting expert, consistently delivering real value. Your responsibilities encompass creating, refining, and iterating customer-facing reports crucial for key operational decisions. Reporting and timely data are fundamental to our customers' business strategies, making your role indispensable. Your expertise, especially in Power BI, will significantly enhance our customers' experience, enabling them to optimise their operations effectively.




Our culture is extremely important to us, we are looking for an individual who would enjoy working with a talented, friendly and highly collaborative team!




/About us:

We’re a friendly team of experts, optimists, big-thinkers and problem-solvers, and are lucky enough to work with some of the largest and most successful companies in the world. We’ve had an exciting journey so far! Expanding rapidly in response to the success of our products in the market, so we’re on the lookout for more brilliant people to join our team.

myairops is a leader of SaaS products to the aviation industry with customers across the globe with diverse challenges including corporate flight departments, charter brokers, medical providers, military and business aviation operators.




/More about the role:

The Reporting Analyst is part of the Support function, working within our Customer Success Team.




/ Primary Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

Report Creation and Design: Build and create a variety of reports in SSRS and Power BI, tailored to customer specifications, ensuring the delivery of meaningful insights.
Data Analysis and Querying: Execute SQL scripts for data analysis, leveraging SQL and SSRS to design and query customer-facing reports, showcasing a keen eye for detail and accuracy.
Documentation and Process Improvement: Create comprehensive documentation and processes, capturing knowledge and lessons learned. Foster a culture of continuous improvement by implementing learnings to prevent recurrence and elevate the customer experience.
Collaboration and Team Focus: Collaborate closely with the wider team to drive continuous improvement initiatives related to reporting and data, leveraging collective expertise for optimal outcomes.
Dynamic Dashboard Creation: Utilise Power BI to create dynamic queries and visually engaging dashboards, aligned with customer requirements, to facilitate insightful decision-making.
Problem Solving and Communication: Tackle data query challenges with tenacity and precision, while confidently communicating with both technical and non-technical colleagues.
Customer Value and Relationship Building: Design reports and dashboards with a customer-centric approach, ensuring they add real value to our customers and strengthen relationships, both internally and externally.
Continuous Learning and Adaptability: Stay updated with the latest advancements in reporting tools and techniques, adapting swiftly to changes to maintain efficiency and effectiveness




/Skills, Qualifications and Experience required:




/Essential:

Technical Skills: Proficiency in SSRS, Power BI, and SQL for report creation, data analysis, querying, and dashboard design. With demonstrable professional experience in report creation, data analysis, and dashboard design, preferably in a SaaS software development or related environment
Data Visualisation: Strong understanding of data visualisation principles to create meaningful and visually appealing reports and dashboards. Demonstrated success in creating and delivering impactful reports and dashboards that have added value to customers and contributed to business objectives.
Documentation and Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills for documenting processes, creating comprehensive reports, and effectively conveying insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Experience in creating comprehensive documentation, implementing process improvements, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement within a team or organisation
Problem-Solving and Adaptability: Strong problem-solving skills to tackle data query challenges and adapt quickly to changing reporting tools and techniques.
Collaboration and Teamwork: Ability to work collaboratively within a team and with cross-functional teams to drive continuous improvement initiatives and enhance reporting capabilities.
Customer-Centric Approach: Ability to understand customer requirements and design reports and dashboards that add significant value to our customers and strengthen relationships. Experience working collaboratively with diverse teams and a strong focus on meeting customer needs and expectations through reporting and data analysis.




/Advantageous:

Proficiency in Power BI for querying and dashboard design
Evidence of continuous improvement activities, setting and documenting of best practice standards.
Ability to Utilise Power BI to create dynamic queries and visually engaging dashboards, aligned with customer requirements, to facilitate insightful decision-making




/Location

We're based in Farnborough, Hampshire working on a hybrid schedule of 3 days a week in office, 2 days working from home.




/Benefits include:

Competitive Salary
Group Pension Scheme – up to 5% contributions matched
Life Assurance
Income Protection
Travel Insurance
Private Healthcare (after probation)
Discounts at popular retailers




Due to the volume of applications received, only candidates selected for interview will be contacted. If you do not hear from us within 20 working days then your application has been unsuccessful on this occasion.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Closing Date

2026-08-21

Job Title: Senior Data Analyst

Location: Durrington / Hybrid working

Contract Type: Permanent

Hours: 37 hours per week

Salary: from £45k

About The Role

Southern Water is undertaking one of the most ambitious transformations in the UK water sector.

Over the next five years, we will invest £7.8bn to improve resilience, environmental performance, operational performance and customer outcomes. At the centre of that transformation sits Data, Analytics and AI. We're building a future where decisions are increasingly informed by evidence, where data becomes a strategic asset, and where analytics helps drive better outcomes for customers, communities, regulators and the environment.

Our ambition is to become a truly data-driven organisation, embedding analytics into everyday decision-making and ensuring insight is translated into meaningful action. That means moving beyond reporting and dashboards towards deeper understanding, better recommendations and smarter decisions across every part of the business.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will play a critical role in making that ambition a reality.

This is an opportunity to work on challenging, high-impact problems that matter. You'll partner with stakeholders across the organisation, help shape decisions through evidence and insight, and influence how Southern Water uses data to improve operational and business performance.

For someone who enjoys combining analytical rigour, business understanding and stakeholder influence, this role offers the opportunity to make a genuine impact while helping build the future of analytics within one of the UK's largest water companies.

About The Role


As a Senior Data Analyst, you'll lead medium-to-high complexity analytical work from problem definition through to decision-ready recommendations.
You'll work closely with operational, commercial and strategic stakeholders to understand business challenges, frame analytical approaches and deliver clear, actionable insight. You'll help move conversations beyond reporting and into understanding causes, opportunities and decisions.
Alongside delivering your own analysis, you'll contribute to raising analytical standards across the organisation, helping build a culture where evidence, critical thinking and curiosity drive better outcomes.
This role sits at the intersection of data, business and decision-making, making it ideal for someone who enjoys solving real-world problems and influencing meaningful change.


What You'll Be Doing

Solve High-Value Business Problems


Lead end-to-end analytical initiatives, from understanding the business problem through to delivering recommendations and measurable outcomes.
Translate complex or ambiguous questions into clear analytical approaches, hypotheses and success measures.
Identify opportunities where data and analytics can improve decisions, performance or customer outcomes.
Help stakeholders move from intuition-based decision making towards evidence-based action.


Deliver Insight That Drives Action


Design and deliver high-quality analytical products, including dashboards, metrics, models and decision-support tools.
Transform complex analysis into clear, compelling stories that influence business decisions.
Communicate findings confidently to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Ensure insights are actionable, relevant and aligned to business priorities.


Build Trusted Analytical Products


Develop robust metrics, reusable analytical assets and automated solutions that support recurring decision-making.
Ensure analytical outputs are reliable, transparent and fit for purpose.
Work closely with data engineering and BI teams to improve data availability, quality and accessibility.
Contribute to the development of scalable analytical approaches that can be reused across multiple business areas.


Raise Analytical Standards


Review and assure analytical work produced by colleagues.
Support the adoption of analytical standards, frameworks and best practices.
Promote good analytical thinking, quality assurance and evidence-led decision making.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of methods, tooling and ways of working.


Support And Develop Others


Coach and mentor analysts through feedback, collaboration and knowledge sharing.
Support the growth of analytical capability across the wider team.
Contribute to communities of practice, learning initiatives and capability development activities.
Help create an environment where analysts are supported, challenged and able to develop their careers.


What Success Looks Like


In this role, you will help create an environment where:
Business decisions are supported by trusted evidence and insight.
Analytical products are reliable, repeatable and consistently valuable.
Stakeholders understand both what is happening and why.
Data is used more effectively to improve operational and business outcomes.
Analytical capability continues to mature across the organisation.
Colleagues grow in confidence, capability and impact.
Most importantly, your work will help ensure analytics creates measurable value and delivers meaningful outcomes for customers, the environment and the business.


Essential

What You'll Bring


Proven experience delivering high-value analytical work within a complex organisation.
Strong capability in problem framing, analytical design and method selection.
Experience translating business challenges into clear analytical approaches and recommendations.
Strong data storytelling and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex findings clearly to non-technical audiences.
Experience designing and developing metrics, dashboards, models or analytical products that support decision-making.
Ability to influence stakeholders, challenge assumptions and drive evidence-based conversations.
Experience working with large and complex datasets.
Strong analytical thinking, curiosity and problem-solving skills.

Closing Date

2026-08-21

Job Title: Senior Data Analyst

Location: Durrington / Hybrid working

Contract Type: Permanent

Hours: 37 hours per week

Salary: from £45k

About The Role

Southern Water is undertaking one of the most ambitious transformations in the UK water sector.

Over the next five years, we will invest £7.8bn to improve resilience, environmental performance, operational performance and customer outcomes. At the centre of that transformation sits Data, Analytics and AI. We're building a future where decisions are increasingly informed by evidence, where data becomes a strategic asset, and where analytics helps drive better outcomes for customers, communities, regulators and the environment.

Our ambition is to become a truly data-driven organisation, embedding analytics into everyday decision-making and ensuring insight is translated into meaningful action. That means moving beyond reporting and dashboards towards deeper understanding, better recommendations and smarter decisions across every part of the business.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will play a critical role in making that ambition a reality.

This is an opportunity to work on challenging, high-impact problems that matter. You'll partner with stakeholders across the organisation, help shape decisions through evidence and insight, and influence how Southern Water uses data to improve operational and business performance.

For someone who enjoys combining analytical rigour, business understanding and stakeholder influence, this role offers the opportunity to make a genuine impact while helping build the future of analytics within one of the UK's largest water companies.

About The Role

  • As a Senior Data Analyst, you'll lead medium-to-high complexity analytical work from problem definition through to decision-ready recommendations.
  • You'll work closely with operational, commercial and strategic stakeholders to understand business challenges, frame analytical approaches and deliver clear, actionable insight. You'll help move conversations beyond reporting and into understanding causes, opportunities and decisions.
  • Alongside delivering your own analysis, you'll contribute to raising analytical standards across the organisation, helping build a culture where evidence, critical thinking and curiosity drive better outcomes.
  • This role sits at the intersection of data, business and decision-making, making it ideal for someone who enjoys solving real-world problems and influencing meaningful change.

What You'll Be Doing

Solve High-Value Business Problems

  • Lead end-to-end analytical initiatives, from understanding the business problem through to delivering recommendations and measurable outcomes.
  • Translate complex or ambiguous questions into clear analytical approaches, hypotheses and success measures.
  • Identify opportunities where data and analytics can improve decisions, performance or customer outcomes.
  • Help stakeholders move from intuition-based decision making towards evidence-based action.

Deliver Insight That Drives Action

  • Design and deliver high-quality analytical products, including dashboards, metrics, models and decision-support tools.
  • Transform complex analysis into clear, compelling stories that influence business decisions.
  • Communicate findings confidently to both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Ensure insights are actionable, relevant and aligned to business priorities.

Build Trusted Analytical Products

  • Develop robust metrics, reusable analytical assets and automated solutions that support recurring decision-making.
  • Ensure analytical outputs are reliable, transparent and fit for purpose.
  • Work closely with data engineering and BI teams to improve data availability, quality and accessibility.
  • Contribute to the development of scalable analytical approaches that can be reused across multiple business areas.

Raise Analytical Standards

  • Review and assure analytical work produced by colleagues.
  • Support the adoption of analytical standards, frameworks and best practices.
  • Promote good analytical thinking, quality assurance and evidence-led decision making.
  • Contribute to the continuous improvement of methods, tooling and ways of working.

Support And Develop Others

  • Coach and mentor analysts through feedback, collaboration and knowledge sharing.
  • Support the growth of analytical capability across the wider team.
  • Contribute to communities of practice, learning initiatives and capability development activities.
  • Help create an environment where analysts are supported, challenged and able to develop their careers.

What Success Looks Like

  • In this role, you will help create an environment where:
  • Business decisions are supported by trusted evidence and insight.
  • Analytical products are reliable, repeatable and consistently valuable.
  • Stakeholders understand both what is happening and why.
  • Data is used more effectively to improve operational and business outcomes.
  • Analytical capability continues to mature across the organisation.
  • Colleagues grow in confidence, capability and impact.
  • Most importantly, your work will help ensure analytics creates measurable value and delivers meaningful outcomes for customers, the environment and the business.

Essential

What You'll Bring

  • Proven experience delivering high-value analytical work within a complex organisation.
  • Strong capability in problem framing, analytical design and method selection.
  • Experience translating business challenges into clear analytical approaches and recommendations.
  • Strong data storytelling and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex findings clearly to non-technical audiences.
  • Experience designing and developing metrics, dashboards, models or analytical products that support decision-making.
  • Ability to influence stakeholders, challenge assumptions and drive evidence-based conversations.
  • Experience working with large and complex datasets.
  • Strong analytical thinking, curiosity and problem-solving skills.
  • Experience coaching, reviewing or supporting other analysts through mentoring, feedback or collaborative working.
  • Practical experience using analytical and visualisation tools such as Power BI, Databricks, SQL, Alteryx or equivalent technologies.

Desirable

  • Experience working with modern cloud-based analytics platforms and data ecosystems.
  • Knowledge of semantic modelling, data products or scalable analytics architectures.
  • Exposure to advanced analytics, statistical techniques or predictive modelling.
  • Familiarity with data governance, analytical quality assurance or data ethics.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Murphy is recruiting for a Data Analyst delivering major water infrastructure projects in partnership with multiple clients across the UK.

We provide end-to-end services for major water and sewage companies such as Thames Water, Severn Trent Water, Northern Ireland Water, Yorkshire Water and United Utilities, and for several water-only companies including SES Water, Bristol Water and South East Water.

We also provide a full design, build, operate and maintain service for Uisce Éireann and we currently operate over 25 of their facilities, ensuring they run efficiently and comply fully with EPA regulations.

A day in the life of a Murphy Data Analyst:


Working closely with business reporting stakeholders to understanding reporting and analytical requirements to acquire and present relevant datasets.
Handle large and complex datasets from Primavera P6, SAP SuccessFactors and other key construction centric systems.
Verify the integrity of extracted data.
Awareness of, and support embedding and applying revised Data Governance and Information Security standards and best practices.
Applying Murphy best practices to code management, documentation and release.
Help establish good working practices for the work delivered.
Working as part of the Planning and Project Controls Function, alongside the Resource Management Team.
Project work to be allocated as required, during the duration of the project.
Liaising with planners, operational staff and other technical staff.
Assisting with the designing of maintenance procedures and putting them into operation.
Providing user training, support and feedback as required.
Build effective relationships with all relevant stakeholders.
Provide support and guidance to other team members.
Attend sites to assist remote application users as required.
Ad hoc activities as deemed appropriate by the Head of Planning.
Design and develop applications and workflows using POwer apps and Power Automate to streamline and automate business data processes.
Debugging, testing and troubleshooting systems and applications


Still interested, does this sound like you?


Detailed knowledge and practical experience with Primavera P6 and PowerBI, Power Automate, Power Apps, Dataverse
Experienced with connecting disparate data sources, particularly: Dataverse, Sharepoint, P6
Write and maintain python scripts for data processing, backend logic, API integration and workflow automation
Integrate python processes with Power Platforms solutions
Demonstrable experience with Visual Studio and Azure DevOps toolsets.
Working knowledge of ETL and data ingestion (API knowledge).
Solutions architecture
Understanding of relational databases and data modelling
Experience within construction and/or civil engineering processes and applications.
Happy to travel nationwide across multitude of Murphy projects


What’s in it for you?


27 days holiday, plus bank holidays with the option to buy an additional 2 days holiday each year, holidays increase with length of service
Discretionary annual bonus and annual salary review
Above market rate contributory pension scheme
Life assurance, health screening and enhanced sick pay
Enhanced maternity and paternity pay and a maternity returners bonus
Extra weeks holiday for all employees getting married and a wedding bonus
Subsidised canteen facilities in core locations
Dedicated and continued investment in your professional development
Other Murphy benefits include retail discounts and cashback, discounted gym memberships, cycle to work scheme etc


About Murphy

Murphy is formally recognised as a sector leading employer, Platinum Investors In People accredited along with reported operating profit up 57% on prior year. At Murphy, we are incredibly proud of our diverse workforce. Not only do our people represent over 60 different nationalities, but they also represent a wide range of backgrounds, ethnicities and ages. With a forward order book of £5.8bn and £23m Invested in environmentally friendly plant in 2024 there really is #MoretoMurphy

If you are unable to apply via the usual process, please call James Hing on 07353 134946 to discuss in more detail.

Murphy is unable to employ anyone who does not have the legal right to live and work in the UK

Title: Digital Sales Analyst
Description:
Join us as a Digital Sales Analyst and help shape the future of digital travel. You'll turn data into decisions that enhance customer experience and drive commercial success across our UK&I digital channels.

The role will be published until Friday 31st July

About Our Offer


Personal benefits: Attractive remuneration, discretionary bonus schemes, generous travel benefits, extensive health & well-being support, and more.
Flexible working: Work is something you do, not somewhere you go. We encourage a healthy work-life balance with a dynamic working environment.
A career to shape: Access the TUI Learning Hub to level-up and reach your ambitions.
Broaden your network: We champion intercultural collaboration and provide opportunities to work on global projects and teams.
Community: Get involved with incredible local charity and sustainability initiatives like the TUI Care Foundation.


About The Job

You'll be joining the Digital Sales Analytics team at a formative moment — we're growing the team and building out how we join up reporting and insight across the business, and you'll help shape how that takes form, not just inherit it.Support core trading analytics, reporting and deep-dive insights across sales, traffic and conversion to inform digital growth strategy and commercial decisions.Help generate actionable recommendations to optimise commercial performance, marketing effectiveness and merchandising strategies.Build and maintain scalable, automated reporting and dashboards, supporting self-service and strong data governance (primarily using Adobe Analytics).Analyse promotional performance, pricing changes and incident impacts — including incrementality and cannibalisation analysis — to quantify revenue risk and support trading decisions.Support A/B and multivariate testing by helping define success metrics, analysing results, and translating findings into optimisation opportunities.Partner with commercial, merchandising and marketing teams to understand customer behaviour, identify sales trends and support personalisation initiatives.Communicate clear, insight-led findings to stakeholders across the business.

About You

Experience in digital analytics or a data-driven commercial role, with an analytical mindset and commercial curiosity.Good communication skills, with the ability to translate data into clear, actionable insight for non-technical audiences.Hands-on experience with Adobe Analytics (our primary platform) — familiarity with Adobe Target a plus.Experience with, or willingness to develop skills in, Google-stack tools such as GA4, BigQuery or Looker Studio.Comfortable working with large, complex datasets and drawing out meaningful insight independently.Strong Excel skills; SQL experience useful but not essential.A collaborative, proactive approach — happy to build relationships across teams and contribute ideas as the function grows.

From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

Title: Aggregator Data Analyst
Description:
hackajob is collaborating with Dominos Pizza UK & Ireland to connect them with exceptional professionals for this role.

About The Role

Aggregator Data Analyst

Milton Keynes or Manchester | Hybrid (2 days in office)

At Domino’s, data helps us make better decisions every day. As an Aggregator Data Analyst, you’ll turn complex data from platforms like Uber Eats and Just Eat into clear, useful insights that support our teams and franchise partners.

This is a great opportunity to shape how we report and understand aggregator performance, helping drive smarter decisions across the business.

What You’ll Bring


Experience in a data or analytics role (typically 3+ years)
Strong skills in SQL, Excel and Power BI (or similar tools)
A degree in a relevant field (e.g. Data, Business, Maths)
Confidence working with large datasets and multiple data sources
Ability to explain data clearly to non-technical audiences


It’s a Bonus If You Have


Experience in retail, QSR, or commercial/trading environments
Knowledge of Python or R
Experience improving reporting or building dashboards


What You’ll Be Doing


Producing weekly and monthly reports on sales, demand, discounting and performance
Building and improving aggregator reporting packs for internal teams and franchisees
Tracking promotion and campaign performance across aggregator platforms
Analysing trends, identifying risks and explaining performance clearly
Working with Data Engineering to improve reporting, automation and data quality
Supporting teams with insights that guide day‑to‑day trading decisions


How We’ll Support You


The chance to shape and improve reporting in a growing area of the business
A collaborative team of analysts and data specialists
Opportunities to develop your skills and take on more responsibility as the function grows
A hybrid working approach that balances flexibility and team connection


About Us & Belonging At Domino’s

At Domino’s, everyone brings something different—and that’s what makes us stronger. We value clear thinking, teamwork and people who take pride in what they do.

You’ll be part of a team that turns data into action, helping the business grow while supporting our franchise partners.

You bring the flavour. Together, we deliver delicious.

About Us

Domino's UK & Ireland

Who are we? A forward-thinking, inclusive world's leading pizza company:

Domino's is the world's leading pizza company, with over 1,300 stores across the UK and Ireland. We're committed to using the freshest, highest-quality ingredients to create over 110 million delectable pizzas each year. Plus, we're a tech-savvy and data driven bunch – 90% of our system sales come through digital channels and 75% of our digital orders coming via the app we have 9.5 million active app customers!

Our mission? To be the go-to choice for food delivery and collection by crafting hot, freshly made pizzas that earn us accolades and the loyalty of millions of pizza enthusiasts worldwide. We're all about innovation, growth, and a commitment to excellence.

At Domino's, we embrace the power of diversity and foster an inclusive environment where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered. We celebrate the uniqueness of each individual, regardless of their identity, background, or any other characteristic. When you join our team, you join a family that welcomes and celebrates authenticity!

Our recipe for success? Our DomiDNA:


We Mean Business
We Always Deliver
We’re Open to New Flavours
We’re Tastier Together


If you share our values and are ready to be part of a world-class team that's as passionate about people as we are about pizza, apply now and join the Domino's UK & Ireland family!

Title: Reward & People Data Analyst
Description:
This is an exciting new dual role sitting at the heart of our People Team, playing a crucial part in both our Reward strategy and the implementation and ongoing administration of our new HRIS system. If you love working with data, have a passion for people analytics, and want to make a real impact on how Butlin’s rewards and looks after its teams, this could be the perfect fit.




You'll become a trusted member of our People Team, supporting the systems, data and reward programmes that help our teams thrive. On the Reward side, you'll help deliver some of our most important annual people processes, including our Annual Pay Review, bonus schemes and legislative reporting such as Gender Pay Gap reporting. You'll also keep our existing reward and benefits programmes running smoothly - from B Rewarded! and Backstage Pass to Long Service Awards and our salary sacrifice car scheme, while supporting the introduction of new benefits and incentives as we continue to evolve our offering.




Alongside this, you'll use data to tell the story behind our people. You'll produce regular reports, respond to business requests for analysis and benchmarking, and provide insights that support informed decision-making across the organisation. As we introduce our new HRIS, you'll play an important part in preparing, cleansing and validating People data, helping ensure our information is accurate, consistent and ready for the future. You'll also support data governance activities and help document People process that will underpin our new system.




About You

You'll enjoy working with data but never lose sight of the people behind it. You're analytical, organised and detail-oriented, with the confidence to question anomalies and the curiosity to understand what the numbers are really telling you. You'll be comfortable managing confidential information with integrity and enjoy collaborating with colleagues across different teams to find practical solutions and improve ways of working.




Skills, Knowledge and Expertise




We're looking for someone who has:




Experience in HR, Reward or People Analytics with a strong focus on data analysis.
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills.
Experience creating engaging data visualisations, ideally using Power BI, Tableau or R.
A good understanding of HR processes, reward principles and legal compliance, including payroll and tax considerations.
Excellent attention to detail and a proactive approach to identifying and resolving data issues. Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain insights clearly to different audiences.
The ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively to deadlines in a fast-paced environment.




It would be great if you also have:

Experience with statistical modelling or descriptive statistics.
Previous experience working with an HRIS platform or supporting HR systems implementation.
Experience administering reward or benefits within a large, multi-site organisation.




About Butlin's

Did you know Billy Butlin was the first person to introduce dodgems to the UK? That's right, 'fun' is in our DNA, and we've been delighting guests for almost 90 years. Ask any of our team, past and present, and one of the best things they'll say about working at Butlin's is our culture.




As The Home of Entertainment, we pull out all the stops to provide our guests with an 'Altogether More Entertaining and Fun Break', from all-action family holidays to adult-only Big Weekenders, brought to life through our three mighty values. So, if you're looking for a role where you can Create Smiles, where you aren't afraid to Get Stuck In and where the team genuinely Care For Each Other, we think you'll fit right in!

If you love working with data, have a passion for people analytics, and want to make a real impact on how Butlin’s rewards and looks after its teams, this could be the perfect fit.


You'll become a trusted member of our People Team, supporting the systems, data and reward programmes that help our teams thrive. On the Reward side, you'll help deliver some of our most important annual people processes, including our Annual Pay Review, bonus schemes and legislative reporting such as Gender Pay Gap reporting. You'll also keep our existing reward and benefits programmes running smoothly - from B Rewarded! and Backstage Pass to Long Service Awards and our salary sacrifice car scheme, while supporting the introduction of new benefits and incentives as we continue to evolve our offering.


Alongside this, you'll use data to tell the story behind our people. You'll produce regular reports, respond to business requests for analysis and benchmarking, and provide insights that support informed decision-making across the organisation. As we introduce our new HRIS, you'll play an important part in preparing, cleansing and validating People data, helping ensure our information is accurate, consistent and ready for the future. You'll also support data governance activities and help document People process that will underpin our new system.


About You

You'll enjoy working with data but never lose sight of the people behind it. You're analytical, organised and detail-oriented, with the confidence to question anomalies and the curiosity to understand what the numbers are really telling you. You'll be comfortable managing confidential information with integrity and enjoy collaborating with colleagues across different teams to find practical solutions and improve ways of working.


Skills, Knowledge and Expertise


We're looking for someone who has:


  • Experience in HR, Reward or People Analytics with a strong focus on data analysis.
  • Advanced Microsoft Excel skills.
  • Experience creating engaging data visualisations, ideally using Power BI, Tableau or R.
  • A good understanding of HR processes, reward principles and legal compliance, including payroll and tax considerations.
  • Excellent attention to detail and a proactive approach to identifying and resolving data issues. Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain insights clearly to different audiences.
  • The ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively to deadlines in a fast-paced environment.


It would be great if you also have:

  • Experience with statistical modelling or descriptive statistics.
  • Previous experience working with an HRIS platform or supporting HR systems implementation.
  • Experience administering reward or benefits within a large, multi-site organisation.


About Butlin's

Did you know Billy Butlin was the first person to introduce dodgems to the UK? That's right, 'fun' is in our DNA, and we've been delighting guests for almost 90 years. Ask any of our team, past and present, and one of the best things they'll say about working at Butlin's is our culture.


As The Home of Entertainment, we pull out all the stops to provide our guests with an 'Altogether More Entertaining and Fun Break', from all-action family holidays to adult-only Big Weekenders, brought to life through our three mighty values. So, if you're looking for a role where you can Create Smiles, where you aren't afraid to Get Stuck In and where the team genuinely Care For Each Other, we think you'll fit right in!

Title: Lead Game Analyst - Old School RuneScape
Description:
Office location: Cambridge, UK - This position offers flexible working options, including predominantly remote work. However, applicants should be based (or willing to relocate) within a comfortable commuting distance of our office to attend onsite as required.

Are you a data-driven leader who wants to transform player behaviour into meaningful insights that shape the future of one of the world's most iconic MMORPGs?

As Lead Analyst on Old School RuneScape, you'll lead a team of analysts responsible for uncovering the insights that drive better player experiences, smarter product decisions, and long-term business success. Working closely with Product, Design, Engineering, and senior leadership, you'll help shape the roadmap of Old School RuneScape by turning complex data into clear, actionable recommendations.

This is a highly influential role where you'll combine technical expertise, analytical thinking, and leadership to ensure data sits at the heart of decision-making across one of gaming's most successful live service titles.

What you'll be doing:


Lead, mentor, and develop a team of Product Analysts, fostering a culture of curiosity, collaboration, and analytical excellence.
Build strong relationships with Old School RuneScape teams to develop a deep understanding of product strategy, player needs, and business priorities.
Deliver insights and business intelligence that directly influence product strategy, feature development, and roadmap planning.
Transform complex datasets into clear recommendations that support better decision-making across Product, Design, and Leadership.
Query, analyse, and interpret large datasets to uncover opportunities to improve player engagement, retention, and overall game experience.
Partner closely with Engineering and Design teams to develop and enhance game telemetry and data collection.
Collaborate with central Data teams to define data requirements and contribute to the evolution of shared data products.
Develop deep knowledge of Old School RuneScape's gameplay systems, mechanics, and player behaviours to provide meaningful context to analysis.
Champion data-driven thinking across the game team and promote best practices in analytics and reporting.


What we're looking for:


Experience leading or mentoring analysts within an analytics, business intelligence, or data-focused environment.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets and data cleansing techniques.
A demonstrable understanding of video game player behaviours, particularly in MMORPGs.
Familiarity with modern data platforms and architectures such as AWS, Snowflake, and OLAP technologies.
Experience using data visualisation tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to communicate complex findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Excellent stakeholder management skills and the ability to influence decision-making across multiple disciplines.


What we offer:

When you join Jagex you can look forward to a generous Perks & Benefits package including:


Private Healthcare, including Dental Plan.
Minimum 6% Pension contributions.
Employee Assistance Programme & onsite Counselling.
Life Insurance.
Discretionary annual performance bonus.
Enhanced family leave policies from day 1.
Flexible working hours.
25 days annual leave + Bank holidays & the option to buy/sell holidays + so much more!


Inclusion & Accessibility Statement

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

About Jagex:

We are Jagex: The RuneScape Company- home to one of gaming's most enduring success stories. We create deep, community-powered games with worlds that evolve, inspire, and endure. Our flagship MMORPG franchise, RuneScape has welcomed over 300 million player accounts, inspiring generations of players across PC and mobile for 25 years.

As we begin our 26th year, we are accelerating our strategy to expand RuneScape into a broader ecosystem of forever games and connected experiences, grounded in a renewed commitment to integrity and fair play. This includes the reduction of monetisation mechanics, as well as the launch of RuneScape: DragonWilds into Steam Early Access in 2025 representing a major step in building a multi-genre future for the RuneScape universe. We remain focused on building enduring worlds, empowering player-led innovation, and laying the foundations for the boldest era in RuneScape's history. For more information, visit www.jagex.com.

Headquartered in Cambridge, United Kingdom, We are home to passionate creators, storytellers, engineers, and even a few dreamers, all united by a single mission- to shape the future of online worlds and empower players to make them their own. We're looking for people to join us who share our ambitions & values - We foster an inclusive workplace built on trust, autonomy, and a shared passion for creating experiences which inspire our communities. Our values guide everything we do: We Embrace Fellowship by collaborating and sharing openly. We Hone our Craft by always learning and improving; We Honour our Vows by being honest and transparent; We are our Customers' Heroes by striving to understand our team & player needs, and We Seize The Day by making the most of every opportunity.

Join us. The next era of RuneScape - and the next chapter of Jagex: The RuneScape Company - starts now.

We are committed to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, and we recruit, develop, and promote based on merit and ability.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you during the recruitment process, please let us know when you're invited to interview.

Title: DEMAND ANALYST CITRUS UK
Description:
Who We Are

We are Fyffes, one of the world's leading tropical produce companies. For more than 130 years, we have sourced, shipped and delivered high-quality fresh produce globally. With over 4,000 employees and a strong international network of growers and suppliers, we are committed to shaping wellbeing for the world through sustainable growth, innovation and strong partnerships.

Now we're looking for a Demand Analyst Citrus UK to join our team, turning UK citrus demand into accurate forecasts, insight and planning support.

Here's What You Will Do

Support the commercial team by analysing UK citrus demand and translating orders, forecast trends, promotional activity, seasonality and market dynamics into actionable planning insight. Your work will support service, availability, waste reduction and weekly and seasonal planning.

You will connect demand with sourcing, supply chain and operational teams, providing clear analysis, early risk visibility and performance reporting. The role is analytical and detail-driven, with a strong focus on forecast accuracy, demand visibility and planning discipline.

Some Key Responsibilities


Analyse UK citrus demand using orders, historical sales, promotions, seasonality, weather impacts and market trends.
Prepare and maintain accurate weekly and seasonal citrus forecasts with clearly documented assumptions.
Track forecast versus actual demand and order movements, identifying variances, trends and root causes.
Produce demand, forecast, order and service reporting covering accuracy, bias, fill rate, availability, waste and key service drivers.
Support demand reviews, updates and planning inputs, providing clear insight for internal alignment.
Identify supply-demand gaps, shortfalls, oversupply, timing risks and demand changes early.
Support scenario analysis, mitigation planning, allocation discussions and improvements in reporting, communication and forecasting processes.


What You Need To Bring


Degree in Supply Chain, Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Agriculture or a related field.
1-3 years of experience in demand analysis, demand planning, supply chain, commercial analysis, fresh produce or FMCG.
Experience with retail demand, forecasts, orders, promotions or seasonal planning is advantageous.
Fresh produce, short shelf-life supply chain or citrus experience is beneficial.
Strong Excel and data analysis capability, with ability to translate large datasets into clear insight.
Fluent English; Spanish or another language is advantageous.
Strong attention to detail, proactive communication, structured problem-solving, ownership and ability to work in a fast-paced perishable product environment.


What It Is Like To Work At Fyffes

We are ambitious but grounded, combining high performance with a people-first culture. Our leadership principles guide us every day: we play to win as one Fyffes, shape the future, cultivate a growth mindset, are transparent and inclusive, and are moved by doing good. We thrive on diversity and treat all candidates equally.

Interested? We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Financial Data Analyst
Description:
Role Summary


Responsibilities: Be the data-driven partner the Finance team relies on. Translate business needs into analytics, deliver Power BI dashboards, and define meaningful KPIs across functions to inform decisions
Salary: £40,000 to £45,000 per annum (please note that we have established salary bands that are determined by your level of educational attainment to ensure a fair and consistent approach for all applicants, eliminating the need for individual negotiations)
Benefits: Discretionary profit share bonuses paid twice per year, hybrid and flexible working options, generous holiday allowance, private medical insurance, critical illness cover, income protection, full funding for external training, and more
Role Type: Full-time, permanent role
Start Date: The role will ideally commence in September or October 2026, and you will be asked to state your availability on your application form
Application Deadlines: The application deadline for this role is August 3rd, 2026
Location: This role is available in our Global Headquarters in Cambridge


About The Role

We are looking for a curious, commercially minded Data Analyst to join our Finance and Operations team and play a key role in how data drives decision-making across the business.

This is more than a reporting role. It's an opportunity to influence strategy, improve performance, and build scalable data solutions that senior leaders rely on.

Key responsibilities:


Analyse financial and operational performance to identify trends, risks and opportunities
Define, track and evolve KPIs and key financial metrics
Communicate insights clearly through strong data story telling
Design and Deliver engaging Power BI dashboards and reports to support business decisions
Support forecasting, budgeting and scenario modelling through robust, data-led insights


You will combine strong analytical thinking with hands-on technical expertise, working across tools such as Power BI, Excel, SQL (where relevant) and programming languages like Python or VBA. This includes:


Using advanced Excel, Power Query, Power Pivot, automation and scripting to streamline data processes
Applying programming skills to automate workflows, deepen analysis and enhance reporting capabilities
Building scalable data models and continuously improving processes through automation and optimisation


Working closely with Finance and the People team, you'll help transform how data is used to plan, forecast and drive performance across the organisation. A core part of the role will involve:


Bringing together data from multiple systems, including from multiple systems, including data via APIs across different ERP platforms
Creating a single, reliable source of truth to enable consistent and scalable reporting
Driving improvements in governance and automation


You'll also have the opportunity to explore emerging technologies, including agentic AI, using intelligent automation and AI-driven workflows to further enhance insight and efficiency.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working across the full data lifecycle and is confident both technically and commercially, from extracting and modelling data through to delivering clear, impactful insights.

About Costello Medical

Costello Medical has grown organically since our foundation in 2008 to become one of the most established independently owned agencies delivering medical communications and health economic services. We work with a wide range of clients, including the industry's most successful pharmaceutical and medical technology companies, patient and public health bodies, and charitable organisations. We have been listed in the Top 100 Best Companies to Work For list since 2017, the Boston Globe's Top Places to Work list in 2025 and were proud to receive B Corporation accreditation in 2022. Learn more about us, our work, and our mission on our website: https://www.costellomedical.com/

Requirements

About You

Essential requirements for the role are:


Degree in a quantitative field (minimum 2:1 or equivalent). We welcome applications from a diverse range of specialisms, including but not limited to; Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Finance, Data Science, Data Analytics
A minimum of 2 to 3 years of experience in data analytics work in a finance context
Strong Power BI skills, including building and maintaining dashboards/reports
Solid finance/FP&A knowledge, including the ability to define and track KPIs and financial metrics
Data cleaning, validation, and quality assurance with a focus on data accuracy and reliability
Understanding of data governance, security, and privacy best practices
Excellent communication and data storytelling, including the ability to be able to present insights to both finance and non-finance audiences
Proven cross-functional collaboration and stakeholder management
Ability to document data sources, transformations, methodologies, and dashboards; maintain data lineage
Strong time management, prioritisation, and ability to work in a fast-paced environment


Desired requirements for the role are:


Experience with a coding tool (e.g Python) for advanced analyses or familiarity with statistical methods
Proficient SQL for data extraction, joining sources and shaping datasets
Advanced Excel skills (Power Query, Power Pivot)
FP&A experience, including budgeting, forecasting, scenario modelling
Experience designing robust data models (star/snowflake schemas) and scalable analytics solutions
Ability to automate/report scheduling and improve dashboard delivery processes
Familiarity with data storytelling frameworks and dashboard usability improvement
Experience within the ‘'big 4'' professional services networks


Benefits

What We Offer

Alongside our award-winning company culture, where every team member is celebrated, respected, and has their voice heard, we are proud to offer a comprehensive benefits package that includes:


A starting salary of £40,000 to £45,000 per annum, depending on your previous experience, as well as a discretionary profit share bonus paid twice per year. Please note that we have established salary bands to ensure a fair and consistent approach for all applicants, eliminating the need for individual negotiations
25 days' annual leave plus bank and public holidays, as well as a holiday buy and sell scheme
Flexible working hours and the chance to work from home for up to half of your working time after passing probation
Flexible benefits scheme offering cash payments, additional pension contributions and more
Private Medical Insurance which offers comprehensive cover on a "medical history disregard" basis
Paid study leave and funding for external qualifications
Critical Illness Cover, Income Protection and Life Assurance
Paid and unpaid sabbaticals based on length of service


Learn more about our full reward package and the other benefits of working for Costello Medical: https://www.costellomedical.com/careers/working-at-costello-medical/benefits-package-in-china/

Hybrid Working Policy: We believe that having face-to-face time in the office holds many benefits and is central to ensuring that the community we have created, where people have a workplace they belong to and feel part of, is never lost. At the same time, we recognise that homeworking can improve work life balance, reduce commuting times and costs, and provide the opportunity to juggle personal commitments.

Therefore, we offer flexible working arrangements that allow our colleagues who have passed probation to work from home up to half their time, measured across a 2-week rolling period. During your probationary period (normally the first 6 months of the role), you will be able to work from home 1 day per week.

The Application Process

You are required to submit your CV and a cover letter via our online application form. In your cover letter, you must explain why you think you would be suited to the role and why you would like to join Costello Medical, with examples to support both. Your CV should clearly state the dates of all qualifications and grades achieved where applicable.

Then, our recruitment process includes a telephone interview, and a technical assessment for you to complete in your own time. If successful, you will be invited to an on-site interview.

As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workforce and, throughout the recruitment process, there are a number of reasonable adjustments we can provide: https://www.costellomedical.com/reasonable-adjustments/

Please note that, whilst we embrace AI to innovate and improve processes, your role may involve working with confidential data that cannot be uploaded to AI. Therefore, it is important for us to assess your own skills and abilities, so we recommend that you do not rely solely on this technology to generate any application materials.

Visa Sponsorship

Dependant on the salary you are offered and due to the Skilled Worker visa regulations, you will only be eligible for visa sponsorship if you meet at least one of the following criteria:


You are classified as a New Entrant, which includes applicants who are under the age of 26, currently studying in the UK or a recent graduate in the UK, amongst other qualifying criteria. Please read more about the New Entrant criteria: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/skilled-worker-visa-caseworker-guidance/skilled-worker-caseworker-guidance-accessible#bookmark63
You have a PhD in a subject that is relevant to the Financial Data Analyst role
You obtained your first Skilled Worker visa in the UK prior to 4th April 2024 and have continuously held a Skilled Worker visa since then (i.e., you will be switching employer)


You will be asked to provide details of your right to work in the UK within your application, which will be used to help assess your eligibility for visa sponsorship.

Please check the following link to read more about the criteria on the UK government website: https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/when-you-can-be-paid-less. Please contact recruitment@costellomedical.com should you have any questions.

  • 25 days' annual leave plus bank and public holidays, as well as a holiday buy and sell scheme
  • Flexible working hours and the chance to work from home for up to half of your working time after passing probation
  • Flexible benefits scheme offering cash payments, additional pension contributions and more
  • Private Medical Insurance which offers comprehensive cover on a "medical history disregard" basis
  • Paid study leave and funding for external qualifications
  • Critical Illness Cover, Income Protection and Life Assurance
  • Paid and unpaid sabbaticals based on length of service

Learn more about our full reward package and the other benefits of working for Costello Medical: https://www.costellomedical.com/careers/working-at-costello-medical/benefits-package-in-china/

Hybrid Working Policy: We believe that having face-to-face time in the office holds many benefits and is central to ensuring that the community we have created, where people have a workplace they belong to and feel part of, is never lost. At the same time, we recognise that homeworking can improve work life balance, reduce commuting times and costs, and provide the opportunity to juggle personal commitments.

Therefore, we offer flexible working arrangements that allow our colleagues who have passed probation to work from home up to half their time, measured across a 2-week rolling period. During your probationary period (normally the first 6 months of the role), you will be able to work from home 1 day per week.

The Application Process

You are required to submit your CV and a cover letter via our online application form. In your cover letter, you must explain why you think you would be suited to the role and why you would like to join Costello Medical, with examples to support both. Your CV should clearly state the dates of all qualifications and grades achieved where applicable.

Then, our recruitment process includes a telephone interview, and a technical assessment for you to complete in your own time. If successful, you will be invited to an on-site interview.

As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workforce and, throughout the recruitment process, there are a number of reasonable adjustments we can provide: https://www.costellomedical.com/reasonable-adjustments/

Please note that, whilst we embrace AI to innovate and improve processes, your role may involve working with confidential data that cannot be uploaded to AI. Therefore, it is important for us to assess your own skills and abilities, so we recommend that you do not rely solely on this technology to generate any application materials.

Visa Sponsorship

Dependant on the salary you are offered and due to the Skilled Worker visa regulations, you will only be eligible for visa sponsorship if you meet at least one of the following criteria:

  • You are classified as a New Entrant, which includes applicants who are under the age of 26, currently studying in the UK or a recent graduate in the UK, amongst other qualifying criteria. Please read more about the New Entrant criteria: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/skilled-worker-visa-caseworker-guidance/skilled-worker-caseworker-guidance-accessible#bookmark63
  • You have a PhD in a subject that is relevant to the Financial Data Analyst role
  • You obtained your first Skilled Worker visa in the UK prior to 4th April 2024 and have continuously held a Skilled Worker visa since then (i.e., you will be switching employer)

You will be asked to provide details of your right to work in the UK within your application, which will be used to help assess your eligibility for visa sponsorship.

Please check the following link to read more about the criteria on the UK government website: https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/when-you-can-be-paid-less. Please contact recruitment@costellomedical.com should you have any questions.

Title: AI Analytics Specialist
Description:
About Tripadvisor

We believe that we are better together, and at Tripadvisor we welcome you for who you are. Our workplace is for everyone, as is our people powered platform. At Tripadvisor, we want you to bring your unique perspective and experiences, so we can collectively revolutionize travel and together find the good out there.

Tripadvisor is the world’s largest online travel site, visited by 390 million travellers each month, and our Experiences business, Viator, is a fast-evolving and highly data-driven part of the organisation.

At Viator, data is at the heart of how we build great products. We use it to understand our customers, improve decision-making, and drive measurable business impact.

About The Role

The Analytics & ML Marketing team is looking for an AI Analytics Specialist who is, at their core, a data analyst — and who is genuinely obsessed with how AI can change the way companies work with data. Not someone who has read about it, but someone who has been building with it, has opinions on it, and is energized by the idea of applying it at scale inside a real organization.

This role sits at the crossroads of analytical craft, AI fluency, and organizational change. You will not be expected to build every piece of the puzzle alone — but you will be the person who understands how the pieces fit, defines what needs to exist, drives it forward with engineers and data teams, and makes sure the whole organization can actually use it. You will diagnose real pain points, shape the right solutions, and bring both technical and non-technical colleagues along for the transformation.

What You'll Do


Diagnose and fix real friction. Talk to marketing colleagues across performance, CRM, personalization, and incentives. Understand where data access is slow, where workflows are overcomplicated, and where decisions get made without the right information — then define and drive solutions that fix those problems at the root, not the surface.
Make data conversational. Champion and help deploy AI-powered interfaces — natural language querying, automated insight summaries, smart alerting — so that any marketing colleague can interrogate data directly, without writing SQL or raising a ticket.
Shape the semantic layer. Work with data and engineering teams to ensure our metrics are canonically defined, consistently named, and structured in a way that makes self-service reliable and AI tools trustworthy. You will not build it alone — but you will own the vision and drive the standards.
Define agentic workflow opportunities. Identify where agentic analytics systems — pipelines that monitor performance, surface anomalies, and trigger proactive insight — would unlock the most value, and work with the right teams to bring them to life.
Automate what should not need a human. Spot recurring analytical workflows — weekly reports, campaign snapshots, performance reviews — and push to replace manual effort with automated, AI-assisted pipelines through prompt engineering and workflow design.
Build org-wide data literacy. Create the tools, training, and shared frameworks that genuinely shift how the marketing organization works with data — making AI tools and self-service analytics accessible to everyone, not just analysts.


Skills & Experience


A strong analyst first — SQL, data modeling, and structured thinking are your foundation, not just a line on your CV
Genuinely excited about AI: you have been building with it, have explored agentic workflows and conversational analytics tools, and have clear views on where it creates real value versus noise
You see a broken workflow and think about how to fix it — you are drawn to real pain points and you know the difference between solving something and patching it
As comfortable running a data literacy workshop for a marketing team as discussing semantic layer design with a data engineer — you move fluently between both worlds
Structured, self-directed, and delivery-oriented — you bring rigor to an enablement program the same way an engineer brings it to code
Bachelor's degree in Analytics, Statistics, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field required; Master's degree preferred. Equivalent practical experience considered.


Required


3-5 years in analytics or data science, ideally in travel, ecommerce, or a marketplace environment
Strong SQL and hands-on experience with modern BI and analytics tooling (Looker, Tableau, Hex, dbt, or similar)
Proven ability to make analytical complexity accessible and actionable for both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Track record of driving adoption of new tools or analytical ways of working across teams
Active, hands-on experience with AI tools — prompt engineering, LLM-powered workflows, or conversational analytics — applied to real problems


Nice to Have


Exposure to agentic analytics concepts or tools, and a view on where they create practical value
Familiarity with semantic layer concepts and tooling (dbt metrics, Cube, LookML, or equivalent)
Understanding of marketing analytics: attribution, funnel analysis, segmentation, and campaign measurement
Python for lightweight automation or tooling
Background in data literacy programs, analytics enablement, or internal CoE initiatives


What Makes This Role Different

Traditional analytics roles are built around depth — owning a domain, producing analysis, answering questions well. This role is built around breadth and leverage: how do you make the whole organization better at using data, not just the analytics team?

AI Analytics Specialist


Makes data questions answerable by anyone
Owns the capability that powers many domains
Redesigns them around AI and self-service
Builds data literacy so colleagues find their own answers
Designs agentic workflows that run end to end
Brings technical and non-technical teams through the change together
Fluent across the AI analytics landscape — semantic layer, conversational tools, automation
Proactively identifies where AI creates the most leverage
Success = decision made faster, without waiting for an analyst


Traditional analytics


Answers data questions
Owns a dashboard or domain
Works with existing workflows
Communicates findings
Automates individual reports
Translates data for stakeholders
Depth in one tool or stack
Reactive to requests
Success = insight delivered
