Title: Data Scientist
Description:
At OQC, we aren't just theorising about the future; we're building it. Born from a philosophy of bold innovation, we've successfully transitioned quantum computing from an academic dream into a commercial reality. The most exciting thing is that we're just getting started and we've recently closed our £260 million Series C funding round - the largest fundraise ever completed by a quantum computing company in Europe.

The Purpose

As a Data Scientist at OQC, you'll help accelerate the development of next-generation quantum hardware through data-driven insight, predictive modelling, and simulation. Working at the intersection of science, engineering, and software, you'll transform complex datasets into actionable intelligence that shapes critical hardware decisions and drives technological progress.

The Role

We're looking for a Data Scientist to develop predictive models, analyse complex engineering datasets, and build the software tooling that enables informed decision-making across our hardware programmes. You'll collaborate closely with hardware, measurement, and software engineers to improve performance, optimise designs, and support the development of future quantum technologies.

What You'll Be Working On


Develop and optimise predictive models to support hardware design, specification, and engineering decision-making
Analyse large, multi-parameter datasets to identify trends, quantify performance, and generate actionable insights
Build, maintain, and improve software tools for data analysis, modelling, and simulation
Apply software engineering best practices including version control, testing, code reviews, and documentation
Collaborate with multidisciplinary engineering teams to define requirements and solve complex technical challenges
Create clear visualisations, technical reports, and documentation to communicate findings and recommendations
Identify opportunities to improve analytical workflows, modelling approaches, and data-driven processes across the business


What We're Looking For


Bachelor's degree in Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline (or equivalent practical experience)
Experience applying statistical analysis and predictive modelling techniques to solve complex technical or business problems
Proven ability to independently deliver data science projects or workstreams
Experience developing software or analytical tools that support data-driven decision-making
Strong experience working with large-scale datasets and data management infrastructures
Strong proficiency in Python and scientific computing libraries including NumPy, SciPy, Pandas, and data visualisation tools
Solid understanding of numerical methods, probability, and statistics
Familiarity with machine learning techniques and large-scale data analysis
Understanding of software engineering best practices including Git, code review, automated testing, and CI/CD
Experience working with databases and object/blob storage technologies
Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical disciplines
Understanding of data governance principles and best practices


The 'Nice-to-Haves'


Postgraduate qualification in a relevant scientific, engineering, mathematical, or computing discipline
Experience with scientific or systems modelling, including engineering, thermal, electromagnetic, or materials domains
Experience with machine learning or deep learning frameworks
Experience working within Agile software development environments
Experience implementing automated testing frameworks or test-driven development practices
Experience mentoring colleagues and engaging with a broad range of technical stakeholders
Exposure to quantum technologies, radio-frequency electronics, thermodynamics, or systems engineering environments
Ability to communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences


Why Join OQC

You will join a world-class team at the forefront of the next computational era. We offer a culture of bold innovation, the chance to work with unique lab infrastructure, and the opportunity to see your work redefine the limits of computation.

Learn more about our benefits and positive work culture here: https://oqc.tech/company/careers-at-oqc/

Title: Performance and Data Lead (YMCA DownsLink Group)
Description:
JOB DESCRIPTION

Salary: £40,000 per annum.
Contract type: Permanent.
Working pattern: Full-time.

Closing date: Sunday 19 July, 2026 at 12:00 a.m.
Interview date: TBC.

How to apply:

To apply for this role, please visit our website: Performance and Data Lead | YMCA DownsLink Group .

Who to contact if you have a query:

If you would like any further information or an informal discussion about this post, please contact:  Peter.Foster@ymcadlg.org . 

Should you require any assistance with our application process, please email us at:  Recruitment@ymcadlg.org .

YMCA DownsLink Group is the leading charity for children and young people across Sussex and Surrey. We offer safe homes, mental health support and trusted advice.

We believe that every child and young person has the right to be safe, heard and to shape their own future. We work alongside them to make that happen.

We are here for children and young people, many of whom face multiple challenges and need our support.

Our Values - we do what’s right, we work with heart, and we build real connections – guide us in all our actions.

Working within the Impact and Improvement Team, you will be responsible for championing data- driven decision making. You will analyse and report on risk and performance across large and complex datasets, translating this into actionable insights to inform strategic decision making.

In delivering the role, you will develop the organisation's data strategy and oversee the creation of engaging dashboards and efficient data models in Microsoft Power BI, Pyramid, In-Form/Salesforce, MS Business Central, HealthBoxHR and other analytics platforms.

With excellent people skills, you will act as a mentor in the development of the Data C Systems Support Officer and will help to improve data literacy skills across the organisation as a whole.

Key responsibilities:


Use data visualisation tools to regularly present and communicate insights and complex analysis to a range of audiences in a clear, concise, and compelling way.

Lead on small analysis projects and support on larger analysis projects throughout the full project lifecycle.

Work with the Head of Impact and Improvement, identify and proactively exploit opportunities to streamline or automate data collection processes for greater efficiency.

Lead on data migration and system deployment for the upgrades in 2026 for our key system In- Form, contribute to defining and setting up of new reporting, dashboards, and process mapping where needed.

Reporting to the Digital Manager and working closely with business system owners, the Head of Impact and Improvement and IT Managed Service Provider, establishing high quality systems and tools for capturing, organising and using data.

Act the lead for our build on the Salesforce platform, dashboards and reports. Working with service managers, support the design, production, and continuous improvement of this client management system.

Lead the design and delivery of a regular programme of induction and training sessions to build organisational knowledge and capabilities around our data systems.

If you’re enthusiastic about this opportunity but your experience doesn’t align perfectly with every requirement, we encourage you to apply anyway and demonstrate how your experience is transferrable. You may be just the right candidate.

You will already have experience of complex data analysis and reporting on performance across large datasets, along with experience of building engaging dashboards and efficient data models in Microsoft Power BI, Salesforce, or similar analytics platforms. You have analytical and problem- solving skills to interpret and present complex data, along with expert digital skills around use of MS Office 365 and advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel to summarise and graphically represent data. You will understand data migration across systems and how to map effective reporting requirements. Experience with MS Fabric or similar data lakes/warehouses would be very beneficial.

You are a team player who can work with others to deliver a service and challenge colleagues appropriately where required. You have strong planning and time management skills, with ability to deal with competing priorities and deliver results to tight deadlines. You will already have a good understanding of the importance of data and information security, with experience of managing and working with information, systems and databases, designing and producing reports from them.

We are unable to provide work permits or visa sponsorship for this role, so applicants must already have the right to live and work in the UK independently.

We are committed to policies and practices of equity, diversity, and inclusion and to supporting our people to make sure our culture is consistent with this commitment.

If you require assistance or have questions regarding the application process, please do contact us.

YMCA DLG requires all staff and volunteers to be committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and young people, and to respond proactively to safeguarding concerns.

Successful applicants will undergo a thorough background screening process, conducted by an accredited third-party provider. This includes an Enhanced DBS check (with Children’s and Adults’ Barred Lists) as well as comprehensive reference and activity check.

ABOUT US

Additional Information
Your starting salary will be pro rata if the above position is part-time or term-time only.

Title: Administrative Specialist
Description:
Service Operations Administrator




ICT Services | Service Desk | Hybrid Working (3 days in office and 2 from home)




Are you an Excel expert who loves turning data into meaningful insights? Do you enjoy spotting trends, identifying anomalies and using reporting tools such as Power BI to drive better business decisions?




We're looking for a Service Operations Administrator to join our Service Desk team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with strong Excel and reporting skills who enjoys working with data, producing insightful analytics and identifying opportunities to improve operational efficiency.

In this role, you'll be responsible for managing warranty claims, analysing service costs, developing reporting and dashboards, and providing valuable business insight through accurate data analysis and continuous improvement.




What you'll be doing

Managing the end-to-end LLU and Parts Warranty process for Annodata and Kyocera/MCL, including associated reporting
Producing, maintaining and developing service cost analytics, dashboards and management information
Using Excel, Power BI and other reporting tools to provide meaningful insight that supports business decision-making
Proactively identifying anomalies within Vale and other ITSM platforms, working with colleagues across the business to investigate and resolve issues
Supporting the administration and maintenance of tools, knowledge bases and customer documentation used for service cost analysis
Working closely with the Finance team to meet reporting deadlines and ensure financial clarity
Liaising with Field Engineers, Team Leaders and Regional Service Managers to support warranty information retrieval
Highlighting trends, errors and opportunities for improvement across service operations
Supporting warranty claims management and driving continuous improvement initiatives
Undertaking additional duties as required to support the wider Service Desk function




What we're looking for

Advanced Excel skills, including experience working with large datasets, analysis and reporting
Experience producing dashboards and management information
Familiarity with Power BI or similar analytical and visualisation tools
Good knowledge of Vale would be advantageous
Excellent attention to detail and analytical skills
Strong written and verbal communication skills
The ability to prioritise workloads and work effectively under pressure

Title: DEMAND ANALYST MELONS UK
Description:
Who we are:

We are Fyffes, one of the world's leading tropical produce companies. For more than 130 years, we have sourced, shipped and delivered high-quality fresh produce globally. With over 4,000 employees and a strong international network of growers and suppliers, we are committed to shaping wellbeing for the world through sustainable growth, innovation and strong partnerships.

Now we're looking for a Demand Analyst Melons UK to join our team, turning UK melon demand into accurate forecasts, insight and planning support.

Here's what you will do:

Support the commercial team by analysing UK melon demand and translating orders, forecast trends, promotional activity, seasonality, weather impacts and market dynamics into actionable planning insight. Your work will support service, availability, waste reduction and weekly and seasonal planning.

You will connect demand with sourcing, supply chain and operational teams, providing clear analysis, early risk visibility and performance reporting. The role is analytical and detail-driven, with a strong focus on forecast accuracy, demand visibility and planning discipline.

Some key responsibilities:


Analyse UK melon demand using orders, historical sales, promotions, seasonality, weather impacts and market trends.
Prepare and maintain accurate weekly and seasonal melon forecasts with clearly documented assumptions.
Track forecast versus actual demand and order movements, identifying variances, trends and root causes.
Produce demand, forecast, order and service reporting covering accuracy, bias, fill rate, availability, waste and key service drivers.
Support demand reviews, updates and planning inputs, providing clear insight for internal alignment.
Identify supply-demand gaps, shortfalls, oversupply, timing risks and demand changes early.
Support scenario analysis, mitigation planning, allocation discussions and improvements in reporting, communication and forecasting processes.


What you need to bring:


Degree in Supply Chain, Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Agriculture or a related field.
1-3 years of experience in demand analysis, demand planning, supply chain, commercial analysis, fresh produce or FMCG.
Experience with retail demand, forecasts, orders, promotions or seasonal planning is advantageous.
Fresh produce, short shelf-life supply chain or melon experience is beneficial.
Strong Excel and data analysis capability, with ability to translate large datasets into clear insight.
Fluent English; Spanish or another language is advantageous.
Strong attention to detail, proactive communication, structured problem-solving, ownership and ability to work in a fast-paced perishable product environment.


What it is like to work at Fyffes:

We are ambitious but grounded, combining high performance with a people-first culture. Our leadership principles guide us every day: we play to win as one Fyffes, shape the future, cultivate a growth mindset, are transparent and inclusive, and are moved by doing good. We thrive on diversity and treat all candidates equally.

Interested? We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Established in 2004, OLIVER is the world’s first and only specialist in designing, building, and running bespoke in-house agencies and marketing ecosystems for brands. We partner with over 300 clients in 40+ countries and counting. Our unique model drives creativity and efficiency, allowing us to deliver tailored solutions that resonate deeply with audiences. 

As a part of The Brandtech Group, we're at the forefront of leveraging cutting-edge AI technology to revolutionise how we create and deliver work. Our AI solutions enhance efficiency, spark creativity, and drive insightful decision-making, empowering our teams to produce innovative and impactful results. 

Data Analyst

Location: Weybridge, Surrey, United Kingdom

 

Established in 2004, Inside Ideas Group is a rapidly growing global agency network that helps clients drive their businesses forward from the inside. We partner with over 300 clients in more than 40 countries, delivering ideas, technology and creativity that make a measurable impact. 

As part of The Brandtech Group, we are pioneers in using advanced AI technologies to transform the way we create, optimise and deliver work. Our AI capabilities enhance efficiency, support smart decision making and unlock new levels of creativity for our teams and clients. 

We are now looking for a skilled Data Analyst to join our onsite team supporting in London. 

About the Role 

As the Data Analyst, you will be responsible for delivering insight that drives improvements across the full commercial ecosystem spanning customer experience on, retailer investment effectiveness, marketing and sales performance, and the broader post-purchase, onboarding and promotional landscape. 

A core part of your remit will be the design, build and ongoing management of a marketing and sales performance dashboard, underpinned by Google BigQuery and delivered in Looker Studio. This will serve as a critical decision-making tool for marketing, e-commerce, retail and CX teams surfacing insight such as campaign uplift, halo effects, retailer investment impact and share performance across products and markets. 

Alongside this, you will analyse customer journeys, benchmark competitors, oversee content performance and support the planning and execution of onboarding and e-commerce promotions using platforms like GWI to create digital personas and audience profiles. 

You will collaborate closely with CX, marketing, product and e-commerce teams as well as HQ and local market stakeholders to ensure insight informs decisions at every stage. 

This role requires strong analytical capability, expertise in data modelling and SQL, and the ability to translate complex commercial questions into dashboard logic and actionable recommendations. 

What You Will Be Doing 

Marketing & Sales Performance Analytics  

Design, build and maintain a marketing and sales performance dashboard in Looker Studio, underpinned by BigQuery, combining sell-out sales data, market volume and share data, and campaign activity logs. 
Define the underlying data model, transform source files and design the reporting layer to support consistent, scalable KPI delivery. 
Identify and quantify the impact of retailer investment, including uplift in sales and market share during campaign periods. 
Analyse correlations between campaign activity and commercial performance to determine which marketing levers are most effective. 
Measure and report on halo effects across products and retailers resulting from promotional and campaign activity. 
Define and calculate key performance metrics including uplift, baseline, market share, activity intensity and halo effects. 
Work with GFK or other syndicated market data sources to enrich performance analysis with market volume and share context. 

Customer Experience & Digital Analytics 

Conduct analysis of the current customer experience, including user behaviour, content and campaign performance and conversion pathways. 
Benchmark key customer journeys against competitor websites to identify gaps and opportunities. 
Provide regular insight into paid promotions, post-purchase experience and customer satisfaction trends. 
Support planning, optimisation and operational management of post-purchase and onboarding experience. 
Analyse content utilisation, engagement and drop-off points, and recommend enhancements. 
Collaborate with HQ and local teams on onboarding content production and implementation. 
Support feature management including evaluation, testing performance and monitoring adoption, with ongoing analysis of content effectiveness and customer experience updates. 
Reporting, Dashboards & Stakeholder Insight 
Build and maintain dashboards, reports and ad hoc analyses across customer experience, onboarding, e-commerce, retail performance and promotion activity. 
Translate data into clear insights that guide decision-making within marketing, CX, digital and commercial teams. 
Present findings clearly to stakeholders at all levels, ensuring insight is embedded in planning and operational processes. 
Use platforms like GWI to build digital personas and audience profiles that support broader marketing planning. 

What You Need to Be Great in This Role 

Essential 

Proven experience as a data or insight analyst within digital, e-commerce, retail, marketing or agency environments. 
Strong proficiency in Google BigQuery and advanced SQL, including data modelling and reporting layer design. 
Hands-on experience building dashboards in Looker Studio, with a clear understanding of how to structure underlying data models for robust, scalable dashboard performance. 
Demonstrable experience combining sell-out sales, market share and campaign activity datasets to generate commercial insight. 
Ability to define and calculate metrics including uplift, baseline, market share, activity intensity and halo effects. 
Strong marketing analytics capability, including campaign effectiveness measurement across paid and promotional channels. 
Good understanding of digital customer journeys, content performance metrics and e-commerce KPIs. 
Experience with analytics platforms such as Google Analytics GA4, Adobe Analytics or similar. 
Excellent communication and storytelling ability — capable of translating complex business questions into dashboard logic and presenting findings clearly to non-technical audiences. 
Experience working with multi-market teams and managing stakeholder expectations across business functions. 
A passion for data-driven decision-making and an interest in how AI can enhance analytics workflows. 

Preferred: 

Proficiency in Python (pandas) for data transformation, automation and workflow scripting. 
Broader experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP), including automating data ingestion and transformation workflows. 
Experience working in retail, e-commerce or retailer media environments. 
Familiarity with GfK or other syndicated market data sources. 
Experience with additional BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI. 

Our Values 

Be Ambitious to succeed Be Imaginative to push the boundaries of what's possible Be Inspirational to do groundbreaking work Be always learning and listening to understand Be Results-focused to exceed expectations Be actively pro-inclusive and anti-racist across our community, clients and creations 

INSIDE IDEAS GROUP, a part of the Brandtech Group, is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive working environment where all employees are encouraged to reach their full potential, and individual differences are valued and respected. All applicants shall be considered for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, neurodivergence, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by local laws. 

INSIDE IDEAS GROUP has set ambitious environmental goals around sustainability, with science-based emissions reduction targets. Collectively, we work towards our mission, embedding sustainability into every department and through every stage of the project lifecycle.

Title: Performance Specialist
Description:
Closing Date

2026-07-06

Job Title: Performance Analyst

Location: Durrington (Hybrid)

Contract Type: 12 month FTC

Hours: 37

Salary: £34,000 to £38,000 depending on skills and experience

About The Role:

There has never been a more important time to join Southern Water. As we move through AMP8, we are delivering one of the most ambitious capital investment programmes in the industry, focused on improving service resilience, environmental performance and long‑term affordability for customers.

The Customer Strategy, Insight and Experience team is responsible for reporting and investigation of issues which improve process for the customer and the company. We provide key facts and insight to empower the wider team to make the right decisions for the right reasons.

About The Role

This Performance Analyst role (internally will be known as Performance Specialist) is responsible for the provision of performance and management information for and about internal and external stakeholders, related to the Southern Water Wholesale Services Division. General duties include investigating, building, processing, reviewing, analysing and reporting of performance and related issues, as well as supporting on regulatory and process change. Working closely with other roles across the department this role is busy, interesting and diverse.

What You Will Be Responsible For:


Deliver timely, fit for purpose, reporting within agreed SLAs. Continuously improve the efficiency of reports to add value and allow for the focus to be on analysing the data rather than processing it.
Produce performance reporting from numerous data sources regarding business level performance vs targets and process regulatory performance. This will require you to develop an in-depth knowledge of processes and systems to ensure accuracy across many customer focussed roles.
Transform data into clear visualisation to meet the stakeholders needs and allow them to see the complete picture.
Support the wider team by providing the information required to make informed decisions on a wide range of topics (including provision of data to external stakeholders such as MOSL, WaterUK and Ofwat).


What You’ll Bring To The Role:


Good understanding and working experience of compiling and generating performance reports and management information.
Strong data handling, technical skills and experience with information validation using Excel and other analytical tools.
Data manipulation skills and experience.
Experience working with reporting and analysis packages and extracting data from corporate systems (e.g. Service Now or other CRM’s, Qlikview, Alteryx, Power BI or similar).
Ability and confidence to communicate appropriately with a range of audiences including peers, senior colleagues and specialists at all levels of the organisation


Southern Water is at the forefront of transforming Britain’s water industry, investing significantly to enhance resilience, sustainability, and service excellence. With £7.8bn planned investment for 2025-30, this is an unparalleled opportunity to join a business committed to delivering a generational shift in the way water services are managed.

You will be joining at a time of significant change, working alongside a highly skilled leadership team with a clear vision for the future. We offer an environment where senior professionals can make a meaningful impact, influence major strategic decisions, and drive long-term value creation.

At Southern Water, we believe diverse perspectives drive innovation. If you’re passionate about making a positive impact and think you can bring value to our team, we’d love to hear from you—even if you don’t tick every box. Your unique skills and experiences could be exactly what we need.

If this role isn’t quite what you’re looking for but are keen to be contacted about opportunities at Southern Water, you can register your details here: Introduce Yourself (myworkdaysite.com)

Our Commitment to Diversity

We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, religion, or any other protected characteristic. If you need reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Additional Information:

In line with Southern Water’s security requirements, successful candidates will be required to provide evidence of their identity, eligibility to work in the UK, criminal record check (DBS) and verification of their employment and/or education history for the past three years.

Appointment to this role is subject to the successful completion of all preemployment checks, including security vetting.

Please note that if a candidate does not meet the required security standards or fails to pass the vetting process, Southern Water reserves the right to withdraw the offer of employment. Some positions may also require higher levels of security vetting, which may involve providing additional documentation.

  • Experience working with reporting and analysis packages and extracting data from corporate systems (e.g. Service Now or other CRM’s, Qlikview, Alteryx, Power BI or similar).
  • Ability and confidence to communicate appropriately with a range of audiences including peers, senior colleagues and specialists at all levels of the organisation

Southern Water is at the forefront of transforming Britain’s water industry, investing significantly to enhance resilience, sustainability, and service excellence. With £7.8bn planned investment for 2025-30, this is an unparalleled opportunity to join a business committed to delivering a generational shift in the way water services are managed.

You will be joining at a time of significant change, working alongside a highly skilled leadership team with a clear vision for the future. We offer an environment where senior professionals can make a meaningful impact, influence major strategic decisions, and drive long-term value creation.

At Southern Water, we believe diverse perspectives drive innovation. If you’re passionate about making a positive impact and think you can bring value to our team, we’d love to hear from you—even if you don’t tick every box. Your unique skills and experiences could be exactly what we need.

If this role isn’t quite what you’re looking for but are keen to be contacted about opportunities at Southern Water, you can register your details here: Introduce Yourself (myworkdaysite.com)

Our Commitment to Diversity

We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, religion, or any other protected characteristic. If you need reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Additional Information:

In line with Southern Water’s security requirements, successful candidates will be required to provide evidence of their identity, eligibility to work in the UK, criminal record check (DBS) and verification of their employment and/or education history for the past three years.

Appointment to this role is subject to the successful completion of all preemployment checks, including security vetting.

Please note that if a candidate does not meet the required security standards or fails to pass the vetting process, Southern Water reserves the right to withdraw the offer of employment. Some positions may also require higher levels of security vetting, which may involve providing additional documentation.

Title: Data Operations Analyst
Description:
Application Deadline: 21 August 2026

Department: Engineering

Location: London



Description

About UsWe are a fast-growing Series A SaaS startup at the forefront of the next big shift in marketing, transforming the way brands connect with audiences in virtual worlds. This is a shift on par with the rise of social media, and we are building the analytics engine to power it. You will be joining a tight-knit, high-impact team of 40 people, including a product team of 3 and an engineering team of 10, meaning your work will directly shape the trajectory of our platform and company.

About the RoleOur platform runs on data, and this role owns the work that keeps it flowing: pulling, validating, importing and quality-checking so the wider team can move quickly and with confidence. Sitting within the product team, you will own the operational data layer: the day-to-day work of getting accurate, trustworthy data into the hands of product, client services and the wider business, fast.

This is a hands-on product & operations role, not an engineering or a pure-analysis one. You are the dependable go-to who unblocks the team's data needs and keeps everything flowing and accurate. You write SQL confidently, lean heavily on AI tools to move quickly, and you are comfortable reading code and data models, but you take your pride from being the person who keeps data reliable and accessible, not from building pipelines or chasing the next engineering project.





Key Responsibilities


Own data access across the product lifecycle: Be the team's first port of call for data requests, pull, shape and deliver data in the format product, CS and sales need.
Validate and quality-check: Sanity-check, validate and QC data across the platform so the team can trust every number. Spot anomalies, chase them down, and keep our data honest.
Manage imports and ingestion ops: Run routine data imports and ingestion tasks, making sure data lands cleanly, completely and on time.
Work AI-first: Use AI tools (Claude, Cursor, Copilot and similar) to rapidly translate business logic or SQL into complex database queries (including NoSQL / Elastic / Mongo), accelerating repetitive work, and continually improve how the team gets and checks data.
Document and share knowledge: Document data sources, queries and processes so knowledge never sits with just one person. You make the team less dependent on any single individual, including yourself.
Own the long tail: Take ownership of the steady stream of ad hoc requests that keeps the wider team moving.
Partner cross-functionally: Work closely with data engineers and product team, translating between technical data and real business needs.


Skills, Knowledge and Expertise


An operations and service mindset: You take genuine pride in being the reliable go-to who keeps data flowing and accurate. You enjoy solving a team's everyday data needs and you are not looking to use this role as a stepping stone into a pure engineering job.
Data Fluency (SQL & NoSQL): You write and debug SQL confidently, but you are also comfortable navigating non-relational/document databases (like MongoDB and Elasticsearch). You don't need to have raw NoSQL syntax memorized, but you should know how to read nested JSON structures.
AI-first working: You already lean heavily on AI tools to work faster and better, and you are always finding new ways to use them.
Technical literacy: You can read code and data models (including how relational data maps to NoSQL/JSON structures), understand how different systems fit together, and pick up light Python.
Rigour and attention to detail: You are meticulous about data accuracy and quality, and you notice when a number looks off.
Bias for action: You are comfortable in the ambiguity of a Series A startup and happy to roll up your sleeves and get things done.
Clear communicator: You can translate between technical data and business stakeholders without friction.
AI-first working: You already lean heavily on AI tools to work faster and better. Crucially, you possess the critical thinking to audit and validate AI-generated outputs, ensuring code/queries are safe and optimised before running them.
Experience: Around 2–4 years in a data operations, data analyst, BI, revenue/business operations or similar hands-on data role.

Bonus Points
Experience in a data operations, analytics operations or revenue operations function.
Familiarity with the modern data stack and BI tooling.
Background in marketing-related SaaS, virtual environments or gaming.
Familiarity with tools such as Linear and Notion.


Why join us?


Join a business at the forefront of the next big shift in marketing.
Be part of a fast-growing startup with a collaborative, innovative and supportive team.
Be genuinely indispensable, this role unlocks something the whole company depends on, so your impact is visible from day one.
A real, non-engineering growth path: grow into owning our data-quality function, take on ROI and attribution research as the team scales.
25 days holiday as standard, plus a bonus GEEIQ Day to use whenever you choose.
We offer Heka, a monthly wellness allowance you can spend across a wide range of fitness and wellbeing providers, plus a Cycle to Work scheme.
We have a thriving company culture with regular socials, team offsites, and events - quizzes, sports days, Hackathons, Bake Offs, and more. Our eNPS is 52, nearly double the industry average, and it shows, the team genuinely loves working here and learning from each other.
You pick your start time, we just ask that everyone's available during core hours of 10am–5pm. That might mean 8am–5pm, 9am–6pm, or 10am–7pm, whatever works best for you.

Title: Workforce Management (WFM) Planner
Description:
Job Description

Workforce Management (WFM) Planner

Brighton

Full time - Hybrid 35 hours per week

Salary starting from £35,200 Per annum

We make health happen

The Planning department is an integral part of Operations across our Global portfolio, supporting the function to maintain high quality KPIs and performance standards within budgetary limits.

 

The WFM Planner will be responsible for analysing resource requirements and designing effective scheduling solutions that align with operational goals. You’ll work closely with stakeholders to ensure that plans are not only data-driven but also practical and responsive to the evolving needs of our teams. The holder will monitor performance, carry out analysis of historic data, calculate effects of strategic or procedural changes, forecast work volumes, recommend staffing needs, carry out administrative scheduling activity, identify opportunities to maximise use of resource, provide justification for budget alterations and supply operational performance data.

How You’ll Help Us Make Health Happen


Analyse historical and forecasted data to create and publish short- and long-term resource plans.
Develop and deliver scheduling strategies that support operational performance and service level targets.
Collaborate with stakeholders across departments to ensure plans meet business needs and reflect how teams work in practice.
Monitor ongoing and operational performance and variance and adjust intra-day schedules to optimise resource utilisation.
Track and report on key metrics including call volumes, handle times, queue times, agent availability, and service level adherence.
Chair regular meetings to review effectiveness of current plan and departmental performance to drive ongoing improved performance and collaboration.
Identify and communicate the impact of external factors on workload volumes.
Escalate system issues and coordinate responses across relevant platforms.
Perform trend analysis to guide strategic decisions and continuous improvement.
Provide clear, actionable insights and updates to operational teams.
Act as a key point of contact for queries related to daily planning and scheduling.
Compare forecasted vs. actual data daily to identify discrepancies.
Communicate actions and updates to affected business areas.


Key Skills/Qualifications Needed For This Role


Extensive familiarity with software applications including: MS Excel
Workforce Management systems (experience with TotalView / NICE IEX preferrable)
Telephony Platforms (experience with Genesys preferrable)
Email Management Platforms (experience with eGain preferrable)
Messaging and Live Chat Platforms (experience with LivePerson preferrable)
Other Workforce Management and telephony systems
Proven experience in resource planning, MI analysis, or workforce management within a customer service or operations environment.
Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to influence and collaborate across teams.
Analytical mindset with a proactive approach to problem-solving and continuous improvement.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable plans.


Benefits


25 days holiday, increasing through length of service, with option to buy or sell
Bupa health insurance as a benefit in kind
An enhanced pension plan and life insurance
Onsite gyms or local discounts where no onsite gym available
Various other benefits and online discounts


Why Bupa

Bupa Global is the international health insurance division of Bupa. We provide customers who want premium international coverage with products and services to access the healthcare they need anytime, around the world, whether at home or when studying, living, travelling or working abroad.

Bupa Global has offices around the world including London and Brighton (UK), Dublin (Europe), Miami (USA), Dubai (UAE, in partnership with OIC), Egypt and Hong Kong (China) as well as regional offices in mainland China, Singapore, the Dominican Republic, Bolivia, Panama, Guatemala and Ecuador.

Time Type

Full time

Job Area

Administration

Locations:

Victory House, Brighton

Title: Data Scientist - Customer Focus
Description:
Your role

As a Data Scientist - Customer Focus, your mission is to turn complex data into practical insights and predictive solutions that help improve equipment performance, reduce downtime, and support better operational decision-making. In this role, you will work closely with customers, account teams, and technical specialists, giving you the chance to see the direct impact of your work in real operational environments while building experience at the intersection of technology, customer collaboration and business value.

This is an exciting opportunity to work at the forefront of data science and industrial innovation, applying advanced analytics to solve real customer challenges on a global scale.

You will


Explore, prepare, and deliver data analyses, predictive models, and reporting that create measurable value for global customers.
Work closely with customers and account teams to rapidly iterate on solutions using real data, feedback, and operational constraints.
Support the integration of analytics outputs into customer workflows, helping ensure solutions are practical, reliable, and adopted by users.
Investigate deployment or operational issues and translate feedback into technical improvements.
Present insights and recommendations clearly through reports, dashboards, workshops, and customer presentations.
Support pre-sales and delivery activity through demos, business reviews, and occasional on-site customer engagement.
Collaborate with Data Engineering, DevOps, and R&D teams to deploy, validate, and scale analytics solutions.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of models, data workflows, and delivery processes.


To succeed, you will need

We encourage you to apply even if you don't meet every single requirement. We value diverse experiences and perspectives and are excited to see what you bring to the role.

You have 2+ years of experience, working as a Data Scientist, Analytics Specialist, or in a similar role.


A degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, or a related discipline.
Strong practical Python skills and experience with common data science libraries such as pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels, or scikit-learn.
The ability to turn data into clear, actionable insights for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Experience working with predictive modelling, ideally with time-series, sensor, manufacturing, or operational data.
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as Azure and an understanding of APIs or production environments.
Confidence working in customer-facing or consulting-style environments, with strong communication and collaboration skills.


In return, we offer


Culture of trust and accountability
Lifelong learning and career growth
Innovation powered by people
Comprehensive compensation and benefits
Health and well-being


Job location

This role offers a hybrid or remote working arrangement, there may be some travel required as part of the role.

Contact information

Talent Acquisition Team: Brett Hornby

About Edwards

Edwards is a leading developer and manufacturer of sophisticated vacuum products, exhaust management systems and related value-added services. Edwards solutions are integral to manufacturing processes for semiconductors, flat panel displays, LEDs and solar cells. They are also used within an increasingly diverse range of industrial processes including power, glass and other coating applications; steel and other metallurgy; pharmaceutical and chemical; and for scientific instruments in a wide range of R&D applications.

Edwards has over 4,000 employees worldwide engaged in the design, manufacture and support of high technology vacuum and exhaust management equipment. Edwards has state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Europe, Asia and North America.

Title: Business Insights Manager
Description:
Business Insights Manager- Reading (Hybrid)- Permanent- £45,000-£55,000 + Benefits




About the Role

IN2 Consult is partnering with a growing business in Reading to recruit an experienced Business Insights Manager to join their growing team and play a pivotal role in transforming data into commercial value.




This is an exciting opportunity for someone who thrives on solving business

problems through analytics, influencing decision-making, and helping shape strategic direction.

Working closely with senior stakeholders and operational teams, you'll combine technical expertise with commercial understanding to deliver meaningful insights that improve performance, identify opportunities, and support informed business decisions.




If you're passionate about using data to drive change and enjoy working in a collaborative, fast-paced environment, we'd love to hear from you.




Key Responsibilities

Partner with the Business

Build strong relationships with internal leaders and client stakeholders to understand business priorities and translate them into analytical solutions.
Act as a trusted advisor, using data to support strategic planning and operational decision-making.

Deliver Insight That Drives Performance

Analyse multiple data sources to identify trends, risks and opportunities.
Present clear, commercially focused recommendations that influence business outcomes and improve overall performance.
Monitor key performance indicators and proactively identify areas for optimisation.

Lead Data & Reporting Solutions

Design, develop and maintain interactive dashboards and reporting tools that provide clear visibility of business performance.
Ensure reporting is accurate, relevant and accessible to a range of stakeholders.

Improve Data Capability

Champion best practice in data management, governance and quality.
Support the evolution of data processes, models and reporting frameworks to improve efficiency and scalability.
Work with technical teams to optimise data structures and automate reporting where possible.

Drive Continuous Improvement

Lead analytics projects from concept through to implementation.
Identify new ways to leverage data, improve processes and enhance business performance.
Stay informed of emerging technologies and analytical techniques that can add value to the business.




What You'll Bring

Commercial Mindset

Experience using data to solve business challenges and influence strategic decisions.
Ability to balance analytical thinking with commercial awareness.

Stakeholder Management

Proven experience engaging and influencing stakeholders at all levels.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex analysis in a simple, engaging way.

Technical Expertise

Strong SQL skills with experience querying and managing large datasets.
Proficiency in Power BI, Tableau or similar visualisation platforms.
Experience using Python or R for data analysis and statistical modelling.
Knowledge of cloud-based data platforms and modern data architecture, including Azure, is highly desirable.

Title: Data and Reporting Analyst
Description:
Full Time, Permanent

Package: £29,000 - £32,000 per annum + annual company bonus

Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 35 hours per week

Location: Winnersh, (RG41 5TP) - office based contract with flexibility




Are you a Power BI enthusiast who loves turning raw retail data into actionable commercial strategies?




At Smollan, we are the driving force behind some of the world’s biggest brands. We’re seeking a sharp, analytical Data & Reporting Analyst, expert with Power BI to join our Data & Insights team.

In this role, you won't just be managing and storing sales data, you will be the bridge between complex data warehouses and real-world retail strategy. If you love transforming dense datasets into slick, automated Power BI dashboards that help management teams make brilliant commercial decisions, we want to hear from you.




What You'll Be Doing




Power BI & Visualisation: Design, build, and maintain engaging dashboards, graphs, and visualisations that turn complex numbers into clear, compelling stories.
Process Automation: Identify opportunities to increase efficiency, setting up and managing automated data processes to eliminate manual repetition.
Data Mining & Analysis: Mine and analyse large datasets (including sales and field marketing metrics), drawing valid, actionable inferences for our management teams.
Survey Insights: Design and analyse field surveys, presenting key findings and performance indicators (KPIs) with clarity and impact.
Data Governance: Support and develop our reporting processes, maintaining robust records management policies to ensure data integrity across our databases.




What We’re Looking For

Title: Insight Analyst
Description:
Location: Milton Keynes – based in office 1-2 days a week (hybrid)

Hours: Full time

Reporting to: Head of People Science

Salary: £33K-£38K

About WorkBuzz

WorkBuzz was founded in 2018, has raised £12.5m funding and has 400 clients, including iconic brands like Five Guys, Butlins and Virgin. Our mission is to lead a new era of employee listening: AI-first, beyond surveys and turning employee voice into competitive advantage.

Our AI-powered platform makes it easy for mid and large-sized companies to gather real-time employee feedback and uncover what truly matters. Our new AI-native product Dialogue launches us into operational listening, putting employee listening in the hands of operational leaders – where decisions are made and change actually happens.

Trusted across industries, we combine smart technology with a human approach - helping leaders make better decisions, faster. We’re growing fast and looking for mission-driven people to join us!

The Role

We're looking for a tech-savvy, AI-forward Analyst to join our People Science team, turning WorkBuzz employee listening data into clear, accurate and useful insight for clients.

This role is for someone who actively seeks smarter ways to work, freeing up time for the deeper analysis, thinking and storytelling that truly adds value. Working closely with our consultants and guided by the Lead Analyst, the role builds expertise in the WorkBuzz platform, employee listening data and analytical methods, developing towards greater ownership of client and broader business insight work over time.

Key Responsibilities

Turn data into insight at pace


Produce accurate analysis, charts, tables and slides from employee survey results for use in client reports and presentations
Ensure standard outputs including demographic cuts, trend comparisons and summary tables, following established templates and methods, and using automation to increase speed and consistency where possible
Support more advanced analysis, such as driver analysis, segmentation analysis and performance linkage with guidance from the Lead Analyst
Ensure appropriate checks to ensure accuracy of all outputs before they're shared


Support great client delivery


Work with People Science Consultants to understand specific client needs, outputs required and delivery timelines
Ensure report decks are populated with well-formatted, accurate data summaries, flagging interesting patterns or anomalies along the way
Respond to ad-hoc analysis requests quickly and reliably, ensuring consultants are equipped to do their best work
Use and help maintain reusable presentation templates, chart formats and analysis approaches to support consistency across client deliverables.


Support improvements in outcomes and efficiencies


Actively use and champion AI tools to accelerate analysis, charting, summarisation and quality checking
Identify repetitive or manual tasks in the reporting workflow in the development of smarter, automated approaches
Help establish and document AI-assisted methods that the wider team can adopt and build on
Follow, and where possible improve, documented methods, quality checks and analytical assumptions to ensure outputs are accurate and consistent with WorkBuzz standards
Contribute to reusable templates, automated workflows and shared knowledge that make the whole team more effective


Platform knowledge


Build a strong working knowledge of the WorkBuzz platform, including survey setup, data mapping, reporting structures and dashboards
Share feedback, recurring issues and improvement ideas from day-to-day analysis work to help improve reporting and workflows.
Support user testing of new reporting and analysis features when required
Keep up to date with platform releases and new reporting or analysis capabilities, applying them in client work where appropriate


Requirements For Role


Some experience in an analyst, insights, research or data-focused role, or a strong academic background with hands-on data analysis experience from placements, internships or project work
Degree in a relevant subject such as psychology, organisational psychology, business, economics, statistics, data science or adjacent
Solid quantitative analysis skills, comfortable working accurately with survey results, trends, demographic cuts and comparisons
Enthusiasm for AI tools and automation; you’re keen to experiment and find better ways to work
Hands-on experience with AI tools such as Claude or similar, applied to real analytical or reporting tasks
Strong Excel skills including formulas, pivot tables and charting; strong PowerPoint skills to ensure clear and polished client slides
Genuine attention to detail and a critical eye when reviewing outputs
Clear written communication, ensuring complex findings are summarised simply and accurately
Well organised, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced, client-focused environment
Awareness of data protection, confidentiality and responsible AI use when handling people data


Desirable


Experience working with employee survey, engagement or HR data and an interest in employee engagement, culture and the employee experience agenda
Experience with BI or data visualisation tools
Some experience of producing client-facing reports, charts or presentation materials


We seek people who chime with our values and demonstrate, in their own way, behaviours that bring them to life. We’re not looking for one “type” of person - we value individuality and diversity. What matters most is that candidates demonstrate behaviours that support success in-role and make our culture thrive.   

Aspire Higher - We set BIG goals, embrace growth and challenge limits. Reaching for new possibilities, we are curious and continuously learning, if there’s a better way, we’ll find it.  

Own with Impact- We take ownership and hold ourselves accountable, focused on creating meaningful impact. We lean in and own it with purpose and passion.  

Energised by Challenge- We never stand still! We approach challenges with energy and resilience. We thrive on the tough stuff, embracing change as a constant.  

Trust in Action- We build trust through our expertise and through consistent, high-quality delivery. With transparency, reliability and authenticity at our core, we act with integrity and do what we say, delivering on our promises.  

Win Together- We are fuelled by collaboration. We elevate each other, our clients and our partners showing up in meaningful ways. Sharing in success, we celebrate every victory- big or small.  

Why you’ll love working at WorkBuzz 


Our culture – WorkBuzz is fast-paced, dynamic and rewarding. Everyone has a voice and the smartest idea wins.  
Work at the frontier - Dialogue is genuinely new territory. You'll be shaping how AI transforms employee listening, not maintaining someone else's roadmap.
Clear purpose – be part of building something which matters.  
Be the best you can be – we will invest in your development and help you grow.  
Competitive salary, share options, private health insurance and a range of other benefits  
Flexible/ hybrid working – the opportunity to split your time between home working and our Milton Keynes office.  
Check out our Glassdoor reviews!  


We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating a workplace where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered to thrive. Our team is stronger when it is diverse and inclusive.  

We welcome applicants from all walks of life and our hiring decisions are based on merit - regardless of age, disability, race, religion or belief, sex, gender, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, pregnancy or maternity, marital or civil partnership status.  

If you need any adjustments or support during the recruitment process, please don’t hesitate to let us know. We’re here to ensure a fair and accessible experience, removing barriers that may impede someone’s progress based on their unique circumstances.

Title: Product Management Performance Analyst
Description:
Bring more to life.

Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology?

At Abcam, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact.

You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life.

Progress happens together

At Abcam, we believe the scientific community goes further, faster when we go there together. That’s why we work with life scientists to provide biological reagents and solutions that enable faster breakthroughs in critical areas like cancer, neurological disorders, infectious diseases and metabolic disorders. Our talented team of over 1,400 colleagues worldwide is our greatest asset. By applying our expertise and pushing boundaries, together we strive to better serve our customers, strengthen our impact across our industry, and ultimately our positive impact on society.

Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible.

The Product Management Performance Analyst role is part of the wider Product Management team and focused on assisting colleagues and improving their productivity with the timely supply of product performance and planning data so that they can better serve our customers effectively and efficiently. Our vision is for this role to act as a catalyst for positive change across the Product Management team and the wider business.

Working within the Pricing team team and partnering with Product Management leaders within the business, you will be responsible for improving global performance levels of our team with data analysis tools, KPIs and reports to drive outstanding commercial activities.

This position reports to the Pricing Director and is part of the pricing team who support the Product Management organisation at Abcam. This is an on-site role located in Cambridge and an opportunity to work in a fast paced environment in a growing global business with a data driven mindset.

In This Role, You Will Have The Opportunity To


Maintain and improve Microsoft PowerBi performance reporting suites for the Product Management teams
Support with the identification of relevant KPIs and measures which reflect the performance of the Product Management teams
Use reporting and analysis to identify areas to drive change, course corrections or further iterations in product strategies and provide ad hoc analytics support to the function on data analysis, reporting and performance.
Prepare and present data/insights to senior stakeholders as needed
Maintain and improve our data sources to allow faster, more automated reporting, working with our Data Engineering team who are owners of our main data sources.


The Essential Requirements Of The Job Include


Experience in a data analytical position, delivering data management, data analysis, insight and reporting solutions and an expert in PowerBi and DAX, with knowledge of Tableau or equivalent data visualisation software with a commitment to learn quickly
Performance management experience – ability to propose new KPIs based on gaps in performance, innovative and inquisitive and someone who thrives in a dynamic, ever-changing environment with the ability to multitask, prioritise and meet deadlines.
Possess strong communication and presentation skills - passionate about storytelling via data and a proactive self-starter who can pick things up quickly and run with projects autonomously
Output focused - motivated by achieving goals


It would be a plus if you also possess previous experience in:


SQL coding
Salesforce CRM
Microsoft Excel and Powerpoint


Abcam, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info.

Join our winning team today. Together, we’ll accelerate the real-life impact of tomorrow’s science and technology. We partner with customers across the globe to help them solve their most complex challenges, architecting solutions that bring the power of science to life.

For more information, visit www.danaher.com.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Overall Ambition




Nando’s is on a journey to Create Lasting Happiness, making a difference to the lives of our people, our customers, and the communities we work in. Originally started in Johannesburg, South Africa, we pride ourselves on creating memorable experiences for everyone involved in Nando’s: from our employees, communities, founders, and suppliers, but most importantly, our customers.

Whilst we always look back on our history and story with pride, the story is still evolving. We have recently been growing our digital capabilities to ensure the Nando’s experience remains relevant to modern-day customers.




You will be joining a tech team that has grown rapidly in the past five years and is determined to enable change within the restaurant industry by bringing innovative thinking and the latest technology tools and practices to solve some of its most complex problems.







The Data and Analysis Team

We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join our Data and Analysis team. Our remit is to ensure everyone has access to the right information, at the right time, and that Nando’s gets the best value out of our data to achieve our business goals.




Our current focus is extracting insights from the data and creating a single source of accurate, timely data as we continue to increase our data maturity and advance our predictive analytic capability and explore the role of AI in the business.




You will be part of our work to support our strategic goals and build a data-informed attitude for addressing our opportunities and challenges.







Our Values

At Nando’s, our values guide how we work, make decisions, and show up for each other every day:

Pride – We take pride in everything we do, from the quality of our food to the impact of our work.
Passion – We bring energy, enthusiasm, and a love for what we do.
Courage – We speak up, challenge the status quo, and are not afraid to try new things.
Integrity – We do the right thing, act honestly, and build trust with each other.
Family – We care for and support one another, creating a sense of belonging where everyone can thrive.







What we're looking for:

As a Data Analyst you will work across a wide range of domains from financial and operational data, to kitchen tech and stock, helping our teams make better decisions grounded in sound analysis. As a curious problem-solver, you will be able to move between the technical and the commercial; and be comfortable building data models as well as presenting findings to senior stakeholders.




In this role you’ll work independently across the full data stack, understanding the ‘why’ behind requests before jumping to the ‘what’, and contributing to the translation of business problems into well-defined, actionable work. You’ll be supported by a collaborative team and won’t be expected to deliver everything solo from day one, but you’ll be expected to grow into doing so.

You will…







Write and maintain SQL models (in Dataform) to transform and serve reliable data across our pipelines
Support data engineers in the design, building and testing of data pipelines, able to contribute meaningfully, not just consume the output
Build and iterate on cloud-based reports and dashboards in Looker (or similar BI tool), giving our support teams and restaurants clear, actionable insight into performance
Translate business questions into well-scoped analysis and understand the problem and domain before reaching for the answer
Communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, choosing the right medium for the audience
Peer review colleagues’ work and contribute to data quality standards and documentation
Identify opportunities to automate manual processes and data tasks, and take the initiative to implement them







Skills required

You will have proven experience in the following:

Writing complex SQL to query, transform and model data across our cloud data platform (window functions, CTEs, complex datatypes)
Building and maintaining data models in Dataform, or a similar data transformation tool such as Snowflake or DBT, with an understanding of how they fit into the broader pipeline
Building reports and dashboards in Looker or a similar BI tool, serving reliable, self-serve data to the business
Using Git as standard practice — branching, pull requests and code review
Contributing to the translation of business requests into clearly scoped, actionable work items
Applying data quality techniques — writing tests, identifying issues proactively and communicating data limitations clearly
Documenting data models, definitions and lineage to a production standard
Understanding data governance principles and championing good data management practice within the team
Applying and understanding when to use relevant statistical methods to support analysis
Supporting data engineers in designing, coding and testing data processing pipelines
Using AI-assisted coding tools (e.g. Claude) to improve the speed and quality of your work — critically, not blindly — with an awareness of responsible AI use and data governance implications







You will also display

Clear communication with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, able to explain complex findings in plain language and tell the story behind the data
Genuine intellectual curiosity, actively exploring new tools and methods, including AI, and sharing what you learn with the team
Comfort with ambiguity, able to define the right question and find the “so what” before jumping to answer
A product mindset, understanding the business objective behind every piece of analysis, not just the request on the surface
A proactive approach to improving processes, data quality and ways of working, with the initiative to make it happen, not just flag it
A collaborative team attitude, peer reviewing others’ work, sharing knowledge and contributing to a stronger team




Inclusion

At Nando’s, everyone is welcome. Inspired by our Southern African heritage we know and value the richness that diversity brings to Nando’s. We’re committed to creating an inclusive and respectful culture for all; including our existing and future Nandocas, partners and suppliers, customers and the communities we are a part of. We deliberately work hard to create an environment where every individual is valued, respected and can flourish regardless of who they are, their background or outlook on life.




We welcome applications from a diverse range of individuals and will consider any reasonable adjustments in order to enable candidates to perform as well as possible during the recruitment process.

HTML:
Data Analyst


Overall Ambition


Nando’s is on a journey to Create Lasting Happiness, making a difference to the lives of our people, our customers, and the communities we work in. Originally started in Johannesburg, South Africa, we pride ourselves on creating memorable experiences for everyone involved in Nando’s: from our employees, communities, founders, and suppliers, but most importantly, our customers.

Whilst we always look back on our history and story with pride, the story is still evolving. We have recently been growing our digital capabilities to ensure the Nando’s experience remains relevant to modern-day customers.


You will be joining a tech team that has grown rapidly in the past five years and is determined to enable change within the restaurant industry by bringing innovative thinking and the latest technology tools and practices to solve some of its most complex problems.



The Data and Analysis Team

We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join our Data and Analysis team. Our remit is to ensure everyone has access to the right information, at the right time, and that Nando’s gets the best value out of our data to achieve our business goals.


Our current focus is extracting insights from the data and creating a single source of accurate, timely data as we continue to increase our data maturity and advance our predictive analytic capability and explore the role of AI in the business.


You will be part of our work to support our strategic goals and build a data-informed attitude for addressing our opportunities and challenges.



Our Values

At Nando’s, our values guide how we work, make decisions, and show up for each other every day:

  • Pride – We take pride in everything we do, from the quality of our food to the impact of our work.
  • Passion – We bring energy, enthusiasm, and a love for what we do.
  • Courage – We speak up, challenge the status quo, and are not afraid to try new things.
  • Integrity – We do the right thing, act honestly, and build trust with each other.
  • Family – We care for and support one another, creating a sense of belonging where everyone can thrive.



What we're looking for:

As a Data Analyst you will work across a wide range of domains from financial and operational data, to kitchen tech and stock, helping our teams make better decisions grounded in sound analysis. As a curious problem-solver, you will be able to move between the technical and the commercial; and be comfortable building data models as well as presenting findings to senior stakeholders.


In this role you’ll work independently across the full data stack, understanding the ‘why’ behind requests before jumping to the ‘what’, and contributing to the translation of business problems into well-defined, actionable work. You’ll be supported by a collaborative team and won’t be expected to deliver everything solo from day one, but you’ll be expected to grow into doing so.

You will…



  • Write and maintain SQL models (in Dataform) to transform and serve reliable data across our pipelines
  • Support data engineers in the design, building and testing of data pipelines, able to contribute meaningfully, not just consume the output
  • Build and iterate on cloud-based reports and dashboards in Looker (or similar BI tool), giving our support teams and restaurants clear, actionable insight into performance
  • Translate business questions into well-scoped analysis and understand the problem and domain before reaching for the answer
  • Communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, choosing the right medium for the audience
  • Peer review colleagues’ work and contribute to data quality standards and documentation
  • Identify opportunities to automate manual processes and data tasks, and take the initiative to implement them



Skills required

You will have proven experience in the following:

  • Writing complex SQL to query, transform and model data across our cloud data platform (window functions, CTEs, complex datatypes)
  • Building and maintaining data models in Dataform, or a similar data transformation tool such as Snowflake or DBT, with an understanding of how they fit into the broader pipeline
  • Building reports and dashboards in Looker or a similar BI tool, serving reliable, self-serve data to the business
  • Using Git as standard practice — branching, pull requests and code review
  • Contributing to the translation of business requests into clearly scoped, actionable work items
  • Applying data quality techniques — writing tests, identifying issues proactively and communicating data limitations clearly
  • Documenting data models, definitions and lineage to a production standard
  • Understanding data governance principles and championing good data management practice within the team
  • Applying and understanding when to use relevant statistical methods to support analysis
  • Supporting data engineers in designing, coding and testing data processing pipelines
  • Using AI-assisted coding tools (e.g. Claude) to improve the speed and quality of your work — critically, not blindly — with an awareness of responsible AI use and data governance implications



You will also display

  • Clear communication with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, able to explain complex findings in plain language and tell the story behind the data
  • Genuine intellectual curiosity, actively exploring new tools and methods, including AI, and sharing what you learn with the team
  • Comfort with ambiguity, able to define the right question and find the “so what” before jumping to answer
  • A product mindset, understanding the business objective behind every piece of analysis, not just the request on the surface
  • A proactive approach to improving processes, data quality and ways of working, with the initiative to make it happen, not just flag it
  • A collaborative team attitude, peer reviewing others’ work, sharing knowledge and contributing to a stronger team


Inclusion

At Nando’s, everyone is welcome. Inspired by our Southern African heritage we know and value the richness that diversity brings to Nando’s. We’re committed to creating an inclusive and respectful culture for all; including our existing and future Nandocas, partners and suppliers, customers and the communities we are a part of. We deliberately work hard to create an environment where every individual is valued, respected and can flourish regardless of who they are, their background or outlook on life.


We welcome applications from a diverse range of individuals and will consider any reasonable adjustments in order to enable candidates to perform as well as possible during the recruitment process.

Title: People Insight Lead
Description:
Role: People Insight LeadS...

Role: People Insight Lead

Salary: £47,000 - £52,000 dependant on skills and experience + up to 20% bonus + bens

Contract Type: Permanent

Location: Dunstable - LU5 5XE, 3 days working from the office, 2 days working from home

Role Overview

Are you ready to lead how data shapes people decisions at scale? As a People Insight Lead, you will play a critical role in driving a step-change in how insight informs strategy, partnering senior stakeholders to deliver impactful analytics that influence business direction.

This is more than a reporting role; it’s an opportunity to transform the way People data is used, moving from reactive reporting to proactive, insight-led decision making. You will lead the development of a modern, automated reporting ecosystem, building scalable dashboards, embedding self-service capability, and helping to create a unified “single view” of employee data.

Working at the intersection of People, Finance and Technology, you will translate business priorities into clear analytics roadmaps by delivering deep insights on areas such as retention, hiring, talent, engagement and productivity. You will also introduce more advanced and predictive techniques, ensuring data not only explains the past but shapes the future.

A key part of the role is data leadership: you will mentor analysts, elevate data literacy, and champion best practice in data storytelling and governance. You will work directly with senior leaders, influencing decision-making and ensuring insight is embedded into everyday thinking.

This is a highly visible role where you will see the direct impact of your work, building capability, driving innovation and helping create a truly insight-led organisation.

Minimum Criteria


Proven experience designing and delivering scalable reporting solutions (e.g. building Power BI dashboards, defining KPI frameworks, and standardising metrics across a business)
Demonstrable track record of partnering senior stakeholders to translate business challenges into analytics roadmaps and actionable insight outputs
Strong experience working with People/HR data and systems (e.g. Dayforce) and enterprise data platforms (e.g. Azure), including ensuring data quality, governance and compliance
Hands-on experience applying advanced analytical techniques (e.g. trend analysis, predictive modelling, statistical methods) to generate business insight
Experience leading or mentoring analysts and managing delivery pipelines/backlogs, including prioritisation of insight requests aligned to business impact


We believe that everyone is unique and there should be no barriers to entry and no limits to ambition. We are committed to being an inclusive organisation that values diversity and welcomes your application whatever your background or situation.

Under-represented groups such as women, ethnic minorities, people with disabilities & members of the LGBTQIA+ community (those who identify as lesbian, gay, bi, trans and non-binary or those who use a different LGBTQIA+ term), are strongly encouraged to build a career with us. Speak to us about workplace adjustments, part-time and flexible working. Where possible we will support this.
