Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Karo Healthcare, a dynamic and growing personal-care retail company, is all about making smart choices for everyday healthcare. With a wide-ranging portfolio across seven categories including medical products, we own and sell over 80 brands in about 90 countries. Our portfolio includes such brands as E45, Pevaryl, Lamisil, Nutravita, Decubal and many others. Our core belief in empowering people to make intelligent health decisions drives our growth strategy, blending organic expansion and M&A.

Join us at Karo Healthcare on our exciting journey. We're currently looking for a Business Analyst for our office in Maidenhead (UK).

What's in it for you?


Make a real commercial impact - own and shape Revenue Growth Management analytics in an international consumer healthcare business
Be hands-on with modern tech - work daily with Power BI and Databricks in a scalable Data & Analytics setup
Influence decisions across 90 markets - partner with Commercial teams and turn data into pricing, promotion and portfolio actions
Accelerate your career - join a fast-growing, KKR-backed company where ambition, international exposure and development go hand in hand

What you'll be doing


Translate Revenue Growth Management (RGM) and Sales needs into clear analytical requirements, KPIs and functional specifications
Design, build and continuously enhance Power BI dashboards, reports and data models to drive pricing, promotion and assortment decisions
Collaborate closely with Data Engineers to connect front-end analytics with our Databricks Data Lakehouse
Partner with Commercial, Marketing, Finance and Operations stakeholders across markets to turn data into actionable insights
Ensure data accuracy and quality, test solutions and drive user adoption in an international environment
Contribute to broader commercial and performance analytics initiatives as the function grows



Requirements


3+ years' experience in Business Analysis, Data Analytics or similar roles within FMCG, consumer healthcare, retail or other fast-paced, commercially driven environments
Strong hands-on expertise in Power BI (data modelling, DAX, dashboard development, visualization best practices)
Solid understanding of Revenue Growth Management, Sales analytics and commercial performance metrics
Experience working cross-functionally across markets with senior Commercial, Marketing, Finance, Operations and IT stakeholders
Good knowledge of data modelling concepts, Power Query and data governance, with hands-on working ability in Databricks and SQL
Degree in Business, Finance, Engineering, Computer Science or a related field
Fluency in English, written and spoken



Benefits


Opportunity to create, grow, and encourage
There are lots of growth opportunities to meet your personal ambitions
Flexible schedule, hybrid model with the primary work place in the office, and life-work balance
Responsibility for exciting and challenging projects that have a direct, visible impact on our customers and the industry
Very positive work environment in a dynamic, international, and motivated team
Start-up spirit while being a part of the large international organization with strong values

Karo Healthcare has a Diverse & Inclusive environment. We are looking for qualified candidates irrespective of gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, ethnicity, race, religion, national origin, disability or age.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

The Opportunity

Hult International Business School is seeking a Data Analyst to join the External Affairs team based in London, UK. The post-holder will work closely with the Data and Analytics Manager and have a unique opportunity to positively impact the organisation by providing data analytics, developing actionable insights and delivering exceptional business value.

The role suits an individual with an analytical mindset. The successful candidate will have a few years’ experience working with data and analytics and be able to work under pressure. Attention to detail is critical as the work will support decision-making across the business.

Please note that you must have the right to work in the UK to be considered for this position.

Responsibilities


Supporting major statutory student returns, optimising the impact of those returns and using the resultant data to support the organisation.
Collecting and analysing internal and external data and preparing reports, forecasts, models and ‘what if’ scenarios.
Consulting with internal stakeholders (e.g., Sales, Marketing, Operations, Finance, Academic teams, etc.) to develop analyses that lead to actionable insights.
Collating data from multiple sources and databases including sales, marketing, operations, market trends and competition, finance and customer databases to create integrated views that can be used to drive decision making.
Designing and building reports, dashboards and analyses in Excel, SQL, Python, Salesforce, Tableau, Snowflake.


Essential Requirements


A few years’ experience (typically 2–3 years) in a data analyst or similar analytical role
Exceptionally motivated, curious and strong problem-solving skills
Ability to interpret industry trends and business needs – analytical mindset is key
Understanding of database capabilities and experience of integrating, structuring and analysing large amounts of data from diverse sources
Ability to prioritise, meet challenging deadlines and instil urgency
Flexible, open-minded and agile
Self-directed and self-motivated with a demonstrated strength in reporting, planning and project management skills to meet deadlines
Strong communication skills with the ability and desire to collaborate with key business stakeholders
Bachelor’s degree required
Strong SQL skills required; experience with Python, Tableau and Snowflake is highly desirable
Comfortable using AI tools (e.g., Codex, Claude code or similar) and modern development environments such as VS Code to accelerate analysis, coding and reporting workflows

Title: Yield Specialist
Description:
This role sits within Yield Management UK&I Region of our Commercial Division and reports into the Senior Yield Destination Manager

Have you ever wondered how the price of your package holiday is decided, why they sometimes change from the last time you looked or why the cost varies across different destinations?

Well, why not join our Yield department here at TUI to find out how it works in the world of travel! We are looking to recruit a team of Yield Specialists who get a buzz out of spotting patterns and love working with data to ensure a proactive approach to problem-solving and are commercially driven. You will have accountability for the revenue management of your own range of destinations, striving to optimise sales and profitability ensuring the right price is available to our customers at the right time.

We will ensure that you have first-class training and a support network of colleagues in place to help you succeed in the role. You’ll work through a development framework to support your long-term career goals, with progression opportunities available. A quote from one of our past specialists now in a manager role: ‘I really like being part of a big inclusive team and with a shared objective to sell as many seats/hotels at the best price. It means everyone really has to work together to get the best results'.


We offer flexible hybrid working, with one day a week on average in our Luton office to connect, collaborate and build strong relationships with the team. Brand new offices opening in early 2027, close to M1 Junction 10 and Thameslink train line.


For those shortlisted to the final stage we will host in-person assessment days.

Now taking applications until 26th July 2026, please apply as soon as possible, as we expect a high volume of applications for this role. This may lead us to close the vacancy before the application closing date.

About Our Offer


Personal benefits: Salary £31,000 per annum, trading incentive schemes, generous travel benefits, extensive health & well-being support, and more.
Flexible hybrid working: Work is something you do, not somewhere you go. We encourage a healthy *work-life balance with a dynamic working environment.
A career to shape: Access the TUI Learning Hub to level-up and reach your ambitions.
Broaden your network: We champion intercultural collaboration and provide opportunities to work on global projects and teams
Community: Get involved with incredible local charity and sustainability initiatives like the TUI Care Foundation.


About The Job


You'll be working within a team environment responsible for several holiday destinations, striving to optimise sales, profitability and maximise both flight and hotel performance to hit key performance indicators.
In this analytical role, you'll use yield systems and review booking trends to make informed recommendations and decisions that deliver results in line with your destination strategy.
We'll rely on you to constantly monitor and react to demand in the competitive holiday market, offering relevant pricing solutions to maximise opportunities and overcome sales barriers.
Your decisions will directly drive the price offering to our customers and ultimately feed into the overall financial performance of TUI UK&I.
Building strong internal relationships with key stakeholders across the commercial division and beyond will be essential to drive current and future business strategy.
We offer a clear career pathway for progression, giving you the opportunity to drive your own development while learning alongside some of the best in the industry.


About You


Ideally, you’re have proven analytical experience, with the ability to interpret complex data and translate it into actionable insights.
Your numeracy and data analysis skills allow you to spot trends and make informed pricing decisions.
A structured, focused and conscientious approach to work ensures you deliver consistent results.
Your proactive, curious nature drives you to continuously learn and improve your understanding of the travel market.
Understanding different perspectives – both customers' and colleagues' – comes naturally to you, helping you make balanced decisions.
Highly proficient in Microsoft Office, especially Excel for data analysis and reporting


And of course, it helps if you love holidays as much as we do!

We're passionate about innovation and the role AI can play in the future of work. However, for your application, we'd love to hear about your experiences, achievements and aspirations in your own words rather than AI-generated responses.

From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Permanent role - Up to £45k base + bonus + benefits

Central London - Hybrid Working

Role Summary

Our client is looking for a skilled Data Analyst to join their growing team. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with complex datasets, building reliable data pipelines, and turning data into meaningful insights that support strategic business decisions.

You'll work closely with stakeholders across the business to analyse data, improve reporting, and deliver clear, actionable intelligence.

Requirements

Strong SQL skills, including CTEs, joins, window functions and aggregations.
Experience working with cloud data platforms such as Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks or similar.
Experience building and maintaining data transformation pipelines.
Knowledge of dbt or willingness to learn.
Experience using GitHub or similar version control tools.
Python experience (Pandas or similar) for data manipulation and analysis.
Experience with data visualisation tools such as Tableau, Power BI or ThoughtSpot.
Strong analytical, problem-solving and communication skills.
Excellent written and spoken English.

What Will Make You Stand Out

Experience working with semi-structured data.
Knowledge of data validation and quality assurance frameworks.
Experience with digital analytics or marketing data platforms.
Ability to communicate complex data through compelling visualisations and storytelling.
A collaborative approach and a proactive mindset.

If you are interested in the role please apply, to follow up please message me on LinkedIn.

Data Analyst

Permanent role - Up to £45k base + bonus + benefits

Central London - Hybrid Working

Role Summary

Our client is looking for a skilled Data Analyst to join their growing team. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with complex datasets, building reliable data pipelines, and turning data into meaningful insights that support strategic business decisions.

You'll work closely with stakeholders across the business to analyse data, improve reporting, and deliver clear, actionable intelligence.

Requirements

  • Strong SQL skills, including CTEs, joins, window functions and aggregations.
  • Experience working with cloud data platforms such as Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks or similar.

  • Experience with data visualisation tools such as Tableau, Power BI or ThoughtSpot.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving and communication skills.
  • Excellent written and spoken English.

What Will Make You Stand Out

  • Experience working with semi-structured data.
  • Knowledge of data validation and quality assurance frameworks.
  • Experience with digital analytics or marketing data platforms.
  • Ability to communicate complex data through compelling visualisations and storytelling.
  • A collaborative approach and a proactive mindset.

If you are interested in the role please apply, to follow up please message me on LinkedIn.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Accepting Applications Until

24 July 2026

Job Description

Your new role: Data Analyst

We’re looking for a mid-level Data Analyst with 3–5+ years of commercial experience in a data analytics role, ideally within a fast-paced, data-driven business.

As a Data Analyst at Global, you’ll combine your curiosity for insight and your enthusiasm for delivering effective analytical solutions to help evolve Global’s market-leading business.

Key Responsibilities


Building analytical solutions to meet complex business needs (40%): You will develop a deep understanding of the business, then use that knowledge to effectively gather requirements for pieces of work and create innovative solutions that meet those requirements. Insightful data visualisations in Tableau will be your bread and butter in the reports and deliverables you create.
Solving problems to improve robustness in data processes (30%): You will adapt to the changing data landscape, identifying the root causes of issues that arise and finding efficient solutions to them. Your problem solving skills will support your fellow teams in data through collaboration and clear communication.
Developing your skills and supporting the growth of the team (30%): You will apply a continuous learning approach to your work, improving your methods and expanding your perspectives to realise your potential. You will support your team members, uplifting your team to be greater than the sum of its parts.


What You’ll Love About This Role


Think Big: Help us to define the future of analytics at Global, bringing innovation and big ideas to drive the department forwards
Own It: Take responsibility for your successes and failures, being proud of your work and the effort that went into it
Keep it Simple: Creative effective solutions to business problems without overcomplicating the task at hand
Better Together: Collaborate with a team of like-minded data individuals to produce excellent pieces of work in a supportive and friendly environment


What Success Looks Like

In your first few months, you’ll have:


Gained a strong understanding of our business operating model within our DAX business, and some understanding of our classic audio, outdoor and consumer businesses
Built up a foundation of knowledge of the main data warehouses, systems, and data workflows that your squad works with
Gotten hands on with the data, supporting different projects through building solutions, testing data outputs or answering business questions
Spent time with team members and business stakeholders to understand the ongoing projects and workstreams in DAX, and added your ideas into the mix to help shape the direction that the team moves in


What You’ll Need


3–5+ years of commercial experience in a Data Analyst or similar analytical role
Demonstrated experience owning analytical projects from requirement gathering through to delivery and stakeholder presentation
Experience working with large, complex datasets in a commercial environment
Analytical Mindset: A brain with more questions than answers that you use to challenge the assumptions made by both yourself and others. A logical process for dissecting systems to find the actionable insight within.
Attention to Detail: A rigorous approach to problem solving and a keen eye for detail, to help you diagnose the cause behind the symptoms you see and prescribe the right solution for the situation.
Visualisation Expertise: Experience producing high quality reporting with BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI, and experience querying and wrangling data with SQL.
Collaboration, Coaching and Support: Drive for creating and maintaining an inclusive environment where diverse views and experiences are welcomed and celebrated. Energy to encourage and help those around you so the team grows together.
Communication: The ability to break down complex concepts into their core components to explain them to others, including explaining your fantastic ideas to others to bring them on board!

Title: Data Analytics Engineer
Description:
Data Analytics Engineer | Hybrid | Basingstoke




We're working with a growing financial services business looking to bring in a Data Analytics Engineer to help build out their data platform from the ground up.




You'll own the end-to-end lifecycle of data products - ingestion, modelling and presentation - working closely with stakeholders across the business to turn raw data into something they can actually use and act on.




What you'll be doing:

Designing and building performant data ingestion pipelines using Apache Spark, ideally within Azure Databricks
Contributing to the platform's data modelling and architecture
Building dashboards and semantic models, and presenting these directly to stakeholders
Working across the full pipeline from source systems to target repositories, with a strong focus on data quality
Collaborating with Data Scientists to help productionise ML pipelines
Operating within an Agile/DevOps environment




What we're looking for:

Strong SQL and Python skills
Solid understanding of Lakehouse architecture and data management principles
Hands-on experience with Azure (Data Factory, Databricks, Synapse, Storage, DevOps)
Experience designing Star Schema data models for Power BI
5+ years' experience building data platforms in a commercial setting




Nice to have:

Real-time pipeline experience
Azure Data Engineer certification
Exposure to Kafka or other open-source tools
Background in a regulated industry (finance/insurance)




Details:

£60,000 - £70,000
Permanent position
Hybrid (Basingstoke)

Title: Data Operations Analyst
Description:
About Us

We are a fast-growing Series A SaaS startup at the forefront of the next big shift in marketing, transforming the way brands connect with audiences in virtual worlds. This is a shift on par with the rise of social media, and we are building the analytics engine to power it. You will be joining a tight-knit, high-impact team of 40 people, including a product team of 3 and an engineering team of 10, meaning your work will directly shape the trajectory of our platform and company.

About The Role

Our platform runs on data, and this role owns the work that keeps it flowing: pulling, validating, importing and quality-checking so the wider team can move quickly and with confidence. Sitting within the product team, you will own the operational data layer: the day-to-day work of getting accurate, trustworthy data into the hands of product, client services and the wider business, fast.

This is a hands-on product & operations role, not an engineering or a pure-analysis one. You are the dependable go-to who unblocks the team's data needs and keeps everything flowing and accurate. You write SQL confidently, lean heavily on AI tools to move quickly, and you are comfortable reading code and data models, but you take your pride from being the person who keeps data reliable and accessible, not from building pipelines or chasing the next engineering project.

Title: Digital Analyst
Description:
As a Digital Analyst, you’ll be at the heart of understanding how Virgin Atlantic’s digital channels perform and where they can be made even better for our customers. You’ll transform complex data into clear, actionable insight through dashboards, reporting suites, and analysis that give the business confidence in decision-making.




Reporting into the Product Manager for Digital Performance, you’ll be a key user of Adobe Customer Journey Analytics (CJA), helping to unlock its full potential across the organisation. Your work will shine a light on customer behaviour, identify opportunities to improve journeys, and ensure that leaders across Digital, Ecommerce, and Marketing have the evidence they need to prioritise the right initiatives.




This is an exciting opportunity for someone early in their analytics career who wants to make a real impact. You’ll be hands-on with data every day, and your analysis will directly shape the digital experience of millions of customers.




Day to Day




• Build and maintain dashboards and reports that provide a trusted view of website and app performance.

• Ensure reporting is accurate, consistent, and delivered in a way that empowers stakeholders

• Analyse customer journeys to uncover issues, opportunities, and behaviours that impact performance.

• Share insights that highlight both quick wins and longer-term opportunities

• Leverage session replay and behavioural analytics tool to analyse customer journeys, identify friction points and provide recommendations to improve conversions and enhance user experience

• Support the sizing and valuation of opportunities to improve digital channels.

• Provide evidence that helps prioritise initiatives with the biggest impact

• Support the design, implementation, and analysis of A/B and multivariate tests in collaboration with UX and Product teams

• Provide robust, data-led evaluations of test results to identify significant wins or learnings

• Respond to ad-hoc reporting and analysis requests with clarity and speed

• Present findings in a way that builds confidence and encourages action

• Contribute to the growth of a central reporting suite, ensuring Digital teams have a single source of truth.

• Play an active role in embedding a data-driven culture across Virgin Atlantic




About You

- Degree in a numerate, business, or digital discipline, or equivalent work experience

- Knowledge of digital analytics tools (Adobe Analytics, GA4, or similar)

- Familiarity with dashboarding and visualisation tools

(e.g., Adobe Workspace, Tableau, Power BI)

- Strong Excel skills, SQL and Python

- Working knowledge of session replay tools (e.g., Quantum Metric, Content Square, Hotjar)

- An analytical mindset, with curiosity to explore the “why” behind performance trends

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Murphy is recruiting for a Data Analyst delivering major water infrastructure projects in partnership with multiple clients across the UK.

We provide end-to-end services for major water and sewage companies such as Thames Water, Severn Trent Water, Northern Ireland Water, Yorkshire Water and United Utilities, and for several water-only companies including SES Water, Bristol Water and South East Water.

We also provide a full design, build, operate and maintain service for Uisce Éireann and we currently operate over 25 of their facilities, ensuring they run efficiently and comply fully with EPA regulations.

A day in the life of a Murphy Data Analyst:


Working closely with business reporting stakeholders to understanding reporting and analytical requirements to acquire and present relevant datasets.
Handle large and complex datasets from Primavera P6, SAP SuccessFactors and other key construction centric systems.
Verify the integrity of extracted data.
Awareness of, and support embedding and applying revised Data Governance and Information Security standards and best practices.
Applying Murphy best practices to code management, documentation and release.
Help establish good working practices for the work delivered.
Working as part of the Planning and Project Controls Function, alongside the Resource Management Team.
Project work to be allocated as required, during the duration of the project.
Liaising with planners, operational staff and other technical staff.
Assisting with the designing of maintenance procedures and putting them into operation.
Providing user training, support and feedback as required.
Build effective relationships with all relevant stakeholders.
Provide support and guidance to other team members.
Attend sites to assist remote application users as required.
Ad hoc activities as deemed appropriate by the Head of Planning.
Design and develop applications and workflows using POwer apps and Power Automate to streamline and automate business data processes.
Debugging, testing and troubleshooting systems and applications


Still interested, does this sound like you?


Detailed knowledge and practical experience with Primavera P6 and PowerBI, Power Automate, Power Apps, Dataverse
Experienced with connecting disparate data sources, particularly: Dataverse, Sharepoint, P6
Write and maintain python scripts for data processing, backend logic, API integration and workflow automation
Integrate python processes with Power Platforms solutions
Demonstrable experience with Visual Studio and Azure DevOps toolsets.
Working knowledge of ETL and data ingestion (API knowledge).
Solutions architecture
Understanding of relational databases and data modelling
Experience within construction and/or civil engineering processes and applications.
Happy to travel nationwide across multitude of Murphy projects


What’s in it for you?


27 days holiday, plus bank holidays with the option to buy an additional 2 days holiday each year, holidays increase with length of service
Discretionary annual bonus and annual salary review
Above market rate contributory pension scheme
Life assurance, health screening and enhanced sick pay
Enhanced maternity and paternity pay and a maternity returners bonus
Extra weeks holiday for all employees getting married and a wedding bonus
Subsidised canteen facilities in core locations
Dedicated and continued investment in your professional development
Other Murphy benefits include retail discounts and cashback, discounted gym memberships, cycle to work scheme etc


About Murphy

Murphy is formally recognised as a sector leading employer, Platinum Investors In People accredited along with reported operating profit up 57% on prior year. At Murphy, we are incredibly proud of our diverse workforce. Not only do our people represent over 60 different nationalities, but they also represent a wide range of backgrounds, ethnicities and ages. With a forward order book of £5.8bn and £23m Invested in environmentally friendly plant in 2024 there really is #MoretoMurphy

If you are unable to apply via the usual process, please call James Hing on 07353 134946 to discuss in more detail.

Murphy is unable to employ anyone who does not have the legal right to live and work in the UK

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

The Opportunity

Hult International Business School is seeking a Data Analyst to join the External Affairs team based in London, UK. The post-holder will work closely with the Data and Analytics Manager and have a unique opportunity to positively impact the organisation by providing data analytics, developing actionable insights and delivering exceptional business value.

The role suits an individual with an analytical mindset. The successful candidate will have a few years’ experience working with data and analytics and be able to work under pressure. Attention to detail is critical as the work will support decision-making across the business.

Please note that you must have the right to work in the UK to be considered for this position.

Responsibilities


Supporting major statutory student returns, optimising the impact of those returns and using the resultant data to support the organisation.
Collecting and analysing internal and external data and preparing reports, forecasts, models and ‘what if’ scenarios.
Consulting with internal stakeholders (e.g., Sales, Marketing, Operations, Finance, Academic teams, etc.) to develop analyses that lead to actionable insights.
Collating data from multiple sources and databases including sales, marketing, operations, market trends and competition, finance and customer databases to create integrated views that can be used to drive decision making.
Designing and building reports, dashboards and analyses in Excel, SQL, Python, Salesforce, Tableau, Snowflake.


Essential Requirements


A few years’ experience (typically 2–3 years) in a data analyst or similar analytical role
Exceptionally motivated, curious and strong problem-solving skills
Ability to interpret industry trends and business needs – analytical mindset is key
Understanding of database capabilities and experience of integrating, structuring and analysing large amounts of data from diverse sources
Ability to prioritise, meet challenging deadlines and instil urgency
Flexible, open-minded and agile
Self-directed and self-motivated with a demonstrated strength in reporting, planning and project management skills to meet deadlines
Strong communication skills with the ability and desire to collaborate with key business stakeholders
Bachelor’s degree required
Strong SQL skills required; experience with Python, Tableau and Snowflake is highly desirable
Comfortable using AI tools (e.g., Codex, Claude code or similar) and modern development environments such as VS Code to accelerate analysis, coding and reporting workflows

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Overall Ambition




Nando’s is on a journey to Create Lasting Happiness, making a difference to the lives of our people, our customers, and the communities we work in. Originally started in Johannesburg, South Africa, we pride ourselves on creating memorable experiences for everyone involved in Nando’s: from our employees, communities, founders, and suppliers, but most importantly, our customers.

Whilst we always look back on our history and story with pride, the story is still evolving. We have recently been growing our digital capabilities to ensure the Nando’s experience remains relevant to modern-day customers.




You will be joining a tech team that has grown rapidly in the past five years and is determined to enable change within the restaurant industry by bringing innovative thinking and the latest technology tools and practices to solve some of its most complex problems.







The Data and Analysis Team

We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join our Data and Analysis team. Our remit is to ensure everyone has access to the right information, at the right time, and that Nando’s gets the best value out of our data to achieve our business goals.




Our current focus is extracting insights from the data and creating a single source of accurate, timely data as we continue to increase our data maturity and advance our predictive analytic capability and explore the role of AI in the business.




You will be part of our work to support our strategic goals and build a data-informed attitude for addressing our opportunities and challenges.







Our Values

At Nando’s, our values guide how we work, make decisions, and show up for each other every day:

Pride – We take pride in everything we do, from the quality of our food to the impact of our work.
Passion – We bring energy, enthusiasm, and a love for what we do.
Courage – We speak up, challenge the status quo, and are not afraid to try new things.
Integrity – We do the right thing, act honestly, and build trust with each other.
Family – We care for and support one another, creating a sense of belonging where everyone can thrive.







What we're looking for:

As a Data Analyst you will work across a wide range of domains from financial and operational data, to kitchen tech and stock, helping our teams make better decisions grounded in sound analysis. As a curious problem-solver, you will be able to move between the technical and the commercial; and be comfortable building data models as well as presenting findings to senior stakeholders.




In this role you’ll work independently across the full data stack, understanding the ‘why’ behind requests before jumping to the ‘what’, and contributing to the translation of business problems into well-defined, actionable work. You’ll be supported by a collaborative team and won’t be expected to deliver everything solo from day one, but you’ll be expected to grow into doing so.

You will…







Write and maintain SQL models (in Dataform) to transform and serve reliable data across our pipelines
Support data engineers in the design, building and testing of data pipelines, able to contribute meaningfully, not just consume the output
Build and iterate on cloud-based reports and dashboards in Looker (or similar BI tool), giving our support teams and restaurants clear, actionable insight into performance
Translate business questions into well-scoped analysis and understand the problem and domain before reaching for the answer
Communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, choosing the right medium for the audience
Peer review colleagues’ work and contribute to data quality standards and documentation
Identify opportunities to automate manual processes and data tasks, and take the initiative to implement them







Skills required

You will have proven experience in the following:

Writing complex SQL to query, transform and model data across our cloud data platform (window functions, CTEs, complex datatypes)
Building and maintaining data models in Dataform, or a similar data transformation tool such as Snowflake or DBT, with an understanding of how they fit into the broader pipeline
Building reports and dashboards in Looker or a similar BI tool, serving reliable, self-serve data to the business
Using Git as standard practice — branching, pull requests and code review
Contributing to the translation of business requests into clearly scoped, actionable work items
Applying data quality techniques — writing tests, identifying issues proactively and communicating data limitations clearly
Documenting data models, definitions and lineage to a production standard
Understanding data governance principles and championing good data management practice within the team
Applying and understanding when to use relevant statistical methods to support analysis
Supporting data engineers in designing, coding and testing data processing pipelines
Using AI-assisted coding tools (e.g. Claude) to improve the speed and quality of your work — critically, not blindly — with an awareness of responsible AI use and data governance implications







You will also display

Clear communication with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, able to explain complex findings in plain language and tell the story behind the data
Genuine intellectual curiosity, actively exploring new tools and methods, including AI, and sharing what you learn with the team
Comfort with ambiguity, able to define the right question and find the “so what” before jumping to answer
A product mindset, understanding the business objective behind every piece of analysis, not just the request on the surface
A proactive approach to improving processes, data quality and ways of working, with the initiative to make it happen, not just flag it
A collaborative team attitude, peer reviewing others’ work, sharing knowledge and contributing to a stronger team




Inclusion

At Nando’s, everyone is welcome. Inspired by our Southern African heritage we know and value the richness that diversity brings to Nando’s. We’re committed to creating an inclusive and respectful culture for all; including our existing and future Nandocas, partners and suppliers, customers and the communities we are a part of. We deliberately work hard to create an environment where every individual is valued, respected and can flourish regardless of who they are, their background or outlook on life.




We welcome applications from a diverse range of individuals and will consider any reasonable adjustments in order to enable candidates to perform as well as possible during the recruitment process.

Title: Data Operations Analyst
Description:
Application Deadline: 21 August 2026

Department: Engineering

Location: London



Description

About UsWe are a fast-growing Series A SaaS startup at the forefront of the next big shift in marketing, transforming the way brands connect with audiences in virtual worlds. This is a shift on par with the rise of social media, and we are building the analytics engine to power it. You will be joining a tight-knit, high-impact team of 40 people, including a product team of 3 and an engineering team of 10, meaning your work will directly shape the trajectory of our platform and company.

About the RoleOur platform runs on data, and this role owns the work that keeps it flowing: pulling, validating, importing and quality-checking so the wider team can move quickly and with confidence. Sitting within the product team, you will own the operational data layer: the day-to-day work of getting accurate, trustworthy data into the hands of product, client services and the wider business, fast.

This is a hands-on product & operations role, not an engineering or a pure-analysis one. You are the dependable go-to who unblocks the team's data needs and keeps everything flowing and accurate. You write SQL confidently, lean heavily on AI tools to move quickly, and you are comfortable reading code and data models, but you take your pride from being the person who keeps data reliable and accessible, not from building pipelines or chasing the next engineering project.





Key Responsibilities


Own data access across the product lifecycle: Be the team's first port of call for data requests, pull, shape and deliver data in the format product, CS and sales need.
Validate and quality-check: Sanity-check, validate and QC data across the platform so the team can trust every number. Spot anomalies, chase them down, and keep our data honest.
Manage imports and ingestion ops: Run routine data imports and ingestion tasks, making sure data lands cleanly, completely and on time.
Work AI-first: Use AI tools (Claude, Cursor, Copilot and similar) to rapidly translate business logic or SQL into complex database queries (including NoSQL / Elastic / Mongo), accelerating repetitive work, and continually improve how the team gets and checks data.
Document and share knowledge: Document data sources, queries and processes so knowledge never sits with just one person. You make the team less dependent on any single individual, including yourself.
Own the long tail: Take ownership of the steady stream of ad hoc requests that keeps the wider team moving.
Partner cross-functionally: Work closely with data engineers and product team, translating between technical data and real business needs.


Skills, Knowledge and Expertise


An operations and service mindset: You take genuine pride in being the reliable go-to who keeps data flowing and accurate. You enjoy solving a team's everyday data needs and you are not looking to use this role as a stepping stone into a pure engineering job.
Data Fluency (SQL & NoSQL): You write and debug SQL confidently, but you are also comfortable navigating non-relational/document databases (like MongoDB and Elasticsearch). You don't need to have raw NoSQL syntax memorized, but you should know how to read nested JSON structures.
AI-first working: You already lean heavily on AI tools to work faster and better, and you are always finding new ways to use them.
Technical literacy: You can read code and data models (including how relational data maps to NoSQL/JSON structures), understand how different systems fit together, and pick up light Python.
Rigour and attention to detail: You are meticulous about data accuracy and quality, and you notice when a number looks off.
Bias for action: You are comfortable in the ambiguity of a Series A startup and happy to roll up your sleeves and get things done.
Clear communicator: You can translate between technical data and business stakeholders without friction.
AI-first working: You already lean heavily on AI tools to work faster and better. Crucially, you possess the critical thinking to audit and validate AI-generated outputs, ensuring code/queries are safe and optimised before running them.
Experience: Around 2–4 years in a data operations, data analyst, BI, revenue/business operations or similar hands-on data role.

Bonus Points
Experience in a data operations, analytics operations or revenue operations function.
Familiarity with the modern data stack and BI tooling.
Background in marketing-related SaaS, virtual environments or gaming.
Familiarity with tools such as Linear and Notion.


Why join us?


Join a business at the forefront of the next big shift in marketing.
Be part of a fast-growing startup with a collaborative, innovative and supportive team.
Be genuinely indispensable, this role unlocks something the whole company depends on, so your impact is visible from day one.
A real, non-engineering growth path: grow into owning our data-quality function, take on ROI and attribution research as the team scales.
25 days holiday as standard, plus a bonus GEEIQ Day to use whenever you choose.
We offer Heka, a monthly wellness allowance you can spend across a wide range of fitness and wellbeing providers, plus a Cycle to Work scheme.
We have a thriving company culture with regular socials, team offsites, and events - quizzes, sports days, Hackathons, Bake Offs, and more. Our eNPS is 52, nearly double the industry average, and it shows, the team genuinely loves working here and learning from each other.
You pick your start time, we just ask that everyone's available during core hours of 10am–5pm. That might mean 8am–5pm, 9am–6pm, or 10am–7pm, whatever works best for you.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Accepting Applications Until

24 July 2026

Job Description

Your new role: Data Analyst

We’re looking for a mid-level Data Analyst with 3–5+ years of commercial experience in a data analytics role, ideally within a fast-paced, data-driven business.

As a Data Analyst at Global, you’ll combine your curiosity for insight and your enthusiasm for delivering effective analytical solutions to help evolve Global’s market-leading business.

Key Responsibilities


Building analytical solutions to meet complex business needs (40%): You will develop a deep understanding of the business, then use that knowledge to effectively gather requirements for pieces of work and create innovative solutions that meet those requirements. Insightful data visualisations in Tableau will be your bread and butter in the reports and deliverables you create.
Solving problems to improve robustness in data processes (30%): You will adapt to the changing data landscape, identifying the root causes of issues that arise and finding efficient solutions to them. Your problem solving skills will support your fellow teams in data through collaboration and clear communication.
Developing your skills and supporting the growth of the team (30%): You will apply a continuous learning approach to your work, improving your methods and expanding your perspectives to realise your potential. You will support your team members, uplifting your team to be greater than the sum of its parts.


What You’ll Love About This Role


Think Big: Help us to define the future of analytics at Global, bringing innovation and big ideas to drive the department forwards
Own It: Take responsibility for your successes and failures, being proud of your work and the effort that went into it
Keep it Simple: Creative effective solutions to business problems without overcomplicating the task at hand
Better Together: Collaborate with a team of like-minded data individuals to produce excellent pieces of work in a supportive and friendly environment


What Success Looks Like

In your first few months, you’ll have:


Gained a strong understanding of our business operating model within our DAX business, and some understanding of our classic audio, outdoor and consumer businesses
Built up a foundation of knowledge of the main data warehouses, systems, and data workflows that your squad works with
Gotten hands on with the data, supporting different projects through building solutions, testing data outputs or answering business questions
Spent time with team members and business stakeholders to understand the ongoing projects and workstreams in DAX, and added your ideas into the mix to help shape the direction that the team moves in


What You’ll Need


3–5+ years of commercial experience in a Data Analyst or similar analytical role
Demonstrated experience owning analytical projects from requirement gathering through to delivery and stakeholder presentation
Experience working with large, complex datasets in a commercial environment
Analytical Mindset: A brain with more questions than answers that you use to challenge the assumptions made by both yourself and others. A logical process for dissecting systems to find the actionable insight within.
Attention to Detail: A rigorous approach to problem solving and a keen eye for detail, to help you diagnose the cause behind the symptoms you see and prescribe the right solution for the situation.
Visualisation Expertise: Experience producing high quality reporting with BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI, and experience querying and wrangling data with SQL.
Collaboration, Coaching and Support: Drive for creating and maintaining an inclusive environment where diverse views and experiences are welcomed and celebrated. Energy to encourage and help those around you so the team grows together.
Communication: The ability to break down complex concepts into their core components to explain them to others, including explaining your fantastic ideas to others to bring them on board!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
DATA ANALYST

LONDON/HYBRID

UP TO £45,000




This is an opportunity to join a highly regarded analytics environment where you will play a key role in shaping how data is transformed and used to drive decision making. Sitting at the intersection of analytics and data engineering, this position offers hands on exposure to modern data tooling alongside the chance to influence how data is structured, accessed and interpreted.




ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

The Data Analyst will:

Build, optimise and maintain SQL based data transformations within a cloud data warehouse environment including Snowflake
Connect and consolidate multiple data sources such as web analytics, social data, social listening and brand research
Support the development of scalable and reliable data pipelines, improving existing processes and identifying efficiencies
Contribute to a text classification project analysing online content to evaluate brand presence and quality
Validate and quality check datasets to ensure accuracy and consistency across reporting outputs
Work closely with analysts to enable deeper insight generation and more robust reporting
Gradually take ownership of projects, proactively identifying opportunities for new analysis and process improvement

YOUR SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:

The ideal candidate will have the following skills and experience:

Strong commercial experience writing efficient and reliable SQL, including complex joins, aggregations and working with semi structured data
Experience working within a cloud data warehouse environment such as Snowflake, BigQuery or similar
Working knowledge of Python for data analysis and manipulation is desirable
Understanding of data pipelines and transformation workflows, with an interest in developing further using tools such as dbt
Experience with BI and data visualisation tools such as ThoughtSpot, Tableau or Power BI
Ability to validate data and apply quality assurance processes
Confident communicator, comfortable working with stakeholders across teams to translate data into insight

Central London - Hybrid Working

Our client is looking for a skilled Data Analyst to join their growing team. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with complex datasets, building reliable data pipelines, and turning data into meaningful insights that support strategic business decisions.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About the Role




The Data Analyst role is critical to promoting data as a key differentiator for Nomia. You will help drive innovation by assisting Nomia in becoming a truly data-driven organisation - putting data at the heart of all business decisions and providing actionable insights across the business.




Roles & Responsibilities

Be the platform owner for Nomia's data analytics platform, ThoughtSpot
Build dashboards and reporting across various functions, including executive dashboards, financial reporting, and platform insights

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Park Street, London Bridge (3 days remote working, 2 days office based)

Up to £43,000

Hyde is looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst to join our collaborative and values-driven team. This is a fantastic opportunity to grow your career in a supportive environment that champions employee wellbeing, continuous learning, and long-term development.

As a Business Intelligence Analyst at Hyde, you will produce high-quality reporting, management information and insight that supports effective decision-making across the business. Working closely with stakeholders across the organisation, you will analyse data from a range of systems, develop clear reporting outputs and help strengthen data quality, governance and performance understanding.

Key Duties


Produce timely, accurate KPI reports, dashboards and management information aligned to agreed business definitions.
Extract, manipulate and analyse data using SQL and BI tools such as Power BI, Snowflake, Sigma or similar platforms.
Identify trends, patterns, risks and opportunities within data to support performance management and decision-making.
Promote strong data quality, governance, documentation and auditable reporting processes.
Work with stakeholders and data teams to translate reporting needs into practical, user-friendly analytical solutions.


Why Join Hyde?

Hyde is part of the Hyde group one of the UK’s leading housing providers, managing and owning around 120,000 homes nationwide. We’re committed to building safe, sustainable communities where people can thrive. With a strong social purpose, long-term investment plans, and a focus on innovation, Hyde is a place where you can grow your career while making a real difference.

As a Business Intelligence Analyst we’re seeking someone who can bring:


Proven experience in BI, reporting or data analysis, delivering accurate management information and insight.
Experience with income data (rent arrears/rental roll) is desirable.
Strong technical skills in SQL and BI or analytics tools such as Power BI, AWS Analytical Services, Snowflake, Sigma or similar.
A proactive mindset and passion for improving reporting, automation and evidence-based decision-making.
The ability to interpret complex data, communicate insight clearly and collaborate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.


The Benefits Of Joining Hyde


Excellent pension scheme
Generous holiday allowance
Life assurance
Award-winning flexible benefits platform
Support for learning and career development
Hybrid working options available


Diversity, Inclusion & Accessibility

Equity, diversity and inclusion are central to life at Hyde. We’re committed to creating a truly inclusive workplace where everyone feels respected, valued and able to be themselves. Our aim is to have a workforce that reflects the diversity of the customers and communities we serve, ensuring that different perspectives are represented in decision-making, service delivery, and the way we shape our organisation. By fostering an environment where all voices are heard and valued, we can better understand the needs of our communities and deliver services that are fair, accessible and impactful.

As a Disability Confident Employer, we’re committed to providing reasonable adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond.

We reserve the right to close this advert early if a suitable candidate is identified.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Murphy is recruiting for a Data Analyst delivering major water infrastructure projects in partnership with multiple clients across the UK.

We provide end-to-end services for major water and sewage companies such as Thames Water, Severn Trent Water, Northern Ireland Water, Yorkshire Water and United Utilities, and for several water-only companies including SES Water, Bristol Water and South East Water.

We also provide a full design, build, operate and maintain service for Uisce Éireann and we currently operate over 25 of their facilities, ensuring they run efficiently and comply fully with EPA regulations.

A day in the life of a Murphy Data Analyst:


Working closely with business reporting stakeholders to understanding reporting and analytical requirements to acquire and present relevant datasets.
Handle large and complex datasets from Primavera P6, SAP SuccessFactors and other key construction centric systems.
Verify the integrity of extracted data.
Awareness of, and support embedding and applying revised Data Governance and Information Security standards and best practices.
Applying Murphy best practices to code management, documentation and release.
Help establish good working practices for the work delivered.
Working as part of the Planning and Project Controls Function, alongside the Resource Management Team.
Project work to be allocated as required, during the duration of the project.
Liaising with planners, operational staff and other technical staff.
Assisting with the designing of maintenance procedures and putting them into operation.
Providing user training, support and feedback as required.
Build effective relationships with all relevant stakeholders.
Provide support and guidance to other team members.
Attend sites to assist remote application users as required.
Ad hoc activities as deemed appropriate by the Head of Planning.
Design and develop applications and workflows using POwer apps and Power Automate to streamline and automate business data processes.
Debugging, testing and troubleshooting systems and applications


Still interested, does this sound like you?


Detailed knowledge and practical experience with Primavera P6 and PowerBI, Power Automate, Power Apps, Dataverse
Experienced with connecting disparate data sources, particularly: Dataverse, Sharepoint, P6
Write and maintain python scripts for data processing, backend logic, API integration and workflow automation
Integrate python processes with Power Platforms solutions
Demonstrable experience with Visual Studio and Azure DevOps toolsets.
Working knowledge of ETL and data ingestion (API knowledge).
Solutions architecture
Understanding of relational databases and data modelling
Experience within construction and/or civil engineering processes and applications.
Happy to travel nationwide across multitude of Murphy projects


What’s in it for you?


27 days holiday, plus bank holidays with the option to buy an additional 2 days holiday each year, holidays increase with length of service
Discretionary annual bonus and annual salary review
Above market rate contributory pension scheme
Life assurance, health screening and enhanced sick pay
Enhanced maternity and paternity pay and a maternity returners bonus
Extra weeks holiday for all employees getting married and a wedding bonus
Subsidised canteen facilities in core locations
Dedicated and continued investment in your professional development
Other Murphy benefits include retail discounts and cashback, discounted gym memberships, cycle to work scheme etc


About Murphy

Murphy is formally recognised as a sector leading employer, Platinum Investors In People accredited along with reported operating profit up 57% on prior year. At Murphy, we are incredibly proud of our diverse workforce. Not only do our people represent over 60 different nationalities, but they also represent a wide range of backgrounds, ethnicities and ages. With a forward order book of £5.8bn and £23m Invested in environmentally friendly plant in 2024 there really is #MoretoMurphy

If you are unable to apply via the usual process, please call James Hing on 07353 134946 to discuss in more detail.

Murphy is unable to employ anyone who does not have the legal right to live and work in the UK

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

The Opportunity

Hult International Business School is seeking a Data Analyst to join the External Affairs team based in London, UK. The post-holder will work closely with the Data and Analytics Manager and have a unique opportunity to positively impact the organisation by providing data analytics, developing actionable insights and delivering exceptional business value.

The role suits an individual with an analytical mindset. The successful candidate will have a few years’ experience working with data and analytics and be able to work under pressure. Attention to detail is critical as the work will support decision-making across the business.

Please note that you must have the right to work in the UK to be considered for this position.

Responsibilities


Supporting major statutory student returns, optimising the impact of those returns and using the resultant data to support the organisation.
Collecting and analysing internal and external data and preparing reports, forecasts, models and ‘what if’ scenarios.
Consulting with internal stakeholders (e.g., Sales, Marketing, Operations, Finance, Academic teams, etc.) to develop analyses that lead to actionable insights.
Collating data from multiple sources and databases including sales, marketing, operations, market trends and competition, finance and customer databases to create integrated views that can be used to drive decision making.
Designing and building reports, dashboards and analyses in Excel, SQL, Python, Salesforce, Tableau, Snowflake.


Essential Requirements


A few years’ experience (typically 2–3 years) in a data analyst or similar analytical role
Exceptionally motivated, curious and strong problem-solving skills
Ability to interpret industry trends and business needs – analytical mindset is key
Understanding of database capabilities and experience of integrating, structuring and analysing large amounts of data from diverse sources
Ability to prioritise, meet challenging deadlines and instil urgency
Flexible, open-minded and agile
Self-directed and self-motivated with a demonstrated strength in reporting, planning and project management skills to meet deadlines
Strong communication skills with the ability and desire to collaborate with key business stakeholders
Bachelor’s degree required
Strong SQL skills required; experience with Python, Tableau and Snowflake is highly desirable
Comfortable using AI tools (e.g., Codex, Claude code or similar) and modern development environments such as VS Code to accelerate analysis, coding and reporting workflows

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Overall Ambition




Nando’s is on a journey to Create Lasting Happiness, making a difference to the lives of our people, our customers, and the communities we work in. Originally started in Johannesburg, South Africa, we pride ourselves on creating memorable experiences for everyone involved in Nando’s: from our employees, communities, founders, and suppliers, but most importantly, our customers.

Whilst we always look back on our history and story with pride, the story is still evolving. We have recently been growing our digital capabilities to ensure the Nando’s experience remains relevant to modern-day customers.




You will be joining a tech team that has grown rapidly in the past five years and is determined to enable change within the restaurant industry by bringing innovative thinking and the latest technology tools and practices to solve some of its most complex problems.







The Data and Analysis Team

We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join our Data and Analysis team. Our remit is to ensure everyone has access to the right information, at the right time, and that Nando’s gets the best value out of our data to achieve our business goals.




Our current focus is extracting insights from the data and creating a single source of accurate, timely data as we continue to increase our data maturity and advance our predictive analytic capability and explore the role of AI in the business.




You will be part of our work to support our strategic goals and build a data-informed attitude for addressing our opportunities and challenges.







Our Values

At Nando’s, our values guide how we work, make decisions, and show up for each other every day:

Pride – We take pride in everything we do, from the quality of our food to the impact of our work.
Passion – We bring energy, enthusiasm, and a love for what we do.
Courage – We speak up, challenge the status quo, and are not afraid to try new things.
Integrity – We do the right thing, act honestly, and build trust with each other.
Family – We care for and support one another, creating a sense of belonging where everyone can thrive.







What we're looking for:

As a Data Analyst you will work across a wide range of domains from financial and operational data, to kitchen tech and stock, helping our teams make better decisions grounded in sound analysis. As a curious problem-solver, you will be able to move between the technical and the commercial; and be comfortable building data models as well as presenting findings to senior stakeholders.




In this role you’ll work independently across the full data stack, understanding the ‘why’ behind requests before jumping to the ‘what’, and contributing to the translation of business problems into well-defined, actionable work. You’ll be supported by a collaborative team and won’t be expected to deliver everything solo from day one, but you’ll be expected to grow into doing so.

You will…







Write and maintain SQL models (in Dataform) to transform and serve reliable data across our pipelines
Support data engineers in the design, building and testing of data pipelines, able to contribute meaningfully, not just consume the output
Build and iterate on cloud-based reports and dashboards in Looker (or similar BI tool), giving our support teams and restaurants clear, actionable insight into performance
Translate business questions into well-scoped analysis and understand the problem and domain before reaching for the answer
Communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, choosing the right medium for the audience
Peer review colleagues’ work and contribute to data quality standards and documentation
Identify opportunities to automate manual processes and data tasks, and take the initiative to implement them







Skills required

You will have proven experience in the following:

Writing complex SQL to query, transform and model data across our cloud data platform (window functions, CTEs, complex datatypes)
Building and maintaining data models in Dataform, or a similar data transformation tool such as Snowflake or DBT, with an understanding of how they fit into the broader pipeline
Building reports and dashboards in Looker or a similar BI tool, serving reliable, self-serve data to the business
Using Git as standard practice — branching, pull requests and code review
Contributing to the translation of business requests into clearly scoped, actionable work items
Applying data quality techniques — writing tests, identifying issues proactively and communicating data limitations clearly
Documenting data models, definitions and lineage to a production standard
Understanding data governance principles and championing good data management practice within the team
Applying and understanding when to use relevant statistical methods to support analysis
Supporting data engineers in designing, coding and testing data processing pipelines
Using AI-assisted coding tools (e.g. Claude) to improve the speed and quality of your work — critically, not blindly — with an awareness of responsible AI use and data governance implications







You will also display

Clear communication with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, able to explain complex findings in plain language and tell the story behind the data
Genuine intellectual curiosity, actively exploring new tools and methods, including AI, and sharing what you learn with the team
Comfort with ambiguity, able to define the right question and find the “so what” before jumping to answer
A product mindset, understanding the business objective behind every piece of analysis, not just the request on the surface
A proactive approach to improving processes, data quality and ways of working, with the initiative to make it happen, not just flag it
A collaborative team attitude, peer reviewing others’ work, sharing knowledge and contributing to a stronger team




Inclusion

At Nando’s, everyone is welcome. Inspired by our Southern African heritage we know and value the richness that diversity brings to Nando’s. We’re committed to creating an inclusive and respectful culture for all; including our existing and future Nandocas, partners and suppliers, customers and the communities we are a part of. We deliberately work hard to create an environment where every individual is valued, respected and can flourish regardless of who they are, their background or outlook on life.




We welcome applications from a diverse range of individuals and will consider any reasonable adjustments in order to enable candidates to perform as well as possible during the recruitment process.

Title: Data Operations Analyst
Description:
Application Deadline: 21 August 2026

Department: Engineering

Location: London



Description

About UsWe are a fast-growing Series A SaaS startup at the forefront of the next big shift in marketing, transforming the way brands connect with audiences in virtual worlds. This is a shift on par with the rise of social media, and we are building the analytics engine to power it. You will be joining a tight-knit, high-impact team of 40 people, including a product team of 3 and an engineering team of 10, meaning your work will directly shape the trajectory of our platform and company.

About the RoleOur platform runs on data, and this role owns the work that keeps it flowing: pulling, validating, importing and quality-checking so the wider team can move quickly and with confidence. Sitting within the product team, you will own the operational data layer: the day-to-day work of getting accurate, trustworthy data into the hands of product, client services and the wider business, fast.

This is a hands-on product & operations role, not an engineering or a pure-analysis one. You are the dependable go-to who unblocks the team's data needs and keeps everything flowing and accurate. You write SQL confidently, lean heavily on AI tools to move quickly, and you are comfortable reading code and data models, but you take your pride from being the person who keeps data reliable and accessible, not from building pipelines or chasing the next engineering project.





Key Responsibilities


Own data access across the product lifecycle: Be the team's first port of call for data requests, pull, shape and deliver data in the format product, CS and sales need.
Validate and quality-check: Sanity-check, validate and QC data across the platform so the team can trust every number. Spot anomalies, chase them down, and keep our data honest.
Manage imports and ingestion ops: Run routine data imports and ingestion tasks, making sure data lands cleanly, completely and on time.
Work AI-first: Use AI tools (Claude, Cursor, Copilot and similar) to rapidly translate business logic or SQL into complex database queries (including NoSQL / Elastic / Mongo), accelerating repetitive work, and continually improve how the team gets and checks data.
Document and share knowledge: Document data sources, queries and processes so knowledge never sits with just one person. You make the team less dependent on any single individual, including yourself.
Own the long tail: Take ownership of the steady stream of ad hoc requests that keeps the wider team moving.
Partner cross-functionally: Work closely with data engineers and product team, translating between technical data and real business needs.


Skills, Knowledge and Expertise


An operations and service mindset: You take genuine pride in being the reliable go-to who keeps data flowing and accurate. You enjoy solving a team's everyday data needs and you are not looking to use this role as a stepping stone into a pure engineering job.
Data Fluency (SQL & NoSQL): You write and debug SQL confidently, but you are also comfortable navigating non-relational/document databases (like MongoDB and Elasticsearch). You don't need to have raw NoSQL syntax memorized, but you should know how to read nested JSON structures.
AI-first working: You already lean heavily on AI tools to work faster and better, and you are always finding new ways to use them.
Technical literacy: You can read code and data models (including how relational data maps to NoSQL/JSON structures), understand how different systems fit together, and pick up light Python.
Rigour and attention to detail: You are meticulous about data accuracy and quality, and you notice when a number looks off.
Bias for action: You are comfortable in the ambiguity of a Series A startup and happy to roll up your sleeves and get things done.
Clear communicator: You can translate between technical data and business stakeholders without friction.
AI-first working: You already lean heavily on AI tools to work faster and better. Crucially, you possess the critical thinking to audit and validate AI-generated outputs, ensuring code/queries are safe and optimised before running them.
Experience: Around 2–4 years in a data operations, data analyst, BI, revenue/business operations or similar hands-on data role.

Bonus Points
Experience in a data operations, analytics operations or revenue operations function.
Familiarity with the modern data stack and BI tooling.
Background in marketing-related SaaS, virtual environments or gaming.
Familiarity with tools such as Linear and Notion.


Why join us?


Join a business at the forefront of the next big shift in marketing.
Be part of a fast-growing startup with a collaborative, innovative and supportive team.
Be genuinely indispensable, this role unlocks something the whole company depends on, so your impact is visible from day one.
A real, non-engineering growth path: grow into owning our data-quality function, take on ROI and attribution research as the team scales.
25 days holiday as standard, plus a bonus GEEIQ Day to use whenever you choose.
We offer Heka, a monthly wellness allowance you can spend across a wide range of fitness and wellbeing providers, plus a Cycle to Work scheme.
We have a thriving company culture with regular socials, team offsites, and events - quizzes, sports days, Hackathons, Bake Offs, and more. Our eNPS is 52, nearly double the industry average, and it shows, the team genuinely loves working here and learning from each other.
You pick your start time, we just ask that everyone's available during core hours of 10am–5pm. That might mean 8am–5pm, 9am–6pm, or 10am–7pm, whatever works best for you.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Accepting Applications Until

24 July 2026

Job Description

Your new role: Data Analyst

We’re looking for a mid-level Data Analyst with 3–5+ years of commercial experience in a data analytics role, ideally within a fast-paced, data-driven business.

As a Data Analyst at Global, you’ll combine your curiosity for insight and your enthusiasm for delivering effective analytical solutions to help evolve Global’s market-leading business.

Key Responsibilities


Building analytical solutions to meet complex business needs (40%): You will develop a deep understanding of the business, then use that knowledge to effectively gather requirements for pieces of work and create innovative solutions that meet those requirements. Insightful data visualisations in Tableau will be your bread and butter in the reports and deliverables you create.
Solving problems to improve robustness in data processes (30%): You will adapt to the changing data landscape, identifying the root causes of issues that arise and finding efficient solutions to them. Your problem solving skills will support your fellow teams in data through collaboration and clear communication.
Developing your skills and supporting the growth of the team (30%): You will apply a continuous learning approach to your work, improving your methods and expanding your perspectives to realise your potential. You will support your team members, uplifting your team to be greater than the sum of its parts.


What You’ll Love About This Role


Think Big: Help us to define the future of analytics at Global, bringing innovation and big ideas to drive the department forwards
Own It: Take responsibility for your successes and failures, being proud of your work and the effort that went into it
Keep it Simple: Creative effective solutions to business problems without overcomplicating the task at hand
Better Together: Collaborate with a team of like-minded data individuals to produce excellent pieces of work in a supportive and friendly environment


What Success Looks Like

In your first few months, you’ll have:


Gained a strong understanding of our business operating model within our DAX business, and some understanding of our classic audio, outdoor and consumer businesses
Built up a foundation of knowledge of the main data warehouses, systems, and data workflows that your squad works with
Gotten hands on with the data, supporting different projects through building solutions, testing data outputs or answering business questions
Spent time with team members and business stakeholders to understand the ongoing projects and workstreams in DAX, and added your ideas into the mix to help shape the direction that the team moves in


What You’ll Need


3–5+ years of commercial experience in a Data Analyst or similar analytical role
Demonstrated experience owning analytical projects from requirement gathering through to delivery and stakeholder presentation
Experience working with large, complex datasets in a commercial environment
Analytical Mindset: A brain with more questions than answers that you use to challenge the assumptions made by both yourself and others. A logical process for dissecting systems to find the actionable insight within.
Attention to Detail: A rigorous approach to problem solving and a keen eye for detail, to help you diagnose the cause behind the symptoms you see and prescribe the right solution for the situation.
Visualisation Expertise: Experience producing high quality reporting with BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI, and experience querying and wrangling data with SQL.
Collaboration, Coaching and Support: Drive for creating and maintaining an inclusive environment where diverse views and experiences are welcomed and celebrated. Energy to encourage and help those around you so the team grows together.
Communication: The ability to break down complex concepts into their core components to explain them to others, including explaining your fantastic ideas to others to bring them on board!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
DATA ANALYST

LONDON/HYBRID

UP TO £45,000




This is an opportunity to join a highly regarded analytics environment where you will play a key role in shaping how data is transformed and used to drive decision making. Sitting at the intersection of analytics and data engineering, this position offers hands on exposure to modern data tooling alongside the chance to influence how data is structured, accessed and interpreted.




ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

The Data Analyst will:

Build, optimise and maintain SQL based data transformations within a cloud data warehouse environment including Snowflake
Connect and consolidate multiple data sources such as web analytics, social data, social listening and brand research
Support the development of scalable and reliable data pipelines, improving existing processes and identifying efficiencies
Contribute to a text classification project analysing online content to evaluate brand presence and quality
Validate and quality check datasets to ensure accuracy and consistency across reporting outputs
Work closely with analysts to enable deeper insight generation and more robust reporting
Gradually take ownership of projects, proactively identifying opportunities for new analysis and process improvement

YOUR SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:

The ideal candidate will have the following skills and experience:

Strong commercial experience writing efficient and reliable SQL, including complex joins, aggregations and working with semi structured data
Experience working within a cloud data warehouse environment such as Snowflake, BigQuery or similar
Working knowledge of Python for data analysis and manipulation is desirable
Understanding of data pipelines and transformation workflows, with an interest in developing further using tools such as dbt
Experience with BI and data visualisation tools such as ThoughtSpot, Tableau or Power BI
Ability to validate data and apply quality assurance processes
Confident communicator, comfortable working with stakeholders across teams to translate data into insight
