Title: Data Designer I
Description:
Work Location:

Toronto, Ontario, Canada

Hours

37.5

Line Of Business

Technology Solutions

Pay Details

$69,700 - $98,400 CAD

This role is eligible for a discretionary variable compensation award that considers business and individual performance.

TD is committed to providing fair and equitable compensation opportunities to all colleagues. Growth opportunities and skill development are defining features of the colleague experience at TD. Our compensation policies and practices have been designed to allow colleagues to progress through the salary range over time as they progress in their role. The base pay actually offered may vary based upon the candidate's skills and experience, job-related knowledge, geographic location, and other specific business and organizational needs.

As a candidate, you are encouraged to ask compensation related questions and have an open dialogue with your recruiter who can provide you more specific details for this role.

Job Description

Data Design and Quality Engineering, owning Data Lineage/Analysis and DQ validation delivery/test case management/ and documentations, improving coordination with business and technology partners for TD Data and ensuring deliverables continue to meet compliance expectations and committed timelines.


Develop and promote data models using industry standard modeling tools
Assess business requirements by researching / analyzing and documenting business data requirements
Define, build and manage data dictionary / catalog for respective data platforms, systems / servers /applications
Ensure metadata and data lineage is captured and compatible with enterprise metadata and data management tools and processes
Run quality assurance reports and resolve data integrity issues to ensure accurate reporting and data records
Gather, review, analyze, profile, validate, and map system data into the prototyping solution for further integration within data warehouse. Ensure reporting and analytical business needs are met
Develop solutions and recommendations for improving data quality and data integrity issues.
Analyze data issues and work with development teams to resolve issues
Understand data initiatives and capabilities, data governance principles and how they apply to the business segment
Ensure business data and information is retained and disposed in compliance with enterprise data standards, policies and guidelines
Contribute to various data activities and processes as assigned
Be knowledgeable of practices and procedures within own area of responsibility and keep updated on emerging trends for own functional area
Protect the interests of the organization – identify and manage risks, and escalate non-standard, high risk activities as necessary
Participate in and help implement continuous process / improvements
Identify, recommend and effectively execute standard practices applicable to the discipline
Adhere to internal policies/procedures and applicable regulatory guidelines
Keep updated on emerging issues, trends, and evolving regulatory requirements and assess potential impacts; Maintain a culture of risk management and control, supported by effective processes in alignment with risk appetite


SHAREHOLDER


Collaborate with technology and business teams to ensure alignment and successful delivery of data solutions.
Facilitate data analysis and investigations for business users and QA teams, assisting with data issues and validating business cases during development and testing phases
Provide support on business impact post-implementation, ensuring the data solution meets the intended business outcomes during the warranty period.
Coordinate business requirements with technical teams with a goal of ensuring smooth integration of data into source code repositories and promoting effective source code management practices.
Be an active participant of business and technical design reviews, ensuring that data architectures meet both operational and business objectives.
Raise and manage change requests in collaboration with business stakeholders, supporting release management processes and ensuring timely delivery.
Understand data governance and data management initiatives to ensure business requirements align with enterprise data governance policies.
Ensure business metadata and data lineage are well-documented and integrated with enterprise metadata management tools, facilitating better business understanding.
Adhere to best practices for data security, ensuring both technical and business aspects are protected from common vulnerabilities.
Document business requirements and technical decisions, ensuring risks, lessons learned, and enhancement opportunities are communicated across teams.
Help safeguarding the organization's interests by identifying business risks, while ensuring compliance with internal policies and regulatory guidelines.
Stay current on industry trends, growing knowledge of business processes, data tools, and best practices to improve collaboration between business and technology teams.
Adhere to internal policies/procedures and applicable regulatory guidelines


EMPLOYEE / TEAM


Participate fully as a member of the team, support a positive work environment that promotes service to the business, quality, innovation and teamwork and ensure timely communication of issues/ points of interest
Support the team by continuously enhancing knowledge / expertise in own area and participate in knowledge transfer within the team and business unit
Keep current on emerging trends/ developments and grow knowledge of the business, related tools and techniques
Participate in personal performance management and development activities, including cross training within own team
Keep others informed and up-to-date about the status / progress of projects and / or all relevant or useful information related to day-to-day activities
Contribute to the success of the team by willingly assisting others in the completion and performance of work activities; provide training, coaching and/or guidance as appropriate.
Contribute to a fair, positive and equitable environment that supports a diverse workforce
Act as a brand ambassador for your business area/function and the bank, both internally and/or externally


Breadth & Depth


Experienced working professional role providing specialized business support, within their own area of specialty
Requires experienced, conceptual and practical knowledge within their own specialty
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills and fluent in at least one programming language
Lead projects or work streams within broader projects with moderate risk and may be accountable for managing ongoing activities processes and functional programs
Accountable for own work and sometimes others; acts as an escalation point or knowledge resource for others in their own area
Experience working with very large datasets and familiarity with big data technologies
Able to work with minimal direction on multiple projects and priorities
Keep abreast of rapid business and technology innovation within business information management field
Familiar with visualization tools
Generally reports to a Senior / Manager role


Experience And / Or Education


Undergraduate degree or Technical Certificate and/or
2+ years of relevant experience from a business administration, statistical, mathematical, scientific or financial background
Python, Azure Databricks
Excellent Analytical and Communication skills.


Who We Are

TD is one of the world's leading global financial institutions and is the fifth largest bank in North America by branches/stores. Every day, we strive to make every interaction, product, and experience remarkably human and refreshingly simple for over 27 million households and businesses in Canada, the United States and around the world. More than 95,000 TD colleagues bring their skills, talent, and creativity to foster deeper relationships, ensure disciplined execution, and build a simpler, faster banking experience. TD is deeply committed to being a leader in client experience, that is why we believe that all colleagues, no matter where they work, are client facing. Together, we are reimagining what banking can be for our clients, colleagues and communities.

Our Total Rewards Package

Our Total Rewards package reflects the investments we make in our colleagues to help them and their families achieve their financial, physical, and mental well-being goals. Total Rewards at TD includes a base salary, variable compensation, and several other key plans such as health and well-being benefits, savings and retirement programs, paid time off, banking benefits and discounts, career development, and reward and recognition programs. Learn more

Additional Information

We’re delighted that you’re considering building a career with TD. Through regular development conversations, training programs, and a competitive benefits plan, we’re committed to providing the support our colleagues need to thrive both at work and at home.

Please be advised that this job opportunity is subject to provincial regulation for employment purposes. It is imperative to acknowledge that each province or territory within the jurisdiction of Canada may have its own set of regulations, requirements.

Colleague Development

If you’re interested in a specific career path or are looking to build certain skills, we want to help you succeed. You’ll have regular career, development, and performance conversations with your manager, as well as access to an online learning platform and a variety of mentoring programs to help you unlock future opportunities.

If you’re passionate about helping clients and building deep, lasting relationships, TD offers diverse career paths where you can grow your expertise and make a meaningful impact.

We're committed to your success and foster a respectful workplace where diverse perspectives are valued, everyone has fair opportunities to grow, and you can unlock your full potential to achieve your career goals. Here at TD, we hire and develop the best.

Training & Onboarding

We will provide training and onboarding sessions to ensure that you’ve got everything you need to succeed in your new role.

Interview Process

We’ll reach out to candidates of interest to schedule an interview. We do our best to communicate outcomes to all applicants by email or phone call.

Accommodation

Your accessibility is important to us. Please let us know if you’d like accommodations (including accessible meeting rooms, captioning for virtual interviews, etc.) to help us remove barriers so that you can participate throughout the interview process.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Data Engineer

Description

POSITION/JOB TITLE: Data Engineer

DEPARTMENT: Engineering


REQUIREMENTS: Qualified candidates must be legally authorized to be employed in the United States on a full-time basis for any position. Omnitech will not provide sponsorship for employment visa status (e.g., H-1B or TN status) for this position.***
On-site position, NO remote. ****


Do you have a data-driven, engineering mind and a consulting-type personality?

Omnitech is an established, locally owned software engineering firm specializing in helping businesses create and fulfill opportunities through custom technology solutions. We are a Microsoft Partner that values a community of mentors to cultivate skills and interests that align with our clients’ needs. We understand that the journey to the solution can be just as important as the destination – for clients and our engineers.

As a Data Engineer at Omnitech, you would qualify for multiple labels including business intelligence consultant, data warehouse consultant, data acquisition (ETL/ELT) consultant, database administrator consultant, data analyst, data architect, data scientist to name a few. We don’t believe in narrowly defined labels and hierarchy. We believe in helping people solve problems.

DESIRED SKILLS: (The applicable candidate must possess a number of the following.)


Strong T-SQL skills
SQL Server design and development experience
Understanding of data profiling techniques
Understanding of performance optimization, data warehousing
Understanding of common data extraction techniques across a diverse set of sources including structured and non-structured data
Experience with data cleansing and conforming techniques using ETL/ELT processes
Develop standards and best practices to ensure data standardization and consistency as required
Strong experience with dimensional modeling, star schema and Kimball Data Warehouse methodologies
Design and develop data warehousing solutions for clients across a variety of industries and business sizes utilizing Microsoft's SQL Server Platform
Perform the role of subject matter expert for Microsoft Business Intelligence technologies including Power BI and Microsoft Fabric
Knowledge of Semantic models and Data Analysis Expressions (DAX)
Experience with Big Data technology (e.g. Spark, Parquet) a plus
Familiarity with storage technologies (e.g. SAN, NAS, etc.) a plus
Translate business requirements and technical designs into well-developed solutions that meet client business goals
Ability to explain the pros and cons of architectural decisions
Evaluate and recommend new technologies as required
Design and implement technology best practices, guidelines and repeatable processes
Provide technical assistance and cross training to other team members and clients
Participate in the business intelligence community to promote the use of the Microsoft BI platform and general data warehousing best practices
Assist in pre-sales, scoping and requirements gathering process
Ability to work closely with other project team members such as sales analysts, project managers, and software engineers


Requirements


3 years Data experience
A Bachelor’s Degree in computer science, with emphasis in mathematics, or a similar analytical degree
T-SQL, SQL Server, ETL/ELT, Cloud Data Platforms
Desire for continuous learning and to pursue professional certifications
Proven ability to consult and mentor others


Benefits And Perks


Competitive salary with annual reviews
Paid Time Off and Paid Holidays
Insurance options include Health, Vision, and Supplemental Options
Employer-paid Dental, Short-Term and Long-Term Disability
Safe Harbor 401(k) with company match
Dependent Care Flex Accounts
Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Food, snacks, drinks
Tuition reimbursement


Personal Characteristics


Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, confident with customers
Detail-oriented, well-organized, and excellent ability to multi-task
Energetic, comfortable working in a fast-paced environment
Hard-working and motivated, able to take initiative and meet deadlines
Comfortable working in a team-based environment
A hands-on attitude in a friendly work environment.


If you excel in a team centric learning environment and you’re passionate about technology, please apply.


Omnitech participates in E-Verify and will provide the federal government with Form I-9 information to confirm work authorization.
Omnitech is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Title: Anaplan Analyst
Description:
We are working with a technology company who is looking for a Anaplan Analyst jpin their team. This role will focus on Anaplan to enhance forecasting, KPI reporting, planning, and reporting processes.




Responsibilities

Design, build, and maintain Anaplan models to support business processes such as revenue forecasting, profitability analysis, and operational planning.
Manage data imports, exports, and transformations within Anaplan.
Ensure models are scalable, efficient, and aligned with best practices.
Identify and implement improvements to existing Anaplan models to enhance performance and usability.
Monitor system performance and troubleshoot issues as needed.
Provide weekly insights on business performance through KPI dashboards in and outside of Anaplan
Develop dashboards and reports within Anaplan to provide real-time insights into key business metrics.
Continue to build process enhancements around reporting, reduce manual processes to increase reporting turnaround times and accuracy
Support ad-hoc analysis and scenario planning to guide strategic decision-making.




Qualifications




Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Data Analytics, or a related field.
Minimum of 2 years of Anaplan experience, including model building.
Anaplan Level 1 and 2 Model Builder certification preferred
Experience in the payments or financial services industry preferred but not required.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights.
Familiarity with payments industry processes such as revenue sharing, fee structures, and profitability reporting is a plus.
Advanced Excel skills; familiarity with SQL or other database tools is a plus.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

Title: Data & Analytics Consultant
Description:
THE WORK:

You’ve Never Been Satisfied with “Good Enough.”




You want to make an impact, not just manage projects, but change how the world gets built. At Accenture Infrastructure & Capital Projects, you’ll do exactly that. You’ll help develop and deliver the factories, grids, transit systems, and public infrastructure that keep communities moving - and do it smarter, safer, and more sustainably than ever before.




You’ll work alongside people who think big and act bold - project managers, engineers, technologists, and strategists who blend real-world experience with digital innovation and AI.




Together, we’re transforming how capital projects are planned, managed, and executed, creating a better way to build for the future.




Because “good enough” builds the past. You’re here to build what’s next, on a team that outperforms every norm.




Visit us here to learn more about Accenture Infrastructure & Capital Projects




(Internal Title: Business System Configuration / Development II)
Client Relationship & Stakeholder Management: Act as the primary bridge between the client’s business teams (Engineering, Construction, Procurement) and the technical data team. You will navigate complex stakeholder dynamics and manage expectations in a high-pressure environment.
Requirements Translation: Lead requirements workshops and working sessions to uncover business needs, clarify ambiguous requirements, and define KPIs aligned to how capital programs are actually managed. Translate business questions into clear technical specifications, data models, and reporting logic.
Business Intelligence & Visualization: Design and develop interactive, high impact executive dashboards in Power BI. You will be responsible for the "last mile" of data delivery—ensuring insights are accurate, intuitive, and actionable. Apply domain knowledge and program management/project controls expertise to ensure reporting products align to best practices.
Data Logic & Engineering: Work within the client’s Snowflake & Power BI environment to design robust data models. While you will collaborate with architects, you must be capable of writing complex SQL transformations to clean, normalize, and prepare EPC data (Oracle Primavera P6, and other PMIS tools etc.) for reporting.
Quality Assurance: Own the validity of the data. You will trace numbers from the dashboard back to the source to ensure trust and alignment with business rules.
Process Oversight: Collaborate with the technical team to monitor CI/CD pipelines (GitHub/DevOps) and troubleshoot data ingestion issues that impact reporting availability.
Working Conditions:
Hybrid: This role's work location is primarily onsite with our client and partners to enable delivery and cultivate our client relationships, but will see some travel to various client sites at key project milestones.




HERE’S WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Business, Engineering, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in Business Intelligence, Project Controls, Data Analytics, or Analytics Engineering.
Stakeholder Management: Proven track record of managing "hard" client relationships, navigating pushback, and driving consensus across technical and non-technical teams.
Power BI Mastery: Advanced proficiency in Power BI, including DAX, data modeling, and UI/UX design principles for executive reporting.
Domain Knowledge: Understanding of capital project controls, scheduling, or construction management
Snowflake & SQL: Strong SQL skills with the ability to query, transform, and model data within Snowflake. You must understand how to prepare data for optimal BI performance.
Data Translation: Experience taking raw data from disparate systems (CSV, Excel, JSON) and turning it into a cohesive business story (Star schema, dimensional modeling).
Agile Collaboration: Comfort working with JIRA, Git, and agile methodologies.
Holding recognized Project Management certification (CAPM, PMP, etc.)




BONUS POINTS IF YOU HAVE:

Technical Depth: Familiarity with Python, dbt, or Azure Data Factory for advanced ETL scenarios
Certifications: SnowPro Core, Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300), and PMP/CSM
Ability to arrange priorities and escalate competing risks to meet deadlines
Keen eye for detail and adept problem-solving abilities
Must have demonstrated experience relaying and receiving information (written and verbal)
Effective skills in Microsoft Office
Equipped to work independently and as part of a team




Compensation at Accenture varies depending on a wide array of factors, which may include but are not limited to the specific office location, role, skill set, and level of experience. As required by local law, Accenture provides a reasonable range of compensation, based on full-time employment, for roles that may be hired as set forth below.




The recruiting efforts for this position are intended to fill an existing position.

The base pay range shown below is intended as a guideline to reflect the majority of offers for this role. It does not represent a maximum limit — in some cases, actual compensation may exceed the range where appropriate.

Title: Candidature spontanée
Description:
Patriarche, c’est une agence d’architecture augmentée.

Chez Patriarche, on croit que les meilleurs projets naissent de la rencontre entre les bonnes expertises.

Notre modèle rassemble, sous un même toit, des professionnel.le.s de plusieurs disciplines qui collaborent à chaque étape d'un projet, de la réflexion stratégique jusqu'à sa réalisation et son évolution...et bien au-delà. Parce qu'un projet ne s'arrête pas à sa livraison. Il évolue, s'adapte et continue de vivre avec celles et ceux qui l'habitent.

Architecture, design intérieur, urbanisme, architecture de paysage, design graphique, immobilier, communication, développement, gestion de projet… Chaque expertise contribue à enrichir la réflexion et à concevoir des lieux qui répondent réellement aux besoins de leurs usagers.

Présente à l'international, Patriarche réunit aujourd'hui plus de 100 expertises au sein de 13 agences à travers le monde. Malgré cette envergure, chaque bureau conserve sa couleur et sa valeur locale.

Rejoindre Patriarche, c'est évoluer dans une équipe ouverte, curieuse et collaborative, où les idées circulent librement et où chacun a l'occasion de contribuer, d'apprendre et de laisser sa marque.

13 agences dans le monde | 5 entités | 100 expertises | 33 nationalités

Et pourquoi “Patriarche” ?

Tout simplement parce qu'il s'agit du nom de famille de Bernard Patriarche, architecte et fondateur de l'agence.

Description Du Poste

Chez Patriarche, on est toujours heureux de rencontrer des personnes passionnées qui ont envie de contribuer à des projets ambitieux et de collaborer au sein d'une équipe multidisciplinaire.

Que vous soyez architecte, technologue en architecture ou que vous évoluiez dans une expertise complémentaire, votre parcours pourrait trouver sa place chez nous.

Entouré.e d'une équipe solide, vous pourriez contribuer à la réalisation de projets variés, de toutes les échelles, dans les secteurs public et privé, ici comme à l'international. Selon votre profil et votre expérience, vous pourriez être appelé.e à participer aux différentes étapes de conception et de réalisation : développement de concepts, coordination, production de plans, détails techniques, suivi de chantier ou accompagnement des équipes de projet.

Profil qui nous inspire


Vous êtes architecte, technologue en architecture ou vous évoluez dans une expertise connexe.
Vous aimez travailler en équipe et collaborer avec différentes disciplines.
Vous faites preuve de rigueur, de curiosité et d'autonomie.
Vous avez envie de contribuer à des projets stimulants et de continuer à faire évoluer votre pratique.
Vous maîtrisez des outils comme Revit ou AutoCAD.
Vous avez une bonne connaissance des méthodes de construction et de la réglementation en vigueur.
Vous êtes basé.e dans la région de Québec (la présence au bureau est requise).
Vous maîtrisez le français comme langue de travail au quotidien.


Ce qui nous distingue


Des projets variés, ici comme à l'international.
Une équipe multidisciplinaire qui travaille en collaboration.
Un environnement où les idées circulent et où chacun peut contribuer.
Des occasions d'apprendre, de partager et de faire évoluer sa pratique.


Conditions et avantages

Intégrer Patriarche, c’est rejoindre un groupe avec des valeurs, qui œuvre chaque jour pour le bien-être et l’épanouissement de son équipe.


Poste permanent à temps plein (35h/semaine).
5 semaines de vacances par année.
Assurances collectives complètes (santé, dentaire, vision, etc.), 100 % payées par l’employeur.
Allocation cellulaire et remboursement du transport en commun.
Des party d’employés de haut niveau (!!)
Un environnement de travail stimulant, collaboratif et ouvert sur le monde.


Nous offrons d'autres avantages qui sauront répondre à tes besoins et à tes aspirations professionnelles.

Si tu es passionné.e par l'architecture, que tu possèdes les compétences et l'expérience requises, et que tu souhaites rejoindre une équipe où tu pourras développer ton plein potentiel, nous serions ravis de recevoir ta candidature.

Merci de nous faire parvenir ton curriculum vitae ainsi qu'une lettre de motivation décrivant ton intérêt pour ce poste. Nous étudierons attentivement chaque candidature et contacterons les personnes correspondant le mieux à nos critères pour une entrevue.

Title: Senior Data Analytics Engineer - Data Insights
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Senior Data Analytics Engineer - Data Insights based in Canada .

This role offers the opportunity to shape a company-wide data ecosystem by building trusted analytics foundations from the ground up.

You will transform complex data sources into reliable models, metrics, and insights that empower smarter business decisions.

Working at the intersection of data engineering, analytics, and business strategy, you will help define how teams understand and use information.

You will collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to create scalable data solutions, improve self-service analytics, and strengthen data-driven decision-making.

The position is ideal for an experienced analytics engineer who enjoys solving complex problems, building impactful data assets, and improving data quality at scale.

You will join a remote-first environment where ownership, collaboration, and continuous innovation are highly valued.

Accountabilities

As a Senior Data Analytics Engineer - Data Insights, you will play a key role in designing and developing the company’s analytics foundation. You will build scalable data models, establish trusted metrics, and enable teams across the organization to make informed decisions through accessible and reliable data.


Partner with Analytics Engineers, Data Engineers, Data Analysts, and business stakeholders to understand decision-making needs and translate them into scalable data solutions.
Architect, build, test, and maintain reliable data models and pipelines using modern analytics engineering practices.
Develop and optimize data transformations within Snowflake and dbt environments to create trusted analytical datasets.
Design and implement metrics layers, dimensions, and KPI frameworks that support self-service analytics and consistent business reporting.
Establish best practices for data modeling, metric definitions, documentation, and analytics workflows.
Transform raw warehouse data into meaningful data assets that support dashboards, reporting, experimentation, and strategic analysis.
Improve data quality, reliability, and accessibility across the organization by developing scalable solutions.
Collaborate with technical and non-technical teams to ensure data products align with business priorities.
Manage complex analytics projects from concept through implementation while maintaining strong stakeholder communication.
Leverage AI tools and techniques to enhance development workflows, documentation quality, and analytics engineering efficiency.
Stay current with emerging technologies and industry trends to continuously improve data capabilities.


Requirements

The ideal candidate is an experienced analytics engineering professional with strong technical expertise, a passion for high-quality data, and the ability to collaborate effectively in a distributed environment.


5+ years of relevant industry experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, business intelligence, or related fields.
Expert-level SQL skills, including experience with advanced data modeling, query optimization, and analytical transformations.
Strong experience with data warehousing concepts, architecture, and best practices.
3+ years of experience working with Python and dbt for data transformation and analytics development.
Hands-on experience building semantic layers, metrics layers, and self-service analytics solutions.
Proven ability to design and deliver scalable data solutions that support business growth.
Experience managing complex data projects independently from planning through delivery.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and troubleshooting abilities.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Experience working effectively in remote, collaborative environments.
Familiarity with source code management tools such as Git or Bitbucket.
Experience working with cloud platforms such as AWS, GCP, or Azure.
Experience handling semi-structured data formats such as JSON.
Experience with business intelligence tools and analytics solutions.
Knowledge of machine learning analytics engineering or marketing analytics is a plus.


Benefits


Opportunity to work in a remote-first SaaS environment focused on building scalable solutions for enterprise customers.
Meaningful, high-impact work influencing product strategy, customer success, and company-wide decision-making.
Company equity participation, providing employees with ownership in long-term growth.
25 days of paid time off annually, increasing to 30 days after five years of employment.
Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance coverage.
Additional protection through life insurance, critical illness coverage, accidental death and dismemberment insurance, and long-term disability coverage.
Annual wellness allowance to support fitness, sports, and outdoor activities.
Paid volunteering days to support community initiatives.
Opportunities for continuous learning, professional development, and career growth.
Flexible remote work environment designed to support collaboration and autonomy.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Senior Data Analytics Engineer - Data Insights based in Canada .

This role offers the opportunity to shape a company-wide data ecosystem by building trusted analytics foundations from the ground up.

You will transform complex data sources into reliable models, metrics, and insights that empower smarter business decisions.

Working at the intersection of data engineering, analytics, and business strategy, you will help define how teams understand and use information.

You will collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to create scalable data solutions, improve self-service analytics, and strengthen data-driven decision-making.

The position is ideal for an experienced analytics engineer who enjoys solving complex problems, building impactful data assets, and improving data quality at scale.

You will join a remote-first environment where ownership, collaboration, and continuous innovation are highly valued.

Accountabilities

As a Senior Data Analytics Engineer - Data Insights, you will play a key role in designing and developing the company’s analytics foundation. You will build scalable data models, establish trusted metrics, and enable teams across the organization to make informed decisions through accessible and reliable data.

  • Partner with Analytics Engineers, Data Engineers, Data Analysts, and business stakeholders to understand decision-making needs and translate them into scalable data solutions.
  • Architect, build, test, and maintain reliable data models and pipelines using modern analytics engineering practices.
  • Develop and optimize data transformations within Snowflake and dbt environments to create trusted analytical datasets.
  • Design and implement metrics layers, dimensions, and KPI frameworks that support self-service analytics and consistent business reporting.
  • Establish best practices for data modeling, metric definitions, documentation, and analytics workflows.
  • Transform raw warehouse data into meaningful data assets that support dashboards, reporting, experimentation, and strategic analysis.
  • Improve data quality, reliability, and accessibility across the organization by developing scalable solutions.
  • Collaborate with technical and non-technical teams to ensure data products align with business priorities.
  • Manage complex analytics projects from concept through implementation while maintaining strong stakeholder communication.
  • Leverage AI tools and techniques to enhance development workflows, documentation quality, and analytics engineering efficiency.
  • Stay current with emerging technologies and industry trends to continuously improve data capabilities.

Requirements

The ideal candidate is an experienced analytics engineering professional with strong technical expertise, a passion for high-quality data, and the ability to collaborate effectively in a distributed environment.

  • 5+ years of relevant industry experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, business intelligence, or related fields.
  • Expert-level SQL skills, including experience with advanced data modeling, query optimization, and analytical transformations.
  • Strong experience with data warehousing concepts, architecture, and best practices.
  • 3+ years of experience working with Python and dbt for data transformation and analytics development.
  • Hands-on experience building semantic layers, metrics layers, and self-service analytics solutions.
  • Proven ability to design and deliver scalable data solutions that support business growth.
  • Experience managing complex data projects independently from planning through delivery.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and troubleshooting abilities.
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
  • Experience working effectively in remote, collaborative environments.
  • Familiarity with source code management tools such as Git or Bitbucket.
  • Experience working with cloud platforms such as AWS, GCP, or Azure.
  • Experience handling semi-structured data formats such as JSON.
  • Experience with business intelligence tools and analytics solutions.
  • Knowledge of machine learning analytics engineering or marketing analytics is a plus.

Benefits

  • Opportunity to work in a remote-first SaaS environment focused on building scalable solutions for enterprise customers.
  • Meaningful, high-impact work influencing product strategy, customer success, and company-wide decision-making.
  • Company equity participation, providing employees with ownership in long-term growth.
  • 25 days of paid time off annually, increasing to 30 days after five years of employment.
  • Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance coverage.
  • Additional protection through life insurance, critical illness coverage, accidental death and dismemberment insurance, and long-term disability coverage.
  • Annual wellness allowance to support fitness, sports, and outdoor activities.
  • Paid volunteering days to support community initiatives.
  • Opportunities for continuous learning, professional development, and career growth.
  • Flexible remote work environment designed to support collaboration and autonomy.

Title: Executive Assistant
Description:
Executive Assistant to a Vice President




Salary range is 78,000-85,000, terrific bonus, robust pension plan full and extensive immediate benefit plan, 3 weeks of vacation, and employee perks!




This is a hybrid role, and you will be required to work in the office TWO days per week and the office is downtown Toronto and a short walk from Union Station.




The OPPORTUNITY: Our client is a large organization that has a long, stable history and it prides itself on being a great place to work as well as having diverse partnerships within the community. They are seeking an Executive Assistant to support a busy and dynamic VP. In this role you will be responsible for assisting the VP with high-level administrative support. The successful candidate will possess drive, initiative and a passion to contribute to the success of the Vice President and to the success of organization! If you are an individual who enjoys working in a collaborative environment as well as for an organization that makes a significant and meaningful difference in the lives of many, then we want to hear from you!




Executive Assistant duties and responsibilities:

Provides administrative support to a Vice President including complex calendar management, managing many changing priorities, managing/arranging internal and external meetings etc.
Arranges travel, flights, hotels, car service and provides a detailed itinerary. Prepares expenses in an accurate and timely manner.
Supports the VP by managing a busy inbox, flagging urgent requests.
Prepares presentations and a variety of correspondence.
Proactively follows up on action items/deadlines and deliverables.
Represents the VP in a professional, customer-focused manner, serves as the main point of contact for internal and external stakeholders.
Arrange and handle requests for meetings, speaking engagements, media interviews, conferences, etc., recognizing sensitivity and confidentiality in relation to contacts, importance of attention, availability, etc.
Manage the administrative responsibilities associated with the Vice President’s membership on external boards, committees and agencies, including coordinating schedules with external and internal groups.
Plan and organize staff engagement events.
Coordinate and participate with the Vice President in setting priorities, scheduling work projects, advising on the status of work, target dates, scheduling meetings and the scheduling impact of new or pending projects/initiatives.
Handles confidential and sensitive documents as required.
Develop relationships with other Executive Assistants, clients, strategic partners, and third-party providers to support business objectives.
Maintains contact and distribution lists as well as other documents related to the Board and Committees.




Qualifications:

A minimum of 5+ years of recent relevant experience supporting a Senior Executive.
Excellent organizational, prioritization, and has superb time management skills.
Superior writing, proofreading, editing and formatting skills.
Highly proficient in the use of MS Office Suite programs with advanced Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and Outlook.
Experience managing multiple tasks/activities and highly proficient in setting and communicating priorities and timelines, handles scheduling conflicts with diplomacy.
Highly detail oriented.
Demonstrated ability to maintain confidentiality, integrity, professionalism, and diplomacy at all times and comfortable speaking with external clients/stakeholders.
Strong interpersonal/communication skills to interact respectfully and effectively with diverse individuals.
Collaborative, self-motivated and resourceful combined with a solutions oriented mind-set.
Demonstrated sound judgement and a solutions-oriented mind-set.
A university degree or college diploma is preferred.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Azure Data Engineer

Toronto, ON

Contract , Hybrid Opportunity

Note: Hands on Experience with Azure cloud required only, 7+ Years candidate will consider




* Design and develop ETL pipelines using Azure Databricks and Delta Lake, focusing on batch processing (autoloader) and Spark structured streaming.

* Create and manage end-to-end environments, including catalogs, schemas, tables, materialized views, functions, and volumes using Unity Catalog.

* Implement slowly changing dimensions (SCD1 and SCD2) on dimension tables and build change data capture (CDC) pipelines.

* Utilize Lakehouse federation to create foreign catalogs for accessing data from external sources.

* Optimize data processing through effective partitioning and liquid clustering in Databricks.

* Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure data governance and security practices are adhered to.

* Participate in CI/CD pipeline development and DevOps practices to enhance deployment efficiency.

Mandatory Skills

* Expertise in Azure Databricks

* Expert-level proficiency in SQL

* Regular experience with CI/CD practices

Preferred Skills

* Knowledge of data warehousing concepts

* Experience with data modeling and performance tuning in Spark

* Familiarity with data governance and security practices

Qualifications

* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.

* 10+ years of experience in Data Engineering projects is required.

Title: Analyst, Finance (12-Month Contract)
Description:
Job ID: 291795

Job Type: Full Time, 12-Month Contract

Function: Finance

Address: 160 Bloor Street East, 11th Floor, Toronto, ON

Working Model: Hybrid

Vacancy Status: This position is for an existing, open vacancy

Belong to Something Beautiful

At Sephora Canada, beauty is about feeling seen, valued, and empowered, individually and collectively. It is connecting deeply with others, celebrating diversity and inclusivity, unlocking your potential and making a difference every day. Together, we belong to something beautiful.

Ready for a career glow-up? As Financial Analyst, you’ll be supporting financial forecasting, budgeting, business planning, reporting, and ad hoc analysis. The work you do will impact beauty, as you deliver accurate financial insights that guide our retail, online, and omni business decisions. You’ll be part of a team that’s united in beauty, supported by those who are equally as passionate about driving strong financial planning and analysis at Sephora Canada.

What You’ll Do


Support financial processes, including budgeting, forecasting, month-end close, and reporting
Ensure ongoing forecasts incorporate trends and relevant information
Prepare monthly and quarterly financial reports, including variance analysis and commentary
Prepare analysis and reporting that include both financial and non-financial content, including recurring financial results, forecast presentations, and ad-hoc business cases
Support the monthly close process including preparation of variance reports and analysis with a high degree of accuracy and timeliness
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather financial data and understand business operations
Support ad-hoc projects and financial analysis and reporting requests as needed


What You’ll Bring


Bachelor’s degree in business or finance, with knowledge of accounting principles and financial statements
1-2 years of related financial planning and analysis (FP&A) or financial reporting/accounting experience; Retail or CPG industry experience preferred
Experience in supporting a full budget cycle, from establishing targets/goals to socialization, to business partners
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills with ability to build relationships across the organization including non-finance personnel
Strong prioritization skills; proven ability to be successful in a fast-paced and rapidly changing work environment
Demonstrated success in critical thinking and decision making, understanding when to seek assistance/guidance
Proactive, highly motivated, and innovative professional who is not afraid to challenge the status quo
Experience with Excel modelling and comfort with financial systems, preferably SAP Business Objects
You have comfort using AI tools and technologies to enhance day-to-day productivity


#FSCHIRING

What You’ll Get


Caring Community. You’ll collaborate with teammates who are equally passionate about innovating, doing the right things, and driving the industry forward – together, united in beauty.
Fulfilling Path. Your career glow-up starts here with access to opportunities that will challenge, stretch, and develop your skills. You’ll have access to the global community of luxury group LVMH (Louis Vuitton Moët Hennessy) for endless career opportunities.
Meaningful Work. As you make an impact on beauty, you’ll feel and see the positive change (consumer, industry, and social) your individual voice is a part of.


Rewards As Unique As You Are

Eligibility requirements apply to certain benefits and may vary depending on job classification and length of employment.


Wealth. We’re passionate about pay equity and transparency. The compensation ranges between CAD$66,500.00 - CAD$78,200.00. The final pay offered will depend on various factors, including but not limited to the actual qualifications and experience of the individual, and any additional non-discriminatory, legitimate business factors relevant to the position and/or location.
Health. Choose a healthcare plan to fit you and your family’s needs with medical, dental, vision, life, and disability coverage. Access to our Well-Being@Sephora program to support your mental, financial, physical, and social well-being.
Balance. You’ll be trusted to find the perfect blend of work/life balance that actually works for you with a hybrid work schedule, vacation and flex days, and maternity/paternity leave.
Growth. A career glow-up is built into every role, with access to training and development, as well as tuition reimbursement.
Perks. Think you’ve tried it all? Enjoy a 30% discount on all merchandise/services, opportunities for gratis, and flash sale discounts on LVMH brands.
Support. Tap into volunteer and donation matching, Employee Assistance Program, and free mental health resources with 24/7 access to Dialogue.


Join us and Belong to Something Beautiful.

Sephora Canada strongly believes in equal opportunity when it comes to employment and advancement. Our goal is to be inclusive, diverse, and representative of the communities where we work while creating an environment where every person can belong, grow, and build a beautiful career. This commitment applies to all candidates and employees regardless of race, ethnicity, citizenship, creed, place of origin, religion, sex, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, family status, marital status, disability, age, and/or any other diversity dimensions. Requests for accommodation due to a disability (visible or otherwise), other protected characteristics or other reasonable circumstances can be made at any stage of the recruitment process and during employment by contacting our People team.

As part of our commitment to transparency and efficiency, we would like to inform you that we utilize Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies in our recruitment process to assist in the initial screening and evaluation of applications.

Title: Marketing Analyst (CM)
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Marketing Analyst (CM) based in Canada.

This role offers the opportunity to transform marketing data into actionable insights that improve campaign performance and business growth. You will analyze advertising initiatives, evaluate experiments, and develop recommendations that enhance customer acquisition strategies. Working with marketing, analytics, and business teams, you will help optimize reporting processes, attribution models, and budget management tools. The position combines analytical expertise, statistical thinking, and marketing knowledge to support data-driven decision-making. You will leverage modern analytics platforms and reporting tools to uncover opportunities and improve performance across global campaigns. This is an exciting opportunity for a detail-oriented analyst who enjoys solving complex problems and making measurable business impact in a remote environment.

Accountabilities


Prepare analytical reports and performance summaries for advertising campaigns to identify optimization opportunities.
Research campaign performance trends and develop actionable recommendations to improve key marketing performance indicators.
Evaluate, monitor, and analyze A/B testing initiatives, providing accurate results and recommendations for future experiments.
Track daily marketing and product performance metrics, identify significant deviations, and investigate the reasons behind performance changes.
Analyze and improve attribution models, lifetime value (LTV) models, and marketing measurement approaches.
Optimize marketing reports, dashboards, and budgeting tools to improve efficiency and decision-making.
Collaborate with marketing and analytics teams to translate data insights into strategic recommendations.
Support continuous improvement of campaign measurement processes and reporting frameworks.
Use data-driven approaches to identify opportunities for improving acquisition, retention, and overall marketing effectiveness.


Requirements


Bachelor's degree in a technical, mathematical, economic, analytical, or related field.
Strong proficiency in Google Sheets, including advanced functions and pivot tables.
Good knowledge of BigQuery and experience working with large datasets.
Strong understanding of mathematical statistics and its application in marketing analytics.
Experience analyzing marketing campaigns, performance metrics, and business data.
Familiarity with BI tools such as Tableau, Apache Superset, or similar platforms.
English proficiency at B2 level or higher.
Ability to analyze complex data, identify patterns, and communicate actionable insights clearly.
Experience working with MS SQL is a plus.
Basic knowledge of Python is an advantage.
Familiarity with web analytics tools such as Google Analytics and Google Tag Manager is preferred.
Experience with Google Ads platforms and campaign analytics is a plus.
Availability to collaborate with international teams, preferably with at least 3 hours of overlap with team working hours.


Benefits


Fully remote full-time opportunity with flexibility to work from anywhere.
28 calendar days of annual vacation.
7 additional wellness days per year.
Employee referral bonuses of up to $5,000.
50% coverage for professional training, international conferences, and industry events.
Corporate discounts for English language learning.
Health benefit support, including reimbursement options for eligible employees.
Workplace setup support, including equipment and coworking or home office expense reimbursement.
Internal recognition program with rewards, merchandise, team activities, and wellness benefits.
Opportunity to work with international teams and develop expertise in global marketing analytics.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

The Driver Earnings team at Lyft is responsible for managing our suite of driver earnings products, driving top business goals around financial metrics and marketplace performance. The team develops and improves automation & execution of driver earnings products, develops new earnings products, and manages financial performance of these products to manage the growth of the business.

We are looking for an analyst to join the team that is responsible for working with cross-functional stakeholders to analyze and improve existing earnings products and manage the products’ financial outcomes within the broader Marketplace organization. You will influence existing and future strategy with your data analysis, insights, and storytelling to deliver strategic recommendations on experimentation and product improvements. We’re looking for a data & process-driven individual who has extraordinary attention to detail and a track record of analytical problem-solving.

Responsibilities:


Support marketplace operations to provide world-class analysis, product recommendations, and reporting to stakeholders.
Become an expert in marketplace trends and support weekly reporting structures for the Rideshare organization.
Develop domain-specific subject matter expertise in our lever portfolio and support cross-functional initiatives to improve existing products and develop new ones.
Analyze marketplace product & financial levers and provide cross-functional teams with actionable insights on improvements and problem areas.
Operate within Rideshare decision-making frameworks with a critical lens, evaluating areas of improvement and business growth opportunities.
Prepare regular business reviews for leadership, highlighting key areas of focus.
Work with stakeholders and other teams to track, report, and reconcile budget allocation across marketplace programs.
Analyze data, conduct research and synthesize feedback into plans, processes and playbooks.


Experience:


1-3 years of experience in a high growth environment in data and/or financial analytics.
Very strong attention to detail, experience developing processes to remove human error.
Proficient in SQL and quantitative analysis, you can deep dive into large amounts of data, draw meaningful insights, dissect business issues and draw actionable conclusions.
Outcome-driven approach to developing recommendations and demonstrated ability to develop a story and execution plan to support our goals and objectives.
Exceptional communication (listening, written, and oral) skills with the ability to present findings and recommendations targeted to the audience in question.
A drive to understand the core issues along with the determination to uncover the solution.
Excels at dealing with ambiguity and change management.


Benefits:


Extended health and dental coverage options, along with life insurance and disability benefits
Mental health benefits
Family building benefits
Child care and pet benefits
Access to a Lyft funded Health Care Savings Account
RRSP plan with company match to help save for your future
In addition to provincial observed holidays, salaried team members are covered under Lyft's flexible paid time off policy. The policy allows team members to take off as much time as they need (with manager approval). Hourly team members get 15 days paid time off, with an additional day for each year of service
Lyft is proud to support new parents with 18 weeks of paid time off, designed as a top-up plan to complement provincial programs. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible.
Subsidized commuter benefits and Lyft ride credits


Lyft is committed to creating an inclusive workforce that fosters belonging. Lyft believes that every person has a right to equal employment opportunities without discrimination because of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, pardoned record of offences, or any other basis protected by applicable law or by Company policy. Lyft also strives for a healthy and safe workplace and strictly prohibits harassment of any kind. Accommodation for persons with disabilities will be provided upon request in accordance with applicable law during the application and hiring process. Please contact your recruiter if you wish to make such a request.

Lyft highly values having employees working in-office to foster a collaborative work environment and company culture. This role will be in-office on a hybrid schedule — Team Members will be expected to work in the office at least 3 days per week, including on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays. Lyft considers working in the office at least 3 days per week to be an essential function of this hybrid role. Your recruiter can share more information about the various in-office perks Lyft offers. Additionally, hybrid roles have the flexibility to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per year. #Hybrid

The expected base pay range for this position in the Toronto area is $83,800 CAD - $104,750 CAD, not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits. Salary ranges are dependent on a variety of factors, including qualifications, experience and geographic location. Your recruiter can share more information about the salary range specific to your working location and other factors during the hiring process.

Lyft may use artificial intelligence to screen applicants, however, Lyft employees make the ultimate selection and hiring decisions.

This is a new position.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
About the Role:




Global Furniture Group is a major office furniture manufacturer headquartered in the Toronto area. Our Data and Analytics team turns operational data from across the business into reporting and insight that supports sales, finance, and operations.




We are looking for a Junior Data Analyst to gather requirements, design, develop, and maintain reporting solutions, and to help the business make decisions grounded in accurate, well structured data. Beyond building reports, you will be expected to understand the data well enough to spot issues, answer questions, and improve how we use our information.




Key Responsibilities:




Develop and maintain dashboards and reports, and perform data and process analysis as required.
Define requirements with stakeholders, then build models and analyses that support data driven decisions and recommendations.
Connect data from multiple sources to uncover insight that informs critical business decisions.
Partner with the IT team to establish and maintain the infrastructure that advances our business intelligence efforts.
Interpret data and analyze results using statistical techniques.
Acquire data from primary and secondary sources and maintain the underlying databases.
Review reports, extracts, and performance indicators to filter and clean data and confirm accuracy.
Work with management to prioritize business and information needs.
Other duties as assigned.




Required Qualifications:




Bachelor degree or diploma in analytics, computer science, statistics, business, or a related field.
Working knowledge of data models, database design, data mining, and segmentation techniques.
Experience with reporting tools, databases, and at least one programming or scripting language.
Strong analytical skills, with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and present large amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy.
Strong Excel skills.
Basic SQL, with some VBA an asset.
Foundational statistics, including standard deviation, correlation, and distributions.
Clear written and verbal communication, with the ability to explain findings to non technical audiences.




Preferred Qualifications:




Python for data processing and automation
Experience with Pyramid Analytics, or a comparable business intelligence platform such as Power BI or Tableau
Exposure to IBM AS400 or DB2, or a similar ERP data environment
Familiarity with CRM data, for example OnContact
Prior experience supporting finance or sales reporting




What Success Looks Like:




In the first few months you will become comfortable with our reporting environment, take ownership of recurring report cycles, and begin proposing improvements that save time and increase accuracy. Over time you will grow into a trusted analyst the business relies on for clear, accurate, and timely information, with room to deepen your skills in Python automation and Pyramid Analytics as the team and our capabilities grow.




This role requires a range of duties, including but not limited to this job description.




We thank all candidates for their interest, however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted. Valuing diversity, equity and inclusion in the workplace and supporting the principles of Canada's Employment Equity Act. Global Furniture Group values employment equity and is an equal opportunity employer. We will accommodate the needs of applicants with disabilities during the recruitment process. Those requiring accommodation should advise Human Resources and Global will provide suitable accommodation in a manner that considers the applicant’s accessibility needs due to disability. Global Furniture Group offers a competitive salary, benefits package and opportunities for professional growth and development. If you're ready to join a team that is committed to delivering the best products and services to our customers, please apply now.

Title: Business & Data Analyst
Description:
Location: Toronto-661 University

Department: Planning, Reporting & Compliance

This posting is to fill a current vacancy.




The Role

To provide advanced data analytics, validation, and visualization support to Finance-led special projects. This role focuses on transforming clinical, operational, and financial datasets into clear, actionable insights to support executive decision-making, strategic planning, and operational improvement initiatives.




Key Responsibilities-

Data Integration & Management: Extract, integrate, and structure complex clinical, operational, and financial datasets from multiple systems.
Data Validation & Governance: Lead data quality assessment, validation, and reconciliation to ensure reliable inputs for executive reporting.
Advanced Analysis & Insight Generation: Independently analyze data to identify trends, drivers, and opportunities related to cost, performance, and operational efficiency.
Executive Visualization & Storytelling: Develop dashboards and visual outputs (e.g., Power BI) and translate insights into clear, actionable recommendations for senior leadership.
Special Projects Delivery: Support priority initiatives (e.g., cost savings, process optimization) through targeted data analysis.
Process & Reporting Improvement: Identify opportunities to automate, standardize, and improve data flows and reporting processes.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with Finance, Laboratory Operations, and Digital & Data teams to define requirements and deliver insights aligned to organizational priorities.




Knowledge And Skills-

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with ability to work through complex and ambiguous datasets.
Ability to translate clinical and operational data into financial and strategic insights.
Strong communication skills with ability to present insights to non-technical and executive audiences.
High attention to detail and data integrity.
Oral communication and interpersonal skills, to provide technical advice and guidance to peers and liaise with all levels of internal and external stakeholders/contacts.
Written communication skills to prepare commentary on analyses develop and deliver presentations, training documents and procedural guidelines.




Education And Experience-

Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Health Informatics, Finance, Statistics, or a related field.
5–7 years of experience in data analysis, preferably within healthcare, public sector, or complex operational environments.
Advanced Excel including data manipulation, Power Query, and data modeling is required.
Experience with Power BI or similar visualization tools is preferred.
Working knowledge of SQL is considered an asset.




Attributes and Competencies-

Works independently to structure analyses, validate data, and develop insights to support decision-making.
Exercises judgment in interpreting data, identifying risks and issues, and recommending solutions to support Finance and operational leadership.




Duration: Permanent

Hours of Work: Full time, 36.25 hours per week

Compensation Group: Ontario Public Service Employees Union

Compensation Range :$47.93 - $59.26

Posting Date: 07-13-2026

Closing Date: 07-23-2026




Please note: applications will be received no later than 11:59pm on the date preceding the closing date as indicated on the Job Requisition.

Note: Internal candidates will be considered first.

While we thank all applicants for their interest, only those selected to move forward in the recruitment process will be contacted. Any information obtained during the course of recruitment will be used for employment recruitment purposes only, and not for any other purpose.

PHO is committed to ensuring equity in employment. Our goal is to create a diverse, inclusive workforce that reflects the communities we serve and to ensure our services and communications are accessible to all individuals. Any candidate who requires a job posting in an alternative format may email a request to HR_Inquiries@oahpp.ca. Once an applicant has been selected for an interview, they can inform PHO about any accommodations they may require at any stage of the interview process.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Serving the needs of all families with young children, Carter’s Inc. is the largest North American apparel retailer exclusively for babies and young children, encompassing Carter’s, OshKosh B’gosh, Skip*Hop and Little Planet brands. Meaningful work, constant learning, genuine people, and a community guided by core values that promote inclusion and innovation is in everything we do. There are many reasons to build your career at Carter's.

How You’ll Make An Impact

We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Data Analyst to support better and efficient decision making for the business. This role requires a combination of business acumen, technical skills, and the ability to translate data into actionable insights. The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in building and maintaining complex dashboards using BI tools used by the company and working with large retail datasets. This role will partner closely with business leaders in Canada as pricing, assortment, planning, and allocation capabilities are implemented and improved.

Planning Operations and Reporting (80%)


Design, develop and maintain reporting and act as a Tableau expert to create complex dashboards, standardized reports and self-service reporting solutions.
Perform data validation, quality assurance, and troubleshooting to ensure accuracy and consistency across reports.
Write and maintain SQL queries for advanced data analysis and data manipulation when needed.
Provide ad hoc reporting to the Planning & Allocation team as needed.
Supports teams with training on Tableau (or any other BI Tool adopted for reporting) and training material.


Relationship Management and Collaboration (20%)


Develop a strong collaboration with the corporate BI team for any added support.
Partners with cross-functional project leads and monitor project timeline at times in partnerships with different functions.
Support Retail senior leadership team with data, insights, and presentation materials on future capabilities and project status updates when required for larger projects, work closely with outside parties to implement solutions.


WE’D LOVE TO HEAR FROM YOU IF: Must have:


2-3 Years of Experience in a Data Analyst role.
Advanced knowledge and proficiency in Tableau.
Strong SQL skills for querying large data sets.
Strong communication and collaboration skills


Preferred Skills And Experience


Degree or Diploma in Data Analytics or related field or equivalent work experience.
Exposure to Retail Planning, KPIs and fundamentals.


Our Team Members


Lead Courageously: Have a strong sense of personal values that align with our Company values
Collaborates Broadly: Build cooperation, trust, and thrive in a consensus driven environment
Customer Focus: Proactively seek opportunities to leverage data and fact-based insights to serve customers and/or internal clients
Drive Growth: Set aggressive goals and implement plans precisely
Cultivates Innovation: Respectfully challenge the “we’ve always done it this way” mentality and explore new ways to achieve desired outcomes


Make a Career At Carter’s


Career Development: Success starts from within, and we have several paths from which you can choose to


enhance your career evolution. From Carter’s University to Toastmasters to mentorship programs and more,

we encourage you to utilize these tools to elevate your professional prowess.

NOTE: This job description is not intended to be all-inclusive. The duties described may be changed or reassigned at the discretion of management, and the employee may be required to perform duties that are not listed in the

Job Description.

Carter’s | OshKosh is an equal opportunity employer and encourages all interested and qualified candidates to apply. Should you require any type of accommodation, please do not hesitate to contact us.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst (Expert)

Location: Toronto, ON

Work Model: Hybrid (1 day per week in office)

Duration: 15-Week Contract

Start Date: August 2026

Hours: 37.5 hours per week

Pay Range: $64 - $77/hr







Our client, a large enterprise organization, is seeking an experienced Data Analyst to support a major business transformation initiative. This role will focus on data validation, analysis, system testing, and issue investigation within a complex enterprise environment

.The successful candidate will work closely with project teams to ensure data accuracy, support testing activities, analyze reporting outputs, and identify the root causes of data-related issues during a critical technology transition




Responsibilities

Analyze and validate data across multiple systems and data sources
Support testing activities to ensure data accuracy, completeness, and consistency
Investigate data discrepancies and identify root causes of issues
Review business and technical documentation to understand data flows and relationships
Develop and execute SQL queries to validate data and reporting outputs
Partner with project teams to resolve data quality and reporting issues
Document findings, recommendations, and resolution activities
Participate in project meetings and provide insights on data-related observations and risks

8+ years of experience in a Data Analyst role

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Publicis is an omni-channel communications agency with over 600 employees across our Canadian operations. The office is the largest in our industry in Canada and boasts talent across various disciplines of marketing and advertising expertise. Publicis carries a balanced split of Canadian, U.S. and Global clients for which we are Agency of Record. Publicis supports a range of D&I actions through our Driving Change team as well as giving back to the community through a long-standing participation in the Out of the Cold Program. With a strong, active and familial culture, Pub United is the agency’s social club, hosting events as wide reaching as Curling, Trivia Nights and more.




Overview




We’re looking for a Junior Data Analyst to join our team and help bring data to life across our clients’ digital experiences. In this role, you won’t just report numbers - you’ll interrogate them, asking why a metric moved, what it means for the business, and what we should do about it.




You’ll support the implementation and optimization of web analytics solutions using Google Analytics 4 (GA4), turning data into insights that drive smarter decisions. You’ll monitor performance, uncover trends, and deliver meaningful recommendations on both a regular and ad-hoc basis. As you do so, you’ll ensure your data is accurate and reliable, while taking a strategic approach to uncover opportunities that drive the business forward.




Responsibilities




Provide technical expertise on Google Analytics 4 (GA4), Google Tag Manager (GTM), and other relevant digital analytics platforms.
Design and implement detailed tracking and tagging plans, partnering with development teams to ensure accurate and comprehensive tracking of user behaviour across client web properties.
Contribute to a range of analytics initiatives; including report automation, analytics tagging, data visualization, requirements gathering and documentation, and ad hoc analysis, proactively surfacing the “so what” behind the data
Assist in developing KPIs and measurement frameworks aligned to business objectives, asking the right questions during requirements gathering to ensure we’re measuring what matters
Identify opportunities to improve how we collect, integrate, and analyze data
Generate routine reporting, scorecards, and interactive dashboards (including weekly, monthly, quarterly and ad-hoc requests), while actively identifying & sharing key insights from the data
Collaborate cross-functionally with various teams across the organization and contribute to client-facing communication that builds strong, trusted relationships




Qualifications




About You




You’re curious by nature and excited about using data to improve experiences, performance and decision-making. You enjoy digging into data, asking questions, and finding insights that make an impact.




This might be the right fit if you:




Find yourself asking “but why?” when others accept the surface-level answer
Feel uncomfortable presenting data without having a point of view on what it means
Get a thrill from untangling confusing datasets or spotting new trends




You’ll succeed in this role if you:




Have exceptional time management skills & can juggle multiple priorities effectively
Are proactive, hard-working, and excited by digital possibilities, finding smart solutions to deliver results
Set high quality standards for yourself and care about getting the details right
Are accountable, and do what it takes to deliver on your commitments
Welcome feedback & continuously look for ways to grow




Qualifications:




1+year(s) of hands-on experience using Google Analytics 4
Basic understanding of HTML, CSS and JavaScript
Proficient in MS Excel for data analysis
Ability to design and build dashboards in Tableau, Looker Studio, PowerBI, or similar data visualization tools.
Strong written and verbal communication skills are essential. The ideal candidate should feel comfortable presenting and participating in group discussions involving both internal stakeholders and clients
Experience using Google Tag Manager and BigQuery is a plus




Additional information




Salary




Transparency matters to us. The salary range for this position is CAD $55,000 - CAD $65,000 per year. Actual compensation within this range will be based on a variety of factors, including relevant experience, knowledge, skills, and applicable certifications. This range reflects what we reasonably expect to offer based on current market data.




AI Use




We use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of our hiring process, such as reviewing applications or analyzing resumes. These tools assist our recruitment team but never replace human decision-making. We believe in a human-first approach, where your experience and potential are recognized by people.




Publicis Canada is committed to building a diverse workforce representative of our community. We encourage and are pleased to consider all qualified candidates, without regard to race, colour, citizenship, religion, sex, marital / family status, sexual orientation, gender identity, aboriginal status, age, disability or persons who may require an accommodation, to apply. If you require a specific accommodation please contact Human Resources.
