Title: Consultant, Analytics Data Engineer
Description:
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.

At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.

To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com

What You'll Be Doing

The Marketing Data Strategy & Governance team is a strategic Data Product delivery team within CIBC Personal and Business Banking, applying rigor in Data Engineering & Analytics with strong sense of business partnership, to drive impact for our clients and business outcomes. Reporting to the Director, Marketing Data Strategy, the Consultant, Analytics Data Engineer will lead the design, execution and maintenance of Client-Centric Data Products that support CIBC’s Marketing and Data modernization programs. As a motivated self-starter, your curiosity in data, deep understanding of the business priorities, pared with expertise in cloud-native data engineering principles, you will be an integral part in delivering innovative data solutions and data assets that bring business value. You will practice effective development with Agile methodology, DevOps, CI/CD and governance built-in to the delivery on Databricks Analytics Platform. You are able to collaborate closely with data product owners, Data Scientists, PBB Analytics, and various technology and program partners.

At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. You’ll have the flexibility to manage your work activities within a hybrid work arrangement where you’ll spend 1-3 days per week on-site, while other days will be remote.

How You'll Succeed


Analytics savvy - Contribute to the ongoing development and enhancement of analytics, business case and visual analytic tools that leverage industry best practices. Recommend improvements to overall analytical approach for the team through interaction with internal stakeholders and through industry research.
You bring a unique data career – Experienced in the field of Client Analytics, Data Engineering, Statistical analysis, data modeling, ETL and Data Flow design, or Data Sciences, combining expertise from multiple domains to understand how best to solve business problems with data.
You are an entrepreneur – You carve out your own path by practicing independent and critical thinking, conducting business and technical problem assessment to guide the solution design. You pay attention to what matters and delivers best in-class working solution with sense of accountability.
You are a team player – You know that collaboration means more than a race, you are able to work in a team setting and understand the impact of your actions to others. You practice open and mindful communication, respecting diverse opinions and perspectives.


Who You Are


You practice industry best-practice in Data Engineering & Analytics. At least 2-3 years’ experience in developing enterprise-grade Data Products and data pipelines, leveraging distributed Analytics Platforms such as Databricks, PySpark, Spark Dataframe API, Scala, Azure Data Factory, practicing CI/CD and DevOps in Agile setting. Experience in Databricks AI/BI, PowerBI would be a bonus.
You give meaning to data. You enjoy investigating complex problems, and making sense of information. You're confident in your ability to communicate detailed information in an impactful way.
You understand that success is in the details. You notice things that others don't. You ask the right questions. Your critical thinking skills help to inform your decision making.
You act like an owner. You thrive when you're empowered to take initiative, go above and beyond, and deliver results.
You embrace and champion change. You'll continuously evolve your thinking and the way you work in order to deliver your best.
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values - trust, teamwork, and accountability.


What CIBC Offers

At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential. We aspire to give you a career, rather than just a paycheck.


We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive salary, incentive pay, banking benefits, a benefits program*, defined benefit pension plan*, an employee share purchase plan, a vacation offering, wellbeing support, and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
We cultivate a culture where you can express your ambition through initiatives like Purpose Day; a paid day off dedicated for you to use to invest in your growth and development.
Subject to plan and program terms and conditions


What You Need To Know


CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com
CIBC is committed to clarity in our hiring process. All roles posted are opportunities we’re actively recruiting for, unless stated otherwise.
You need to be legally eligible to work at the location(s) specified above and, where applicable, must have a valid work or study permit.
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills test (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency).
We use artificial intelligence tools during the recruitment process. Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.

Title: Data Platform Risk and Operations Analyst
HTML:
Requisition ID: 250546

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Data Platform Risk & Operations Analyst will support the Enterprise Data & Artificial Intelligence, Platform Data & Operations team, by providing risk analysis, documentation, reporting & data management support. This role combines technical expertise in data visualization and dashboard management with strong risk management acumen to deliver actionable insights and help reduce risk across Scotiabank’s legacy and emerging data and AI capabilities. Expertise in data platforms, Cloud, security and operations is considered an asset.

The role reports to the Senior Manager, Operations, Data Platform & Operations.

Is this role right for you? In this role, you will:

  • Contribute to the design of the overall risk and issues management framework, delivery/operating model and related processes in compliance with the Bank’s broader Enterprise standards;
  • Supports periodic effectiveness reviews and contributes to continuous improvement of the team’s internal processes;
  • Working with the Senior Manager and other relevant stakeholders, develop actionable project plans to address data/platform risks and issues within required timelines;
  • Manage the team’s internal and/or external risk & issues management documentation, reporting, tracking, and dashboards, providing insights and recommendations to the Senior Manager as well as ensuring accuracy in metrics, KPIs and KRIs;
  • Prepare and deliver weekly and monthly updates to various forums and stakeholders;
  • Manages preparation and writing of audit related documentation, required evidence, and closure packages and coordinates responses/communication with relevant stakeholders;
  • Maintain awareness of data security, compliance and platform concepts to support ongoing reporting and risk management needs;
  • Ensure adherence to data reporting standards and methodologies;
  • Uphold integrity and compliance by reporting any practices that may compromise the organization as well as execute responsibilities with efficiency, productivity, and adherence to institutional policies and regulations.

Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? We'd love to work with you if you have:

  • Minimum five (5) years’ experience working in a technology risk/audit, data and data platforms, technology or banking environment with various operational, regulatory and technical risks and controls;
  • University degree in Business, Data Analytics, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience;
  • Proven experience working with large, matrix organization with various stakeholders, priorities and constraints.
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills, with ability to effectively communicate with both technical and non-technical audiences at various levels within the organization;
  • Ability to evaluate the design effectiveness, operating effectiveness and sustainability of various operational and/or technical controls developed and implemented by the data platform teams.
  • Strong interpersonal and negotiation skills to ensure alignment and buy-in from stakeholders;
  • Strong technical reporting and data visualization skills in PowerBI, Excel with exceptional attention to detail especially in reporting, metrics and data;
  • Significant experience with Enterprise project management and communication tools (e.g., JIRA, Confluence, SharePoint);
  • Proven ability to understand regulatory, legal and risk concepts and convert them into business requirements;
  • Understanding of banking products/services, operations, systems/infrastructure, policies & processes, controls and organization is preferred;
  • Experience in Enterprise data platform engineering and/or information security and controls is an advantage.

What's in it for you?

  • Diversity, Equity, Inclusion & Allyship - We strive to create an inclusive culture where every employee is empowered to reach their fullest potential, respected for who they are, and are embraced through bias-free practices and inclusive values across Scotiabank. We embrace diversity and provide opportunities for all employee to learn, grow & participate through our various Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) that span across diverse gender identities, ethnicity, race, age, ability & veterans.
  • Accessibility and Workplace Accommodations - We value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Scotiabank continues to locate, remove and prevent barriers so that we can build a diverse and inclusive environment while meeting accessibility requirements.
  • Upskilling through online courses, cross-functional development opportunities, and tuition assistance.
  • Competitive Rewards program including bonus, flexible vacation, personal, sick days and benefits will start on day one.
  • Dynamic Ecosystem - Free tea & coffee, universal washrooms, and lots of space for team collaboration.
  • Community Engagement - No matter where you choose to work from; we offer opportunities for community engagement & belonging with our various programs.

#AIDataPlatforms

Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. If you require technical assistance, please click here. Candidates must apply directly online to be considered for this role. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Operations Analyst
Description:
Expected Salary Range: $65941 - $82426 Annually

Target Variable Performance Pay: 6%

The salary range shown above reflects the expected compensation for this position. The final salary offered will be determined based on a holistic assessment of the candidate’s experience, education, training, and alignment with the role’s requirements. For positions covered by a collective agreement, placement within the range will follow the applicable step or classification.

The Operations Analyst supports business operations within Toronto Hydro's growing Streetlighting portfolio dedicated to city infrastructure upgrades. This role is responsible for data analysis, reporting, invoicing, vendor management, and budget tracking, including supporting budgeting and forecasting activities. Working closely with leaders, the Operations Analyst helps coordinate projects and supports day-to-day activities.

This posting is for an existing vacancy.

Key Responsibilities


Presents findings through technical and business reports, including process maps, graphs, charts, and tables
Supports Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems, data management, and reporting by maintaining accurate data and preparing regular reports
Maintains system documentation and supports improvements to databases and tools to enhance reporting and decision-making
Assists with project coordination and delivery of departmental initiatives
Supports financial activities, including budgeting, spend tracking, and forecasting
Supports vendor management activities, including coordinating with vendors (e.g., City of Toronto) and processing invoices
Conducts data analysis using large data sets, multiple variables, and cost-benefit analysis to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement


Requirements


Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Commerce, Engineering (Electrical preferred), or a related field
One (1) to Three (3) years of experience in engineering, business analytics, financial management or data management (co-op experience accepted)
Experience in a regulated industry and knowledge of applicable statutory and regulatory obligations is an asset
Experience managing and analyzing large datasets, and knowledge of programming languages (e.g., R, Python, VBA) is an asset
Experience supporting financial management activities, including budgeting, spend tracking, and forecasting
Experience coordinating projects and knowledge of project management principles
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access, Outlook)
Knowledge of Tableau, Alteryx and/or SAP
Strong business acumen
Strong problem solving, analytical and decision-making skills
Highly responsible and proactive, with a customer-focused mindset and a proven ability to build strong, collaborative relationships with stakeholders
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Proven ability to manage competing priorities and adapt in a fast-paced environment


TOTAL REWARDS

To support the overall well-being and success of our employees, Toronto Hydro provides a comprehensive total rewards package which includes:


100% employer paid Health & Dental benefits
24/7 access to Employee & Family Assistance Program and Virtual Healthcare
Company-paid life insurance and long term disability
Spending account to support fitness goals (e.g. gym memberships)
Competitive paid time off (including vacation and parental leave)
Defined Benefits Pension Plan through OMERS
Career development and tuition reimbursement
Exciting employee engagement and appreciation events throughout the year


This position is based in a hybrid environment allowing for remote work two days per week, based on business needs. Employees will be required to come onsite Tuesday to Thursday. You are expected to live in Ontario and within reasonable commuting distance of the office.

Toronto Hydro is committed to leveraging new technologies to support an efficient and inclusive recruitment process. As part of this, artificial intelligence (AI) systems may be used to assist in screening, assessing, or selecting applicants for this position. These tools are used in combination with human decision-making to ensure fairness and consistency.

ACCESSIBILITY AND APPLICATION SUPPORT

If you’re having difficulty using our online application system and require accommodation, please email hr_talent@torontohydro.com or call (416) 542-8000. Toronto Hydro is pleased to provide reasonable accommodation for qualified individuals throughout the job application process. Please note this email is for accommodation requests only. Resumes sent to this email address will not be considered. Learn more about our commitment to accessibility.

Description:

26WD99647, Senior Data Analyst

Position Overview 

Autodesk is seeking an experienced Senior Data Analyst to join the Strategic Data & Research team in our Go-To-Market Data Intelligence team. The GDI team is chartered with delivering accurate foundational data and customer intelligence to inform go-to-market (GTM) teams’ engagement across the customer lifecycle.

Data Analysts play a critical role in shaping GTM focus areas by uncovering insights, influencing business decisions, and partnering across functions to deliver customer intelligence used throughout the customer lifecycle.

We are open to remote candidates based in Canada.

Responsibilities 


Own analytics projects from problem definition through delivery, identifying opportunities to refine the scope as new insights emerge
Drive investigative analyses, using business context and data to determine the next questions worth answering rather than simply executing predefined requests
Collaborate with Program Managers to explore new project ideas or enhancements 
Translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical approaches that drive actionable decisions
Partner with Data Scientists to identify areas of improvement for Data Science models  
Work with Data Engineering and Business Intelligence teams to operationalize our customer intelligence and make it accessible to the business
Present findings and recommendations to stakeholders, influencing decisions through clear, actionable data storytelling
Build scalable reporting to measure progress in key initiatives 
Develop deep expertise in Autodesk’s customer and go-to-market data ecosystem
Work as an individual contributor with opportunities for leadership as we grow the team


Minimum Qualifications 


Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Data Science, or Business Analytics) or equivalent combination of education and relevant professional experience.
4+ years in an Analyst role 
Strong SQL coding skills and comfortable using it everyday 
Experience with Snowflake
Experience working in Python or R 
Familiarity with AI-assisted development tools (e.g., Claude Code or equivalent) to accelerate data work, automate repetitive tasks, and improve analytical productivity
Comfortable with presenting data and storytelling using a variety of tools, ranging from PowerPoint to Power BI to Python


Preferred Qualifications 


Familiarity with Salesforce & Salesforce data objects
Familiarity with Data Science concepts 
Experience working in Git 
Experience working with DBT
Experience with Looker


The Ideal Candidate


Tells compelling stories using data – you weave together a narrative of what is happening in the business based on your analysis to help drive business decisions
Communicates effectively with both technical stakeholders and non-technical stakeholders 
Demonstrates intellectual curiosity by challenging assumptions, asking follow-up questions, and uncovering insights beyond the original request
Independently manages multiple workstreams while proactively communicating progress, risks, tradeoffs, and recommendations to stakeholders
Has a strong attention to detail and care deeply about data quality 
Proactively reaches out to stakeholders to understand the business use case better
Enjoys collaborating with team members to drive impact 


26WD99647, Analyste de données senior

Aperçu du Poste 

Autodesk recherche un analyste de données senior expérimenté pour rejoindre l’équipe « Strategic Data & Research » au sein de notre équipe « Go-To-Market Data Intelligence » (GDI). L’équipe GDI a pour mission de fournir des données de base précises et des informations sur les clients afin d’étayer l’action des équipes de commercialisation (GTM) tout au long du cycle de vie du client.

Les analystes de données jouent un rôle essentiel dans la définition des axes prioritaires de la stratégie de commercialisation (GTM) en mettant en évidence des informations pertinentes, en influençant les décisions commerciales et en collaborant avec les différentes fonctions pour fournir des informations clients utilisées tout au long du cycle de vie du client.

Nous sommes ouverts aux candidatures à distance basées au Canada.

Responsabilités 


Gérer des projets d’analyse de A à Z, de la définition du problème à la livraison, en identifiant les opportunités d’affiner le périmètre à mesure que de nouvelles informations émergent
Mener des analyses exploratoires, en s’appuyant sur le contexte métier et les données pour déterminer les prochaines questions auxquelles il convient de répondre, plutôt que de se contenter d’exécuter des demandes prédéfinies
Collaborer avec les chefs de projet pour explorer de nouvelles idées de projets ou des améliorations
Traduire des questions métier ambiguës en approches analytiques structurées qui conduisent à des décisions exploitables
Collaborer avec les data scientists pour identifier les axes d’amélioration des modèles de science des données
Travailler avec les équipes d’ingénierie des données et de Business Intelligence pour opérationnaliser nos informations clients et les rendre accessibles à l’entreprise
Présenter les conclusions et les recommandations aux parties prenantes, en influençant les décisions grâce à une présentation claire et exploitable des données
Mettre en place un système de reporting évolutif pour mesurer les progrès des initiatives clés
Développer une expertise approfondie de l’écosystème de données clients et de stratégie de commercialisation d’Autodesk
Travailler en tant que collaborateur autonome avec des opportunités d’évoluer vers des fonctions de direction à mesure que l’équipe s’agrandit


Qualifications Minimales 


Licence dans un domaine quantitatif (par exemple, statistiques, mathématiques, informatique, économie, science des données ou analyse de données d’entreprise) ou combinaison équivalente de formation et d’expérience professionnelle pertinente.
Au moins 4 ans d’expérience dans un poste d’analyste 
Solides compétences en codage SQL et aisance dans son utilisation quotidienne
Expérience avec Snowflake
Expérience de travail avec Python ou R 
Maîtrise des outils de développement assistés par l’IA (par exemple, Claude Code ou équivalent) pour accélérer le traitement des données, automatiser les tâches répétitives et améliorer la productivité analytique
Aisance dans la présentation des données et la narration à l’aide de divers outils, allant de PowerPoint à Power BI en passant par Python


Qualifications Souhaitées


Connaissance de Salesforce et des objets de données Salesforce
Connaissance des concepts de science des données 
Expérience de travail avec Git
Expérience de travail avec DBT
Expérience avec Looker


Le Candidat Idéal


Raconte des histoires captivantes à partir des données – vous tissez un récit de ce qui se passe dans l’entreprise en vous appuyant sur vos analyses afin de contribuer à orienter les décisions stratégiques
Communique efficacement tant avec les parties prenantes techniques que non techniques
Fait preuve de curiosité intellectuelle en remettant en question les hypothèses, en posant des questions complémentaires et en mettant au jour des informations allant au-delà de la demande initiale
Gère de manière autonome plusieurs axes de travail tout en communiquant de manière proactive sur l’avancement, les risques, les compromis et les recommandations aux parties prenantes
Fait preuve d’un grand souci du détail et accorde une grande importance à la qualité des données
Prend proactivement contact avec les parties prenantes pour mieux comprendre le cas d’utilisation métier
Aime collaborer avec les membres de l’équipe pour générer un impact


Learn More

About Autodesk

Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made.

We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world.

When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us!

Salary transparency

Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. For Canada based roles, we expect a starting base salary between $83,000 and $122,100. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience and geographic location, and may exceed this range. In addition to base salaries, our compensation package may include annual cash bonuses, commissions for sales roles, stock grants, and a comprehensive benefits package.

Belonging

We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/global-belonging

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Our Client

A leading Toronto-based alternative investment firm is seeking a Senior Data Analyst to join its growing team. This is an exciting opportunity to play a foundational role in modernizing the firm's data infrastructure and helping build scalable, automated processes that support investment operations and financial reporting.




This is a full-time, in-office position located in downtown Toronto.




About the Opportunity

As the Senior Data Analyst, you will take ownership of portfolio and financial data integrity across the organization while helping establish best practices for data management and automation. This is a highly visible role with significant ownership and opportunities for long-term growth.




Key Responsibilities

Own and enhance automated reconciliation processes to improve accuracy and reduce manual effort.
Build and standardize automated data ingestion across portfolio positions, P&L, cash, margin, and fee data.
Develop cross-report validation processes to ensure consistency between investor reporting, internal financial reporting, and third-party data.
Create and maintain daily data health reports, tracking exceptions, outstanding issues, and resolutions.
Document workflows and create comprehensive process documentation.
Automate manual data collection and reconciliation activities using Python or similar technologies.
Support the expansion of reporting capabilities, including performance reporting, investor capital accounts, regulatory reporting, and trade cost analysis.
Oversee AI-enabled workflows and automation initiatives to improve operational efficiency.




Qualifications

2+ years of experience in fund operations, fund administration, prime brokerage, investment operations, or another data-intensive financial services environment.
Advanced Excel skills with experience managing and analyzing large datasets.
Strong process improvement mindset with exceptional attention to detail.
Excellent written communication skills and intellectual curiosity.
Experience with portfolio management systems (e.g., Geneva, Advent, Paladyne, or similar) is considered an asset.
Familiarity with prime broker reporting is an asset.




Technical Skills

Experience with some or all of the following is preferred:

Python for data automation and reconciliation
APIs and automated data feeds
AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, or Microsoft Copilot
SQL
Snowflake, Power BI, or similar data platforms

Title: Receptionist
Description:
Overview

Established in 1981, Pace Law Firm has dedicated itself to achieving positive changes in clients’ lives by effectively representing them in navigating the legal process while redefining the client experience. Today, Pace Law remains dedicated to upholding its signature “client-first” brand. With 150 legal professionals in three offices, our firm serves its clients in over 38 languages.

We are seeking a professional and welcoming Receptionist to join our team. This pivotal role serves as the first point of contact for our clients and visitors, setting the tone for professionalism and service that our firm is known for. The ideal candidate will be responsible for greeting guests, managing communication flow, and providing administrative support to ensure efficient operation of the office.

Duties and Responsibilities


Greet and welcome clients and guests, directing them appropriately.
Answer, screen, and forward incoming phone calls while providing basic information when needed.
Answer inquires for the general public.
Manage and monitor the reception area at all times.
Ensure that the reception area and all public areas are presentable and tidy at all times.
Schedule and confirm appointments, and maintain calendars.
Direct daily mail and deliveries to the mailroom.
Order office supplies and keep inventory of stock.
Assist in hospitality tasks for clients and meetings as needed.
Assist with various administrative tasks as needed, such as filing, photocopying, and faxing.


Qualifications


Experience in customer facing role.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.).
Multitasking and time-management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks.
Ability to be resourceful and proactive when issues arise.


Benefits


Extended Healthcare
Health Spending Account
On-site parking


You Can Find More About Our Benefits Website.

Pace Law firm is an equal opportunity employer and encourages applications from all qualified candidates. If you require any accommodation during the application process, please contact HR at Hr@pacelawfirm.com.

Title: Senior Application Support Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Senior Application Support Analyst

Location: Toronto, ON (4x onsite a week)

Employment Type: Contract

Pay Rate: CAD$50/HR INC

Interview Type: Face 2 Face (Onsite Interview Only)

Job Description

What will you do?


Provide world class user support to a wide variety of RBC employees on a day-to-day basis, resolving issues in a timely fashion with a high level of customer satisfaction.
Use your outstanding organizational and communication skills to stay on top of ongoing issues and manage situations involving multiple teams, both technical and business.
Perform system health check, monitoring, alerting, and corresponding action as required.
Lead the investigation and analysis of application operational failures through to resolution, and drive follow-up for root cause identification.
Analyze system monitoring dashboard (Dynatrace), logging system (ELK), and other tools to identify potential issues, conflicts, and risks.
Responsible for ensuring the production and maintenance of high-quality support documents and processes.
Write simple SQL queries against database and generate reports.
Establish healthy working relationships across variety of IT partners / units and platforms to build influence and impact with key business partners.


What do you need to succeed?

Must-have


Experience with Pega platform and Pega-based applications.
Experience with AI-powered, application support-focused solutions
Experience with architecture/process workflow analysis and design.
Deadline-driven and results-oriented; able to meet consistently high-quality standards while handling a variety of tasks and deadlines simultaneously.
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills (verbal and written)
Basic knowledge and troubleshooting of SQL, Unix, Job Scheduling etc.
Experience with monitoring tools and creating dashboards on ELK stack, Dynatrace, and PagerDuty
Flexibility to be part of a rotational on-call support model


Disclaimer: AI tools may assist in the recruitment process; however, all hiring decisions are made by the recruitment team based on a comprehensive evaluation of candidates.

Title: Junior Building Operator
Description:
Title: Junior Building Operator

Department: Commercial

Reports To: Manager, Property Services

General Description

The Junior Building Operator is responsible for ensuring the site is well maintained and in good working order by completing daily cleaning duties, managing HVAC, maintenance, tenant service and support, and all repairs listed below.

Primary Responsibilities


Work with the team to provide overall support to base building and tenants services for the Commercial portfolio
Perform daily litter pick up of parking lots, snow removal where services are not met or performed by contractor
Garbage removal, Plaster drywall, paint, plumbing, electrical, and carpentry as required:
Collecting garbage bags from garbage bins and re-locating them to disposal areas.
Replace and repair ceilings, tiles, t-bar
Patching and painting where required
Attending to plumbing concerns and minor plumbing repairs
Addressing minor electrical issues, installation of light fixtures, duplex outlets, light switches
Oversee and supervise contractors in the building for contracted and non-contracted work
Responsible for accessing MRI Angus, addressing, and closing out work orders in a timely manner
Operational and technical troubleshooting as necessary
Collection of rent and drop off tenant notices/letters as required between various properties
Responsive, anticipating tenant needs and respond in agreed upon time frames to create positive tenant experience
Communicate with Head Office and Leasing, helping to onboard and offboard of tenants (move-in/out), address day-to-day needs including keys, elevator/freight access, signage, and interior space requirements
Participate in regular interior and exterior round check to the building, common areas (washroom, parking), exit/entry points for security, and recommend building improvements
Perform regular inspection/maintenance duties on building equipment (including pumps, fans, boilers, chillers, and motors) as part of a comprehensive preventative maintenance program
Update administrative paperwork including equipment/inspection tags
Participate in updating annual building operation and construction manuals
Daily monitoring of fire safety systems to ensure equipment is fully functional
Monthly testing and maintenance of Elevators and Fire Life Safety systems within scope of the Fire Code
Assist with annual fire safety, verifications, and testing
Assume and initiate correction action for any operational problems
Recognize and acknowledge this role is 24/7 on call that may require responding to after-hours building issues and emergencies from time-to-time
Assist at other locations within the company as requested
Participate as a team, set up and take down tenant appreciation events, BBQ, holiday events, building operator lunches and learns, company events, as required
Perform other duties as assigned

Required Skills And Qualifications


Must have a valid Ontario driver’s license
Team focused
Safety first with a can-do attitude
Ability to perform basic repairs in walls, ceilings, floors, stairs, doors, and light demolitions
Ability to perform minor electrical, plumbing, and mechanical system repairs
Knowledge of building codes and safety standards as set forth by the provincial and federal government
Excellent troubleshooting and problem-solving skills
Effective communication and interpersonal skills
Excellent customer service skills
Detail oriented and good scheduling skills
Proficient with using tools associated with the job
Post Secondary, College, or Trade school diploma is an asset
Previous experience as a Building Operator is an asset

Title: Payroll Professional (HR / Finance / Accounting) - CA
Description:
Payroll Professional (HR / Finance / Accounting)

About Prolific

Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world.

Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills.

The role

We are seeking a group of Payroll Professionals to join a high-level initiative in partnership with a leading technology company. We are building a benchmark to evaluate how research models navigate complex payroll-related tasks and decisions across HR, Finance, and Accounting functions.

You will complete a series of task drawing on your real-world payroll expertise, covering areas such as payroll calculations, compliance, benefits administration, tax withholding, and reporting.

Please note, when you join our platform, you will first go through a screener to ascertain your eligibility for this project, after which you will be invited to the platform.

What you'll bring


Current involvement in payroll activities as part of your job function in Human Resources, Finance, or Accounting
Job title does not matter, provided payroll is part of your role
Familiarity with payroll processes (calculations, compliance, benefits, tax withholding, reporting)
Bonus: experience with payroll software, HRIS systems, or automated payroll workflows


What you'll be doing in the role


Complete a series of task related to payroll processes and scenarios.
Draw on your professional experience to answer questions on payroll calculations, compliance, benefits, and tax withholding.
Provide feedback on the clarity and relevance of task questions.
Share your professional judgment on realistic payroll scenarios presented in the task.


Why Prolific is a great platform to join as a Participant

Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to influence the AI models of the future using professional legal expertise. Once you pass our assessment, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home.

We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and companies with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data is the cornerstone of developing more accurate, nuanced, and aligned AI systems.

We believe that the next leap in AI capabilities won't come solely from scaling existing models, but from integrating diverse human perspectives and behaviours into AI development. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of AI innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

Links to more information on Prolific

Website

Youtube

Privacy Statement

By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific use of your personal personal information.

Title: HR Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking for an HR Data Analyst to join our small, high-impact HR Systems and Reporting team. Reporting to the Senior Manager, HR Reporting and Analytics, you will play a key role in advancing our data and reporting capabilities — turning complex HR data into actionable insights that support decision-making across the organization.




You are analytically minded, a self-starter, and thrive in an environment where precision, collaboration, and continuous improvement matter.




This position will be based at our Head Office in Markham, Ontario. Extendicare offers a hybrid working environment.




Key responsibilities include:

Partner with developers, functional leads, and stakeholders to design data schemas, define data feed requirements, and contribute to enterprise data architecture
Build and maintain dynamic Workday reports to support Operations and HR teams
Support HR initiatives through financial modelling, KPI development, and performance tracking
Assist the Payroll team with Federal and Provincial reporting programs for health care workers
Act as a liaison between HR functional user groups and technical teams — gathering, analyzing, and documenting business requirements and translating them into effective solutions
Leverage AI tools to automate reporting workflows, accelerate data analysis, and surface insights at scale
Ensure requirements are clearly defined, documented, communicated, and validated across all assigned projects
Complete other duties as assigned




What we’re looking for:

Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field
1–3 years of experience in data analysis or reporting, preferably in a fast-paced or growth-oriented environment
Proficiency in SQL Server (complex query writing) and Microsoft Excel
Demonstrated experience or strong interest in working with AI tools (e.g., Microsoft Copilot, generative AI for data analysis, prompt-based reporting)
Experience with Workday and BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, or similar) is a strong asset







What Extendicare has to offer:




At Extendicare, we believe that working as a team creates an environment that allows us to reach our potential. We value each employee, encourage equal opportunity for growth and recognize achievement. As a valued member of our team, you can expect:

Continuous mentorship, support for life-long learning and growth opportunities
Opportunities for advancement and career growth within the organization
A rewarding and meaningful work experience where you can enrich your life and the lives of others through your work.
Employee Family Assistance Program.
Robust benefits package




Extendicare is a leading provider of care and services for seniors across Canada, operating under the Extendicare, ParaMed, Extendicare Assist, and SGP Purchasing Network brands. We are committed to delivering quality care to meet the needs of a growing seniors’ population, inspired by our mission to provide people with the care they need, wherever they call home. We operate a broad network of long-term care homes (both owned and under management contracts), deliver over 24 million hours of home health care services annually, and provide group purchasing services to third parties representing approximately 157,000 beds across Canada. Extendicare proudly employs more than 30,000 qualified, highly trained and dedicated team members who are passionate about providing high-quality care and services to help people live better.

Title: Business Systems Analyst
Description:
About CareRx

Business Systems Analyst (BSA) – Job Description

CareRx is Canada’s leading provider of pharmacy services and solutions to home operators and residents. We offer a range of innovative medication solutions with an unparalleled commitment to the highest standards of care. CareRx provides services in rural and urban areas throughout Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, New Brunswick and Saskatchewan. Our network of pharmacy fulfillment centers delivers high-volume solutions for the cost-effective supply of chronic medication and other specialty clinical pharmacy services.

Job Summary

The Business Systems Analyst – as a core member of the Pharmacy Applications team, this position is an integral part that focuses on driving company-wide IT application and data initiatives from start to completion and supporting the Pharmacy Applications Team on a daily basis. Reporting to the Software Development Manager, the Business Systems Analyst (BSA) bridges the gap between IT and business teams by analyzing user needs, documenting functional requirements, and designing technical solutions to improve operational efficiency. Key responsibilities include creating system requirements, mapping processes, testing software, and providing support.

Why you should join CareRx

Collaborative Team: Work with colleagues who share a passion for shaping the future of senior care.

Make a Real Impact: Feel fulfilled knowing your work directly benefits others within the communities we serve.

Flexible Benefits: For eligible roles, enjoy flexible medical and dental coverage that fits your needs.

Defined Work Schedule: Offers a healthy work-life balance with predictable hours.

Focus on Care: Work in an environment where your clinical expertise takes priority — without the demands of retail pharmacy.

Supportive Culture: Be part of a respectful, inclusive workplace where collaboration, connection and shared purpose drive everything we do.

Stability and Growth: Join a well-established Canadian company with a strong foundation for job security and opportunities to grow your career.

Appreciation in Action: We recognize great work through peer-nominated awards, team shout-outs and everyday moments of appreciation.

Celebrations and Community: From cultural events to team socials and holiday fun — we make time to connect, celebrate and enjoy the moments that bring us together

Core Responsibilities & Duties


Requirements Gathering: Interviewing stakeholders to translate business needs into technical specifications and functional requirements.
System Analysis & Design: Analyzing existing IT systems, data flows, and infrastructure to recommend improvements, feature requests or a new solution.
Support change management activities
Testing & Implementation: Developing test plans, cases, and scripts, and overseeing system integration and user acceptance testing (UAT).
Technical Writing: Creating detailed documentation, including flowcharts, data models, and use cases as well as building operational handover and runbook documentation for each application.
Liaison Function: Acting as the primary point of contact between business users, vendors, and development teams.
Provide ad-hoc support to Pharmacy Application team: develop, manage and maintain relationships with business stakeholders and owners on behalf of the Pharmacy Applications team


Technical Competencies


Demonstrates broad technical proficiency across Microsoft technologies, including the Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word), Windows Server operating systems, Internet Information Services (IIS), and SQL Server is an asset.
Experience working in Agile environments.
Microsoft SQL Server experience is required (an understanding of SQL queries, T-SQL stored procedures, views and functions).
Nice to have some experience with design, implement, and maintain CI/CD pipelines to automate build, test, and deployment processes across environments (Git Hub Actions).
Experience with analyzing and reporting service desk ticketing data in order to identify issues, trends or expectations to drive improvements, search for solutions and reduce reoccurring tickets
Possess key problem-solving skillsets including critical analytical thinking, devising and evaluating potential alternatives and implementing viable solutions
Meet and exceed deliverables and timelines as assigned
Effective organization, planning, and time management skills
Proven ability to foster strong partnerships with other members of the IT team
Demonstrated high level of fluency in both written and spoken English: excellent customer service and interpersonal skill and be able to interact with persons of varying levels of professional expertise in a professional and mature manner
Skilled in critical thought processes and be able to prioritize appropriately
Demonstrates initiative and creativity and strong attention to detail
Self-directed requiring minimal supervision and able to work independently
Motivated to achieve results that reflect a high standard of quality


Compensation Range: $70,000 to $80,000. Actual pay rate will be based on the individual candidate’s credentials and experience.

Location: Please note that this role currently requires 1–2 days per week in the office at our 320 Bay Street location. Beginning January 2027, the office will relocate to Richmond Hill or Oakville location, with an expectation of 2 days per week onsite, increasing to 3 days per week as of June 2027.

Opportunity: This is a current existing position and is FT.

AI Disclosure: CareRx does not use AI to screen candidates

Application Process

CareRx welcomes and encourages applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. Interested, qualified candidates are encouraged to apply.

All applicants must successfully pass satisfactory background screening (depending on the role - Criminal Record Check, Credit Check, Driver’s Abstract, Education Verification, Current Professional Registration) and referencing. Background screening will be completed after an offer of employment has been extended and accepted.

Title: Business Insights Analyst II
Description:
Work Location:

Toronto, Ontario, Canada

Hours

37.5

Line Of Business

Analytics, Insights, & Artificial Intelligence

Pay Details

$81,600 - $115,200 CAD

TD is committed to providing fair and equitable compensation opportunities to all colleagues. Growth opportunities and skill development are defining features of the colleague experience at TD. Our compensation policies and practices have been designed to allow colleagues to progress through the salary range over time as they progress in their role. The base pay actually offered may vary based upon the candidate's skills and experience, job-related knowledge, geographic location, and other specific business and organizational needs.

As a candidate, you are encouraged to ask compensation related questions and have an open dialogue with your recruiter who can provide you more specific details for this role.

Job Description

About this Role

Are you passionate about analytics and working with big data? Want to drive change and become a subject matter expert in fraud management? If so, join the Canadian Fraud Performance Management Team.

You would be responsible for mining big data to discover actionable fraud insights, and to develop and implement fraud strategies in a fast paced environment. You would manage evolving fraud threats while taking into consideration TD risk appetite, Customer and operational experience. With strong technical, analytics background you would join the team to learn and manage 1st and 3rd party fraud.

Accountabilities

Fraud Management


Develop and deploy product and channel-specific fraud strategies to reduce fraud risk balancing loss, Customer experience and operational capacity
Identify new fraud vectors and optimize existing rules using SQL, Python, and fraud authentication solutions
Conduct intra-day and weekly monitoring across products, channels, and risk segments
Provide ad-hoc analysis to support decision-making at the executive level
Continually optimize, improve, and strengthen fraud strategy and analytics capabilities
Independently investigate new, high-risk fraud threats under tight timelines


Job Requirements


Advanced proficiency in SQL, Python for data analysis, automation, and reporting
2+ years of hands-on experience in an analytical capacity
Motivated to constantly strive to find innovative data-informed ways to enhance capabilities
In-depth knowledge of data science tools such as NumPy, pandas, matplotlib or R equivalent
Ability to communicate strategy analysis to both technical and non-technical team members
Passion and drive to take initiative and work independently under tight timelines
Ability to effectively manage multiple work streams and tasks at the same time
Undergraduate degree from a technical domain (e.g. computer science, engineering, mathematics, or statistics)


Preferable


Experience in fraud management is an asset
Machine learning development and deployment is an asset
Automation experience is an asset


Who We Are

TD is one of the world's leading global financial institutions and is the fifth largest bank in North America by branches/stores. Every day, we strive to make every interaction, product, and experience remarkably human and refreshingly simple for over 27 million households and businesses in Canada, the United States and around the world. More than 95,000 TD colleagues bring their skills, talent, and creativity to foster deeper relationships, ensure disciplined execution, and build a simpler, faster banking experience. TD is deeply committed to being a leader in client experience, that is why we believe that all colleagues, no matter where they work, are client facing. Together, we are reimagining what banking can be for our clients, colleagues and communities.

Our Total Rewards Package

Our Total Rewards package reflects the investments we make in our colleagues to help them and their families achieve their financial, physical, and mental well-being goals. Total Rewards at TD includes a base salary, variable compensation, and several other key plans such as health and well-being benefits, savings and retirement programs, paid time off, banking benefits and discounts, career development, and reward and recognition programs. Learn more

Additional Information

We’re delighted that you’re considering building a career with TD. Through regular development conversations, training programs, and a competitive benefits plan, we’re committed to providing the support our colleagues need to thrive both at work and at home.

Please be advised that this job opportunity is subject to provincial regulation for employment purposes. It is imperative to acknowledge that each province or territory within the jurisdiction of Canada may have its own set of regulations, requirements.

Colleague Development

If you’re interested in a specific career path or are looking to build certain skills, we want to help you succeed. You’ll have regular career, development, and performance conversations with your manager, as well as access to an online learning platform and a variety of mentoring programs to help you unlock future opportunities.

If you’re passionate about helping clients and building deep, lasting relationships, TD offers diverse career paths where you can grow your expertise and make a meaningful impact.

$81,600 - $115,200 CAD

TD is committed to providing fair and equitable compensation opportunities to all colleagues. Growth opportunities and skill development are defining features of the colleague experience at TD. Our compensation policies and practices have been designed to allow colleagues to progress through the salary range over time as they progress in their role. The base pay actually offered may vary based upon the candidate's skills and experience, job-related knowledge, geographic location, and other specific business and organizational needs.

As a candidate, you are encouraged to ask compensation related questions and have an open dialogue with your recruiter who can provide you more specific details for this role.

Job Description

About this Role

Are you passionate about analytics and working with big data? Want to drive change and become a subject matter expert in fraud management? If so, join the Canadian Fraud Performance Management Team.

You would be responsible for mining big data to discover actionable fraud insights, and to develop and implement fraud strategies in a fast paced environment. You would manage evolving fraud threats while taking into consideration TD risk appetite, Customer and operational experience. With strong technical, analytics background you would join the team to learn and manage 1st and 3rd party fraud.

Accountabilities

Fraud Management

  • Develop and deploy product and channel-specific fraud strategies to reduce fraud risk balancing loss, Customer experience and operational capacity
  • Identify new fraud vectors and optimize existing rules using SQL, Python, and fraud authentication solutions
  • Conduct intra-day and weekly monitoring across products, channels, and risk segments
  • Provide ad-hoc analysis to support decision-making at the executive level
  • Continually optimize, improve, and strengthen fraud strategy and analytics capabilities
  • Independently investigate new, high-risk fraud threats under tight timelines

Job Requirements

  • Advanced proficiency in SQL, Python for data analysis, automation, and reporting
  • 2+ years of hands-on experience in an analytical capacity
  • Motivated to constantly strive to find innovative data-informed ways to enhance capabilities
  • In-depth knowledge of data science tools such as NumPy, pandas, matplotlib or R equivalent
  • Ability to communicate strategy analysis to both technical and non-technical team members
  • Passion and drive to take initiative and work independently under tight timelines
  • Ability to effectively manage multiple work streams and tasks at the same time
  • Undergraduate degree from a technical domain (e.g. computer science, engineering, mathematics, or statistics)

Preferable

  • Experience in fraud management is an asset
  • Machine learning development and deployment is an asset
  • Automation experience is an asset

Who We Are

TD is one of the world's leading global financial institutions and is the fifth largest bank in North America by branches/stores. Every day, we strive to make every interaction, product, and experience remarkably human and refreshingly simple for over 27 million households and businesses in Canada, the United States and around the world. More than 95,000 TD colleagues bring their skills, talent, and creativity to foster deeper relationships, ensure disciplined execution, and build a simpler, faster banking experience. TD is deeply committed to being a leader in client experience, that is why we believe that all colleagues, no matter where they work, are client facing. Title: Business Analyst - IT I
Description:
Our client is a top financial institution with significant North American holdings. They have operations across most major verticals, including institutional & corporate, wealth management, private clients, commercial banking, treasury, and retail banking.
 
Introduction:
Robertson is seeking a skilled Privacy Business Analyst - IT I to join our client in support of an existing vacancy.

Contract Period: 6 months with potential to extend
Pay Rate: Starting from $26 per hour
Location: Toronto, ON
Location Type: Remote
Business Hours: Monday-Friday, Core Business Hours
 
Job Responsibilities:

Analyze and document business processes related to operational risk, including risk identification, assessment, and mitigation
Collaborate with stakeholders to elicit, define, and document business and functional requirements for Risk IT initiatives
Develop clear and comprehensive business requirement documents (BRDs) and functional specifications to support system enhancements and risk reporting
Apply relational data concepts to support source-to-target data mapping and document data flows for enterprise data lake ingestion and reporting solutions
Partner with Agile teams to support delivery of risk-related solutions, ensuring alignment with business requirements and timelines
Conduct gap analysis and exploratory data analysis to identify improvement opportunities and inform solution design

Experience & Qualification Requirements:
Bachelor’s in information technology degree required
2 years of IT Business Analyst experience required
Demonstrated ability to translate business and technical requirements into clear documentation, including business requirement documents (BRDs), process flows, and data mappings
Solid understanding of Agile delivery methodologies, with experience using tools such as Jira and working within Scrum teams
Strong SQL proficiency and data analysis skills
Working knowledge of enterprise data lake architectures and cloud-based data platforms
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills
Experience with RSA Archer or similar GRC platforms
Certifications in Agile methodologies, business analysis, or risk management
Familiarity with regulatory frameworks related to operational risk (e.g., Basel, OSFI guidelines)
Experience with data visualization and reporting tools, such as Power BI or Tableau
Familiarity with Azure data platforms and services, including Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS), Azure Cosmos DB, Azure Synapse Analytics, and Azure Databricks
Experience with Microsoft Azure
Experience with Jira
Experience with Archer (Nice to Have)
Experience with business requirements
Experience with SQL
Experience with data visualization and reporting tools, such as Power BI or Tableau
Agile and Azure Data Lake is an asset
Risk Terminology

How to Apply: If you are a motivated professional looking to contribute to a leading team, please submit your resume outlining your qualifications and experience relevant to this role. Robertson & the clients we represent, value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace. We invite all qualified individuals to apply.

Background screening is required as part of the onboarding process. The type of screening required (criminal, credit, or other verifications) will vary based on the position and client requirements.

We use AI technology as part of our application review process to assist with screening and assessment. All applications are also reviewed by our recruitment team.
  
Robertson & the clients we represent are equal opportunity employers, committed to diversity and inclusion. Robertson is a certified diverse supplier and actively seeks to foster a representative and inclusive workforce. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals, regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, protected veteran status, Aboriginal status, or any other legally protected factors. We champion building a diverse and inclusive environment.

Title: IT Support Technician
Description:
About Opendoor

At Opendoor our mission is to tilt the world in favor of homeowners and those who aim to become one. Homeownership matters. It's how people build wealth, stability, and community. It's how families put down roots, how neighborhoods strengthen, how the future gets built. We're building the modern system of homeownership giving people the freedom to buy and sell on their own terms. We’ve built an end-to-end online experience that has already helped thousands of people and we’re just getting started.

About The Role

The IT Support team delivers technical support and office technology services to all Opendoor personnel. We support end user devices (primarily Mac, with some Windows) and core productivity tools, own day to day troubleshooting for hardware, software and account access, and partner closely with Operations, People, Finance, Security and Engineering to keep our offices running smoothly.

As an IT Support Technician, you’ll be one of the on-the-ground IT partners for our Toronto office. You’ll be the go-to technical resource for local employees and visitors. You’ll handle everything from new hire setups and hardware troubleshooting to office audio and video support, combining strong technical skills with a customer-obsessed mindset and excellent communication.

What You'll Do

End User Support


Provide hands-on support for laptops (Mac and Windows), mobile devices, and peripherals (monitors, docks, keyboards, headsets). Diagnosing and resolving issues efficiently with minimal disruption to the employee.
Troubleshoot and resolve issues with core tools such as Okta, JAMF, Google Workspace, Slack, Microsoft 365, VPN, printers, and other business applications.
Provide in-office network support and partner with IT Engineering on network setup, configuration, and troubleshooting activities.
Own ticket lifecycle in our ticketing system (Linear or equivalent): triage, troubleshoot, escalate, and close within SLA.
Deliver in-person walk-up support in the office and remote support via chat, email, and video.


Onboarding & Offboarding


Prepare and provision devices for new hires including imaging, device enrollment, user profiles, application access so employees are ready to work on day one.
Support employee onboarding set up: hardware handoff, account access verification, and walkthrough of core tools.
Collect, wipe, and reassign or return devices for transfers, leaves, and terminations, in line with security and data handling standards.


Office Support


Set up and maintain conference room audio and video equipment including cameras, microphones, displays, and room controllers across Zoom, Teams, and Google Meet room configurations.
Support onsite events (all-hands, training, visitors) with reliable audio and video and network experience.
Coordinate with Workplace and Facilities on office moves, seating changes, and new space build-outs (e.g., drops, cabling, labeling).


Endpoint & Asset Management


Enroll and manage devices via our device management tooling to ensure devices meet baseline standards for operating systems versions, security agents, disk encryption and other configurations (e.g., Jamf, Intune, or similar).
Maintain complete and accurate asset inventory for Opendoor laptops, monitors, mobile devices, and peripherals.
Support vendor repair workflows and hardware lifecycle management including deployments, refreshes, returns.


Security & Compliance


Follow identity and access management best practices using Okta and internal tools to ensure employees have appropriate access.
Support multi-factor authentication resets, account lockouts, and basic access requests in line with IT and Security policies.
Build monitoring and detections that enforces security standards on endpoints - disk encryption, screen lock, operating system versions, secure device handling.


Collaboration & Continuous Improvement


Partner with IT Engineering, Security, and Workplace on cross-functional initiatives that improve the employee technology experience.
Document troubleshooting steps, how-tos, and FAQs for internal knowledge base and employee self-service content.
Identify recurring issues and propose improvements to tooling, automation, and processes.
Contribute to on-call and coverage rotations as needed within agreed schedules and time zones.


What You'll Need


3-5 years of hands-on IT support experience in a fast-growing tech company or high-volume environment - experience supporting hybrid offices across multiple locations and time zones is a plus.
Strong troubleshooting skills across macOS and Windows endpoints, core productivity tools (Google Workspace and/or Microsoft 365, Slack/Teams, Zoom), networking basics including VPN and Wi-Fi.
Experience with at least one mobile device management platform such as Jamf, Intune, NinjaOne, or similar.
Familiarity with identity and access management tools - Okta or comparable solution.
Experience with IT service management tools such as Jira or similar.
Experience with audio and video platforms including Zoom Rooms, Teams Rooms and associated hardware.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to explain technical issues in simple, friendly language.
Customer-obsessed mindset with a track record of strong service satisfaction scores or similar service metrics.
Ability to lift and move equipment as needed, typically up to ~40 lbs.


Location

This role is based in our downtown Toronto office, in-person four days per week (Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday). Candidates must be based within commuting distance of the office.

Title: Senior Analyst, Data & Analytics
Description:
Permanent Full Time

The Senior Data Analyst leads analytical framing, experimentation, business analysis, value realization, stakeholder engagement, and solution delivery support for AI-enabled business initiatives, helping teams turn complex or ambiguous opportunities into practical, measurable outcomes.

Working closely with AI Enablement leads, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, business stakeholders, and technology teams, this role translates business opportunities into measurable AI use cases and guides the evaluation, implementation, and adoption of AI solutions through evidence-based analysis and recommendations.

The successful candidate combines strong analytical skills with business acumen, acts as a trusted analytics partner, and bridges technical and business stakeholders to help AI initiatives deliver practical, measurable value while supporting consistent and repeatable delivery practices.

What you will do:


Lead the translation of complex or ambiguous business opportunities into clear AI use cases, analytical questions, success measures, and measurable outcomes that can be tested and evaluated.
Own practical analytical approaches, including experiments, pilots, measurement frameworks, reporting, and interpretation, to assess whether AI-enabled solutions are delivering value.
Build confidence in AI solutions by guiding data collection, validation, testing, quality assurance, and performance monitoring activities.
Partner with Data Engineers and Data Scientists to identify source-system data, manage it through the data lifecycle, and develop analytical products that support AI-enabled solutions.
Turn analytical findings, business context, and evaluation results into clear recommendations that influence stakeholder decisions about AI opportunities, adoption, prioritization, and next steps.
Support adoption and repeatable delivery by developing workshops, training, job-aides, measurement practices, and other materials that help teams understand and apply AI solutions effectively.


What you will bring:

Required


Degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Analytics, Data Science, Economics, Business, Engineering, or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
5+ years of experience applying data, analytics, business analysis, or digital enablement practices to solve business problems and improve outcomes.
Experience translating business needs into analytical questions, success measures, requirements, and practical recommendations.
Experience designing or supporting experiments, pilots, performance measurement approaches, or value realization assessments.
Experience working with business stakeholders and technical partners to define requirements, interpret findings, and support implementation decisions.
Proficiency with analytics and reporting tools such as Power BI, SQL, or comparable platforms.
Working knowledge of data platforms, programming, or cloud-based analytics environments such as Azure, Databricks, Python, or comparable tools.
Experience working in Agile or iterative delivery environments.
Demonstrated ability to lead through influence, collaboration, and subject-matter credibility rather than formal authority.
Experience providing guidance, peer support, or informal coaching to analysts or multidisciplinary partners.
Strong stakeholder engagement, facilitation, and communication skills.
Ability to bridge technical and business audiences effectively.
Reliability Status Security Clearance – this can only be completed with candidates who receive an offer of employment. This is a personal security status that is required as a condition of employment before an employee can gain access to Protected B information, assets or work sites as outlined by the Government of Canada  website.  The cost of submitting these checks will be covered by Canada Life.


Preferred


Experience supporting AI, machine learning, generative AI, automation, or advanced analytics initiatives.
Exposure to AI governance, responsible AI, privacy, or data governance practices.
Experience supporting proof-of-concepts, pilots, or adoption activities for emerging technology solutions.
Strong foundation in research methods, measurement design, evaluation methodologies, or benefits realization.


The base salary for this position is between $80,400- $130,400 annually. This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, commission etc). If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, your recruiter will be able to discuss additional details of our total rewards program with you.

Career opportunities will be open a minimum of 5 business days from the date of posting, closing dates will vary depending on the search activity. All applications received will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Grow with Canada Life

We’re united by a shared purpose: to improve the financial, physical and mental well-being of Canadians. Our company is trusted by 1 in 3 Canadians and contributes to the strength of communities across the country.

We’re looking for people who live our values everyday: we step up, we do the right thing, and we deliver – for our customers, communities and each other. Are you someone who always strives to do the right thing, who steps up for themselves and others, and who delivers with impact? Then we want to hear from you!

What we offer:

We’re committed to supporting our employees through every stage of their career. Here’s what you can expect as a full-time or part-time permanent team member:


Career Development: Opportunities for career advancement, access to industry-leading learning programs and up to $2,000 annually towards education reimbursement.

HTML:
Permanent Full Time

The Senior Data Analyst leads analytical framing, experimentation, business analysis, value realization, stakeholder engagement, and solution delivery support for AI-enabled business initiatives, helping teams turn complex or ambiguous opportunities into practical, measurable outcomes.

Working closely with AI Enablement leads, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, business stakeholders, and technology teams, this role translates business opportunities into measurable AI use cases and guides the evaluation, implementation, and adoption of AI solutions through evidence-based analysis and recommendations.

The successful candidate combines strong analytical skills with business acumen, acts as a trusted analytics partner, and bridges technical and business stakeholders to help AI initiatives deliver practical, measurable value while supporting consistent and repeatable delivery practices.

What you will do:

  • Lead the translation of complex or ambiguous business opportunities into clear AI use cases, analytical questions, success measures, and measurable outcomes that can be tested and evaluated.
  • Own practical analytical approaches, including experiments, pilots, measurement frameworks, reporting, and interpretation, to assess whether AI-enabled solutions are delivering value.
  • Build confidence in AI solutions by guiding data collection, validation, testing, quality assurance, and performance monitoring activities.
  • Partner with Data Engineers and Data Scientists to identify source-system data, manage it through the data lifecycle, and develop analytical products that support AI-enabled solutions.
  • Turn analytical findings, business context, and evaluation results into clear recommendations that influence stakeholder decisions about AI opportunities, adoption, prioritization, and next steps.
  • Support adoption and repeatable delivery by developing workshops, training, job-aides, measurement practices, and other materials that help teams understand and apply AI solutions effectively.

What you will bring:

Required

  • Degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Analytics, Data Science, Economics, Business, Engineering, or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
  • 5+ years of experience applying data, analytics, business analysis, or digital enablement practices to solve business problems and improve outcomes.
  • Experience translating business needs into analytical questions, success measures, requirements, and practical recommendations.
  • Experience designing or supporting experiments, pilots, performance measurement approaches, or value realization assessments.
  • Experience working with business stakeholders and technical partners to define requirements, interpret findings, and support implementation decisions.
  • Proficiency with analytics and reporting tools such as Power BI, SQL, or comparable platforms.
  • Working knowledge of data platforms, programming, or cloud-based analytics environments such as Azure, Databricks, Python, or comparable tools.
  • Experience working in Agile or iterative delivery environments.
  • Demonstrated ability to lead through influence, collaboration, and subject-matter credibility rather than formal authority.
  • Experience providing guidance, peer support, or informal coaching to analysts or multidisciplinary partners.
  • Strong stakeholder engagement, facilitation, and communication skills.
  • Ability to bridge technical and business audiences effectively.
  • Reliability Status Security Clearance – this can only be completed with candidates who receive an offer of employment. This is a personal security status that is required as a condition of employment before an employee can gain access to Protected B information, assets or work sites as outlined by the Government of Canada  website.  The cost of submitting these checks will be covered by Canada Life.

Preferred

  • Experience supporting AI, machine learning, generative AI, automation, or advanced analytics initiatives.
  • Exposure to AI governance, responsible AI, privacy, or data governance practices.
  • Experience supporting proof-of-concepts, pilots, or adoption activities for emerging technology solutions.
  • Strong foundation in research methods, measurement design, evaluation methodologies, or benefits realization.

The base salary for this position is between $80,400- $130,400 annually. This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, commission etc). If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, your recruiter will be able to discuss additional details of our total rewards program with you.

Career opportunities will be open a minimum of 5 business days from the date of posting, closing dates will vary depending on the search activity. All applications received will be reviewed on a rolling basis.
