Title: Manager, Data & Analytics Business Partner
Description:
We’re searching for a Manager, Data & Analytics Business Partner. Are you searching for new possibilities?

Westland Insurance Group Ltd is growing and has an exciting opportunity for a Manager, Data & Analytics Business Partner to join our growing team. This hybrid role is based out of our Head Office in Surrey, BC.

Expected Compensation: The expected hiring range for this role is $106,000- $138,000 with a 35-hour work week. Your rate of pay will be determined based on your individual qualifications and experience, while considering internal equity. 

Westland also offers a competitive annual short-term incentive program that enables employees to share in the rewards of the business and emphasizes the measures that are key to our success.

Westland is committed to continuous learning where employees can grow their potential and write the stories of their careers. Financial assistance is available for approved work-related training/development courses leading to certification, and programs that offer professional development, including seminars and conferences.

Discover What’s Possible, With Westland.

The Westland story is all about growth, and that means plenty of possibility for everyone on our team. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of clients, communities, and each other with equal commitment. As we continue to open new doors, we're inviting amazing people like you to join us.

As a Manager, Data & Analytics Business Partner, you’ll act as a strategic analytics leader partnering with senior business leaders to deliver insights that shape business decisions and drive measurable impact for Westland

Interested in learning more?

Why You'll Love Westland


An open, flexible and welcoming workplace
Plenty of opportunities to grow and learn
Autonomy to drive your own success
Be part of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds
A total rewards program that takes care of your financial, physical, and mental health
Flexible paid Values Days to celebrate days important to you
Paid Volunteer Days for employees as part of Amplifying Communities, a program to support causes that matter most to our employees and clients


Why We’ll Love You


You are a strong analytics leader who thrives on partnering with business leaders to drive decision-making
You bring a consultative mindset and enjoy translating business questions into actionable data insights
You have a strong foundation in data analytics, business intelligence, or a related field, supported by a relevant degree (e.g., Statistics, Computer Science, Data Science, Economics, Mathematics)
You have at least 7 years of experience in data analytics or a related discipline, with demonstrated business impact
You are experienced in working directly with senior stakeholders and building trusted partnerships
You are comfortable working with tools and programming languages such as SQL, Python, or R
You have experience with modern data platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake, or Microsoft Fabric
You are a strong communicator who can translate complex analytics into meaningful insights for non-technical audiences


Once Here, You'll


Act as the primary data and analytics partner for a business unit, building strong relationships with leadership and embedding data into decision-making
Design and deliver high-impact analytics and insights that drive measurable business outcomes such as growth, retention, and efficiency
Lead strategic data initiatives and develop prototypes that demonstrate business value and can scale into enterprise solutions
Improve data literacy and enable self-service analytics by promoting trusted data, tools, and best practices
Collaborate with Data Office teams to align analytics solutions with enterprise data platforms and capabilities
Identify opportunities to leverage data, analytics, and AI/ML to solve business challenges
Contribute to building and scaling the Data & Analytics Business Partner function, with the opportunity to grow and lead a team of analysts over time


Want to get to know each other better? Send your resume our way.

You belong here

We strive to be much more than a great place to work. Here, you join a collective of incredibly vibrant, purpose driven, and open-minded people. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of communities, clients, and each other with equal commitment. You will be supported and respected for who you are and for the voice you add to the conversation.

At Westland, you will feel the power of community. Westland Insurance Ltd. is an inclusive organization that values diversity in its workforce. We listen, champion equity and diversity, and create safe welcoming spaces where everyone has a voice and is free to be themselves. Westland Insurance Ltd. encourages applications from all qualified individuals and will accommodate applicants' disability-related needs, up to the point of undue hardship, throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.

If you require a disability-related accommodation in order to participate in the recruitment process, please contact the recruitment team by email at careers@westlandinsurance.ca

Title: Data Analyst - Coquitlam
Description:
Data Analyst

Are you a Data Analyst with a passion problem solving? Factors Group are a local and fast-growing manufacturing and health and wellness company where your work will have a direct and meaningful impact on business growth. As part of a collaborative and continuous improvement environment, you’ll have the opportunity to both help us as we migrate to Fabric, and work on projects you’re passionate about, all while building your skills and advancing your career.

Permanent, Full-time

Monday – Friday, 8:00am – 4:30pm

Hybrid – 2000 Brigantine Drive, Coquitlam, BC

$75,000 – $90,000 per annum

In this role, you will:


Analyze and interpret data from multiple business systems to support informed decision-making.
Design and maintain dashboards and reports using Power BI, Tableau, and Excel.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to gather requirements and improve reporting solutions.
Identify and resolve data discrepancies to ensure data integrity and consistency across systems.
Support data governance initiatives and contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality and accessibility.


You have:


A Bachelor’s degree or diploma in Computer Science, Business Systems Analysis, or a related field.
3+ years of experience in data analysis, including data modeling and analytics.
Strong proficiency in Power BI, Tableau, Excel, and Power Query (DAX/LOD).
Working knowledge of t-SQL for data querying and manipulation (Python is an asset).
Experience working with diverse data sources, including databases, cloud platforms, and APIs.


You get:


Great benefits package (healthcare, health & personal spending accounts, wellness days, Employee & Family Assistance Program)
Company-provided RRSP
$300 per year to spend on company products
Employee discount pricing
Employee appreciation events & rewards
Free onsite parking
Bus pass reimbursement
Exclusive discounts with various vendors


Why us?


We’re much bigger than you think – 30+ natural health brands loved across North America.
Proudly Canadian-owned & operated
We are committed to sustainability across people, planet, and profit, embedding responsible practices into how we operate and grow as a business.


Learn more about us here: https://ca.naturalfactors.com/pages/our-nature

FGOffice

This position is open to all qualified applicants who are legally entitled to work in the country in which this job is located. Only electronic applications can be accepted. This position will close when a competitive candidate pool has been identified. We thank you all for your applications and a member of our team will contact you soon. All the best!

All Internal applicants will be given priority for consideration for the first five business days that this position is posted. After that five days, Internal applicants will be considered along with all other applicants; however, are guaranteed at minimum a call from the Talent Acquisition team.

Title: Data Analyst 1
Description:
Come join Pattison Food Group Ltd., a Jim Pattison business, and Canada’s largest Western-based provider of food and health products.

Pattison Food Group Ltd. is a certified Great Place to Work® and consists of Save-On-Foods and other well-known grocery banners. With nearly 300 retail locations, we proudly employ nearly 30,000 team members.

We have an exciting opportunity for a full-time Data Analyst 1 to join our Data & Analytics team in Langley, BC.

Reporting to the Lead Data Engineer, the Data Analyst 1 role supports business decision-making through data analysis, reporting, and insight generation. This position works closely with business stakeholders to understand key questions, translate them into analytical approaches, and deliver clear, actionable insights.

This role contributes to a range of initiatives across areas such as customer analytics, loyalty, marketing, retail operations, and merchandising. Using large and complex datasets, the Data Analyst 1 helps identify trends, measure outcomes, support experimentation, and uncover opportunities to drive business value.

You will be responsible for: (Responsibilities)


Partnering with business stakeholders to understand priorities, business questions, processes, and KPIs.
Analyzing data to generate insights that support decision-making and continuous improvement.
Building reports, dashboards, and visualizations that communicate findings clearly and effectively to stakeholders.
Maintaining dashboards, reports, and other data assets in production to support their ongoing accuracy, reliability, and performance.
Writing SQL queries to extract, manipulate, and prepare data for analysis and reporting.
Using Python and notebook-based tools for exploratory analysis, data wrangling, and statistical work where needed.
Supporting the design, execution, and interpretation of experiments such as A/B testing and other hypothesis-driven analyses.
Validating, troubleshooting, and improving data used in reporting and analysis.
Working with large datasets in cloud-based or modern analytics environments.
Supporting the governance, administration, and cost optimization of data visualization and reporting tools.
Collaborating with data and cross-functional partners to support reporting, analysis, and business needs.
Identifying trends, opportunities, and actionable recommendations through thoughtful analysis.
Building knowledge of core datasets, reporting logic, and business context within the assigned analytics domain.
Supporting broader Data & Analytics team priorities and cross-functional projects as required.



You have: (Requirements)


A degree in a relevant field such as statistics, mathematics, computer science, engineering, economics, or another quantitative discipline.
3+ years of relevant background in data analytics, business analytics, reporting, or a related field.
Working knowledge of SQL for querying and transforming data.
Familiarity with Power BI, Databricks AI/BI, Tableau, or similar visualization and reporting tools.
Ability to build dashboards or reports that support business decision-making.
Exposure to large or complex datasets.
Working knowledge of Python for data analysis is preferred.
Exposure to experimentation, test design, or statistical analysis is an asset.
Familiarity with data modeling or data quality validation is an asset.
Exposure to cloud data environments such as Azure, AWS, or GCP is an asset.
Experience working with Databricks is an asset.



You are: (Competencies)


Able to translate business needs into practical data solutions.
Able to translate data into clear, practical insights for non-technical stakeholders.
An effective written and verbal communicator.
Detail-oriented and committed to quality.
Able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Self-motivated and comfortable working both independently and collaboratively.
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn new tools, datasets, and business contexts.



What we offer:

In addition to a highly competitive salary, we offer a great range of benefits, a company pension plan, free parking, exclusive team member offers, and opportunities for development and career progression.

If you’re looking to join a team that puts people first, you’re in the right place. Apply online today!

Compensation Details:

$75,509.00 - $94,387.00

The compensation offered for this position will take into consideration location, education, skills, experience and other factors.

Description:
Staff - Non Union

Job Category

M&P - AAPS

Job Profile

AAPS Salaried - Institutional Analysis, Level A

Job Title

Data Analyst

Department

Learning, IT and Analytics | IT Services | UBC Sauder School of Business |

Compensation Range

$5,964.75 - $8,573.08 CAD Monthly

The Compensation Range is the span between the minimum and maximum base salary for a position. The midpoint of the range is approximately halfway between the minimum and the maximum and represents an employee that possesses full job knowledge, qualifications and experience for the position. In the normal course, employees will be hired, transferred or promoted between the minimum and midpoint of the salary range for a job.

Posting End Date

July 30, 2026

Note: Applications will be accepted until 11:59 PM on the Posting End Date.

Job End Date

Ongoing

At UBC, we believe that attracting and sustaining a diverse workforce is key to the successful pursuit of excellence in research, innovation, and learning for all faculty, staff and students. Our commitment to employment equity helps achieve inclusion and fairness, brings rich diversity to UBC as a workplace, and creates the necessary conditions for a rewarding career.

Job Summary

UBC Sauder School of Business is one of the world’s leading business faculties. The school consistently ranks among the top 100 in the world and is recognized internationally for excellence in research and learning, an outstanding faculty and unmatched global partnerships. A collaborative and respectful culture within which all members of our community can thrive is a key principle in our strategic plan, and one that underpins our educational mission.

We acknowledge that UBC Sauder at the Vancouver Point Grey campus is situated on the traditional, ancestral and unceded territory of the Musqueam. We acknowledge that UBC Sauder at UBC Robson Square is situated on the traditional territory of the Musqueam, Squamish and Tsleil-Waututh.

The Sauder IT & Analytics team partners with faculty, staff, students, and other institutional stakeholders to deliver and sustain relevant, reliable, and innovative information technology solutions. Operating within a dynamic and fast-paced environment, we are committed to driving evidence-based decision-making in support of academic and operational excellence.

The Data Analyst sources and manages data from multiple systems-of-record, including raw data from databases and application APIs, curated extracts, and ad-hoc datasets. This position supports data management and analytics for the UBC Sauder School of Business, while working closely with institutional data governance and analytics teams.

This role focuses on identifying, sourcing, cleaning, managing and transforming data to provide clear, actionable insights for multiple UBC Sauder stakeholders. The Data Analyst is responsible for identifying and sourcing required data, preparing trusted and analytics-ready datasets, developing impactful visualizations, and enabling self-service analytics for business users using modern technologies such as Microsoft Fabric and AI-enabled analytics tools. The Data Analyst is responsible for a number of analytic areas, including, but not limited to, data preparation and management, data mining, statistical analysis, visual analysis, temporal data analysis and/or text analysis.

This role is ideal for a team-focused professional who specializes in identifying and preparing high-quality data and visualizations that empower business users. This position will play a central role in enabling data-driven decision-making, communicating insights, and advancing a modern data management and analytics environment for the UBC Sauder School of Business.

Organizational Status

Reports to the Assistant Dean, Information Technology & Analytics, UBC Sauder School of Business.

The Data Analyst collaborates proactively with business stakeholders within UBC Sauder, including other data analysts, faculty and administrative stakeholders. The Data Analyst also works with UBC’s Enterprise Data Governance & Business Intelligence teams. Work is reviewed in terms of achievement of high professional standards in the delivery of data and analytics services and broad project objectives and goals.

Work Performed

Business Engagement & Delivery


Partner with stakeholders to understand business needs, gather requirements and translate them into analytical datasets, dashboards, reports and analytical solutions.
Work with stakeholders to identify relevant data sources, clarify data definitions, and understand how data flows across systems.
Support reporting needs across key domains (e.g., finance, HR, learning systems, marketing, admissions).
Enable self-service analytics by creating reusable datasets, semantic models, and reports.
Provide guidance and training to users on how to interpret and use data effectively.
Support business users in understanding the end-to-end flow of data.


Data Discovery, Dataset Development & Data Preparation


Identify, source, and bring together data from multiple information systems, files, databases, and business processes to support reporting and analytics needs.
Build and maintain curated, analytics-ready datasets within Microsoft Fabric (Lakehouse, Warehouse, OneLake).
Clean, transform, standardize and model data using SQL, Power Query, and Fabric Dataflows.
Apply business rules and data transformation logic to ensure datasets are accurate, consistent, and aligned with reporting needs.
Collaborate with other analysts, data managers, and technical partners to source, refine, and prepare data for analytics consumption.
Collaborate with other analysts and data managers to optimize data for performance, accuracy, and long-term maintenance.


Data Quality & Governance


Validate and reconcile data to ensure accuracy, completeness, consistency, and reliability across reports and dashboards.
Document data definitions, calculations, transformation logic, assumptions and lineage for transparency and reuse.
Contribute and adhere to data governance practices, including the use of standardized data definitions, metrics and metadata.
Identify and troubleshoot data issues, working with upstream teams to resolve them.


Dashboarding & Data Visualization


Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI within Microsoft Fabric.
Create clear, intuitive and visually compelling data products that communicate key metrics, trends, insights, and business unit performance.
Define and standardize KPIs and reporting layers.
Promote best practices in data visualization, usability, and performance, with an emphasis on empowering business user self-service where appropriate.


Advanced Analytics & Continuous Improvement


Perform exploratory data analysis to uncover trends, patterns, and opportunities.
Support advanced analytics initiatives by preparing high-quality datasets.
Use novel technologies, including artificial intelligence, to support data management and analysis.
Leverage Microsoft Fabric capabilities (e.g., Notebooks, Pipelines, Data Agents) to enhance productivity and insight generation.
Manage version control of notebooks and data deployments across environments.
Improve dashboards, datasets, and reporting processes based on user feedback, and changing business needs.
Identify, source, clean, prepare and manage data from multiple independent systems-of-record.
Ensure data is continuously available in data lakes and/or data warehouses
Validate data for accuracy, completeness, consistency, and reliability across datasets.
Build dashboards, reports, and curated datasets.
Maintain documentation for datasets, data definitions, KPIs, calculations, and reporting logic.
Ensure alignment with enterprise data standards and governance practices.
Support the full analytics lifecycle, from data discovery and preparation through to reporting, visualization, and insight delivery.
Contribute to the evolution of the organization’s analytics and reporting capabilities.
Performs other duties as assigned.


Consequence of Error/Judgement

The position is expected to exercise sound judgement in organizing work, communicating with stakeholders, following established procedures, documenting issues accurately, and escalating concerns in a timely manner. Poor judgement or inaccurate information may negatively affect the quality, consistency, and continuity of activities within UBC Sauder. The role will have access to a significant amount of data and responsibility for this data. All work must be within the bounds of data security and privacy.

Supervision Received

Works within a team, showing proactive initiative under the direction of the Assistant Dean, Information Technology & Analytics.

Supervision Given

Takes on a leadership role as required in directing ad hoc projects and recommending solutions to ensure optimal reporting and quality of data analysis.

Minimum Qualifications

Undergraduate degree in a relevant discipline. Minimum of two years of related experience, or the equivalent combination of education and experience.


Willingness to respect diverse perspectives, including perspectives in conflict with one’s own
Demonstrates a commitment to enhancing one’s own awareness, knowledge, and skills related to equity, diversity, and inclusion


Preferred Qualifications


Undergraduate or graduate degree in Business Operations, Data Analytics, Computer Science, or a related field.
Experience in data management, data analysis, business intelligence, or other analytics roles.
Experience in proactively identifying, collecting and preparing data for use in data pipelines, reporting, and analytics.
Experience building dashboards and preparing datasets in enterprise environments.
Experience working with large and complex datasets from multiple independent systems of record.


People Skills


Excellent communication and data storytelling abilities.
Strong stakeholder engagement and collaboration skills.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy, quality and documentation.


Technical Skills


Strong experience with Python and Microsoft Fabric (including Power BI, Dataflows, and Lakehouse/Warehouse environments).
Strong experience with SQL (T-SQL, PL/SQL) for data querying, transformation, and validation.
Advanced skills in DAX, semantic modeling, and building interactive reports within the Fabric ecosystem.
Expertise in building Power BI dashboards, reports, and semantic models.
Experience with OneLake, Delta tables, and Medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold) concepts.
Proficiency in Power Query M and/or Microsoft Fabric Dataflows for data preparation.
Familiarity with notebook-based analytics.

Additional Assets

Knowledge of data governance, data quality, and metadata management practices.
Familiarity with cloud-based analytics platforms (Microsoft Azure and AWS preferred).
Experience enabling self-service analytics in large organizations.

Job Summary

UBC Sauder School of Business is one of the world’s leading business faculties. The school consistently ranks among the top 100 in the world and is recognized internationally for excellence in research and learning, an outstanding faculty and unmatched global partnerships. A collaborative and respectful culture within which all members of our community can thrive is a key principle in our strategic plan, and one that underpins our educational mission.

We acknowledge that UBC Sauder at the Vancouver Point Grey campus is situated on the traditional, ancestral and unceded territory of the Musqueam. We acknowledge that UBC Sauder at UBC Robson Square is situated on the traditional territory of the Musqueam, Squamish and Tsleil-Waututh.

The Sauder IT & Analytics team partners with faculty, staff, students, and other institutional stakeholders to deliver and sustain relevant, reliable, and innovative information technology solutions. Operating within a dynamic and fast-paced environment, we are committed to driving evidence-based decision-making in support of academic and operational excellence.

The Data Analyst sources and manages data from multiple systems-of-record, including raw data from databases and application APIs, curated extracts, and ad-hoc datasets. This position supports data management and analytics for the UBC Sauder School of Business, while working closely with institutional data governance and analytics teams.

This role focuses on identifying, sourcing, cleaning, managing and transforming data to provide clear, actionable insights for multiple UBC Sauder stakeholders. The Data Analyst is responsible for identifying and sourcing required data, preparing trusted and analytics-ready datasets, developing impactful visualizations, and enabling self-service analytics for business users using modern technologies such as Microsoft Fabric and AI-enabled analytics tools. The Data Analyst is responsible for a number of analytic areas, including, but not limited to, data preparation and management, data mining, statistical analysis, visual analysis, temporal data analysis and/or text analysis.

This role is ideal for a team-focused professional who specializes in identifying and preparing high-quality data and visualizations that empower business users. This position will play a central role in enabling data-driven decision-making, communicating insights, and advancing a modern data management and analytics environment for the UBC Sauder School of Business.

Organizational Status

Reports to the Assistant Dean, Information Technology & Analytics, UBC Sauder School of Business.

The Data Analyst collaborates proactively with business stakeholders within UBC Sauder, including other data analysts, faculty and administrative stakeholders. The Data Analyst also works with UBC’s Enterprise Data Governance & Business Intelligence teams. Work is reviewed in terms of achievement of high professional standards in the delivery of data and analytics services and broad project objectives and goals.

Work Performed

Business Engagement & Delivery

  • Partner with stakeholders to understand business needs, gather requirements and translate them into analytical datasets, dashboards, reports and analytical solutions.
  • Work with stakeholders to identify relevant data sources, clarify data definitions, and understand how data flows across systems.
  • Support reporting needs across key domains (e.g., finance, HR, learning systems, marketing, admissions).
  • Enable self-service analytics by creating reusable datasets, semantic models, and reports.
  • Provide guidance and training to users on how to interpret and use data effectively.
  • Support business users in understanding the end-to-end flow of data.

Data Discovery, Dataset Development & Data Preparation

  • Identify, source, and bring together data from multiple information systems, files, databases, and business processes to support reporting and analytics needs.
  • Build and maintain curated, analytics-ready datasets within Microsoft Fabric (Lakehouse, Warehouse, OneLake).
  • Clean, transform, standardize and model data using SQL, Power Query, and Fabric Dataflows.
  • Apply business rules and data transformation logic to ensure datasets are accurate, consistent, and aligned with reporting needs.
  • Collaborate with other analysts, data managers, and technical partners to source, refine, and prepare data for analytics consumption.
  • Collaborate with other analysts and data managers to optimize data for performance, accuracy, and long-term maintenance.

Data Quality & Governance

  • Validate and reconcile data to ensure accuracy, completeness, consistency, and reliability across reports and dashboards.
  • Document data definitions, calculations, transformation logic, assumptions and lineage for transparency and reuse.
  • Contribute and adhere to data governance practices, including the use of standardized data definitions, metrics and metadata.
  • Identify and troubleshoot data issues, working with upstream teams to resolve them.

Dashboarding & Data Visualization

  • Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI within Microsoft Fabric.
  • Create clear, intuitive and visually compelling data products that communicate key metrics, trends, insights, and business unit performance.
  • Define and standardize KPIs and reporting layers.
  • Promote best practices in data visualization, usability, and performance, with an emphasis on empowering business user self-service where appropriate.

Advanced Analytics & Continuous Improvement

  • Perform exploratory data analysis to uncover trends, patterns, and opportunities.
  • Support advanced analytics initiatives by preparing high-quality datasets.
  • Use novel technologies, including artificial intelligence, to support data management and analysis.
  • Leverage Microsoft Fabric capabilities (e.g., Notebooks, Pipelines, Data Agents) to enhance productivity and insight generation.
  • Manage version control of notebooks and data deployments across environments.
  • Improve dashboards, datasets, and reporting processes based on user feedback, and changing business needs.
  • Identify, source, clean, prepare and manage data from multiple independent systems-of-record.
  • Ensure data is continuously available in data lakes and/or data warehouses
  • Validate data for accuracy, completeness, consistency, and reliability across datasets.
  • Build dashboards, reports, and curated datasets.
  • Maintain documentation for datasets, data definitions, KPIs, calculations, and reporting logic.
  • Ensure alignment with enterprise data standards and governance practices.
  • Support the full analytics lifecycle, from data discovery and preparation through to reporting, visualization, and insight delivery.
  • Contribute to the evolution of the organization’s analytics and reporting capabilities.
  • Performs other duties as assigned.

Consequence of Error/Judgement

The position is expected to exercise sound judgement in organizing work, communicating with stakeholders, following established procedures, documenting issues accurately, and escalating concerns in a timely manner. Poor judgement or inaccurate information may negatively affect the quality, consistency, and continuity of activities within UBC Sauder. The role will have access to a significant amount of data and responsibility for this data. All work must be within the bounds of data security and privacy.

Supervision Received

Works within a team, showing proactive initiative under the direction of the Assistant Dean, Information Technology & Analytics.

Supervision Given

Takes on a leadership role as required in directing ad hoc projects and recommending solutions to ensure optimal reporting and quality of data analysis.

Minimum Qualifications

Undergraduate degree in a relevant discipline. Minimum of two years of related experience, or the equivalent combination of education and experience.

  • Willingness to respect diverse perspectives, including perspectives in conflict with one’s own
  • Demonstrates a commitment to enhancing one’s own awareness, knowledge, and skills related to equity, diversity, and inclusion

Preferred Qualifications

  • Undergraduate or graduate degree in Business Operations, Data Analytics, Computer Science, or a related field.
  • Experience in data management, data analysis, business intelligence, or other analytics roles.
  • Experience in proactively identifying, collecting and preparing data for use in data pipelines, reporting, and analytics.
  • Experience building dashboards and preparing datasets in enterprise environments.
  • Experience working with large and complex datasets from multiple independent systems of record.

People Skills

  • Excellent communication and data storytelling abilities.
  • Strong stakeholder engagement and collaboration skills.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy, quality and documentation.

Technical Skills

  • Strong experience with Python and Microsoft Fabric (including Power BI, Dataflows, and Lakehouse/Warehouse environments).
  • Strong experience with SQL (T-SQL, PL/SQL) for data querying, transformation, and validation.
  • Advanced skills in DAX, semantic modeling, and building interactive reports within the Fabric ecosystem.
  • Expertise in building Power BI dashboards, reports, and semantic models.
  • Experience with OneLake, Delta tables, and Medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold) concepts.
  • Proficiency in Power Query M and/or Microsoft Fabric Dataflows for data preparation.
  • Familiarity with notebook-based analytics.
  • Understanding of data modeling concepts (star schemas, dimensional modeling).

Additional Assets

  • Experience with enterprise systems such as Workday, CRM/Salesforce, and Student Information Systems.
  • Knowledge of data governance, data quality, and metadata management practices.
  • Familiarity with cloud-based analytics platforms (Microsoft Azure and AWS preferred).
  • Experience enabling self-service analytics in large organizations.
  • Familiarity with Data Agents/Copilot in Microsoft Fabric.
  • Experience working in Higher Education.

Description:
Description

At Amazon, we're working to be the most customer-centric company on earth. To get there, we need exceptionally talented, bright, and driven people. If you'd like to help us build the place to find and buy anything online, this is your chance to make history.

Amazon Canada Fulfillment Services is hiring a Data Analyst to support the Inventory Control / Quality Assurance (ICQA) team. The Data Analyst assigned to ICQA will work closely with the ICQA and operations teams in Inbound and Outbound to provide data and analytical support for ACES initiatives, root cause investigation of defects, and will provide support to ensure compliance to ICQA-related SoX requirements.

The Data Analyst will own the insights, set up analysis, and create automated dashboards and reporting. They will be responsible for identifying data sources (internal / external) to invent and simplify mechanisms and create custom reporting and analytics automation tools.

Operating in a fast-moving and sometimes ambiguous environment, you will be required to work autonomously, taking full control and responsibility for achieving business objectives. This role provides real opportunity to develop original ideas, approaches and solutions in an ever-changing and competitive business climate.

Key job responsibilities


Development of data collection processes and data management systems
Maintenance of data integrity (0% error rate)
Designing of queries, compiling of data, and generation of reports in MS Excel
Charting and graphing of data for reporting purposes
In depth research of defect trends
Data collection and entry as needed
Data mining and problem solving
Back up for Process Assistant duties


Basic Qualifications


2+ years of professional or military experience
Work 40 hours/week, and overtime as required
Work a flexible schedule/shift/work area, including weekends, nights, and/or holidays
Are 18 years of age or older
2+ years of business analyst, data analyst or an equivalent role experience
1+ years of using SQL to extract and manipulate data experience
Bachelor's degree, or High school or equivalent diploma
2+ years of professional work experience, or experience in MS Office, with an emphasis on Excel
Experience writing complex Excel VBA macros
Experience in advanced SQL, Python, Tableau
Experience demonstrating problem solving and root cause analysis
Experience with modern software development, web development frameworks, APIs
1+ years of project management experience


Preferred Qualifications


3+ years of business analyst, data analyst or similar role experience
Experience in written and verbal communication skills to communicate with technical and non-technical audiences, including senior leadership
Experience dealing well with ambiguity, prioritizing needs, and delivering measurable results in an agile environment
Experience programming with at least one software programming language, or experience with full software development life cycle, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, build processes, testing, and operations
Knowledge of data warehousing and data modeling
Experience with data scripting languages (e.g., SQL, Python, R, or equivalent) or statistical/mathematical software (e.g., R, SAS, Matlab, or equivalent)
Bachelor's degree in Business, Engineering, Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Supply Chain or related field.


Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

The base salary range for this position is listed below. As a total compensation company, Amazon's package may include other elements such as sign-on payments and restricted stock units (RSUs). Final compensation will be determined based on factors including experience, qualifications, and location. Amazon offers comprehensive benefits including health insurance (medical, dental, vision, prescription, basic life & AD&D insurance), Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP), Deferred Profit Sharing Plan (DPSP), paid time off, and other resources to improve health and well-being. We thank all applicants for their interest, however only those interviewed will be advised as to hiring status.

CAN, BC, Pitt Meadows - 23.00 - 35.00 CAD hourly


Company - Amazon Canada Fulfillment Services, ULC.

Title: Data Analyst, ReBIRD
Description:
Your Opportunity at ARC’TERYX:
As the Data Analyst, ReBIRD, your expertise in analysis and data-driven insights supports key decisions on product service, repair, and long-term strategy. Reporting to the Senior Data Analyst, ReBIRD you will work closely with cross-functional partners of the ReBIRD program to deliver a best-in-class guest experience that extends the lifecycle of Arc’teryx products. You are a driven professional focused on creating a data-driven culture and enabling decision makers in the short- and long-term strategy to deliver on a best-in-class guest experience.




This role is based out of our North Vancouver office and is open to hybrid remote work. Candidates must be eligible to work in Canada.






Meet Your Future Team:




Under the Circularity umbrella, the ReBIRD team supports the operations of our warranty and repairs program, our service centers within retail, and resale.  The global ReBIRD program focuses on an exceptional Guest experience at all Commercial touchpoints, inclusive of Guest Services, After Sales, Branded activations, and especially within our owned Vertical channels, Retail and eCommerce. 







If you were in the Data Analyst, ReBIRD role now, here are some of the core activities you would be doing:


Performing data manipulation and analysis to support key performance metrics across the service and repair network


Maintaining recurring forecasts and supporting the development of new forecasting techniques


Building and maintaining dashboards in Power BI including troubleshooting and debugging errors, refreshing, and publishing data


Gathering business requirements and translating them into data requirements and specifications


Collaborating with cross-functional teams including Business Intelligence, Technology, Design, Quality Control, and Supply Chain; representing company needs in after-sales returns and service and working on projects to enable informed decision making


Improving data through research and analysis of product returns and trends in support of product insights and design teams


Understanding and teaching others about global service delivery systems and processes in relation to regional differences and constraints for reporting visibility





Here are some of the things you could be working on in the future:





Supporting the creation and reporting of sustainability metrics and measuring the impact of the service and repair program, in partnership with our Sustainability team


Contributing to the evolution of data tools across the business, providing subject matter expertise in future development of data platforms


Integrating APAC regions into greater data analytics work to better inform global ReBIRD analytics


Incorporating raw material data to support forecasting, ordering, managing, and fulfillment






Are you our next Data Analyst, ReBIRD?



You have a Bachelor’s Degree in Math, Economics, Statistics, Finance, Business or a related field


You have 3+ years' experience synthesizing complex analysis into actionable insights and presenting insights to senior leaders


You have excellent interpersonal skills and the ability to summarize and express ideas clearly through visualizations, written documents and presentations


You have extensive experience in data visualization and analysis software (e.g. Power BI, Excel, Tableau, SQL, etc.)


You have experience with data manipulation and management including ETL, user provisioning, and Power BI service management


You have experience balancing customer focus with long-term strategic goals and operational constraints, and you take initiative in documenting and presenting opportunities for improvement


You are proactive in identifying the root cause of issues and developing solutions.


You remain highly flexible and adaptable when faced with ambiguity.


Your passion for your work is paralleled by your passion for getting outside and living it.


You are able and willing to travel as required








CompensationThe salary range for this role is CAD$71,000 - CAD$93,000
















Total Rewards




Most new team members join between the start and midpoint of the range, where you can contribute right away while continuing to grow your craft, your collaborations, and your impact at Arc’teryx. This range reflects market alignment and the scope of the role. Individual pay is determined by your skills, experience, and level of responsibility. 










We’re committed to fair, equitable, and competitive pay, reviewed regularly to ensure internal alignment and market relevance. Transparency is part of how we follow through on our commitments and how we honour the value you bring to our team. 










In addition to base pay, Arc’teryx supports people through benefits designed to sustain both work and life: 












Health & wellbeing - Extended health, dental, and vision coverage, including mental health support, fertility benefits, gender-affirming care and a 24/7 Employee Assistance Program (EAP).


Financial wellbeing - RRSP matching and eligibility for Arc'teryx Annual Incentive Plan and access to Employee Stock Purchase program (ESPP) where applicable.


Time & Flexibility - Paid time off, wellness time, and No Wasted Day program (dedicated paid days to get outside & explore).


Family support - Parental leave top-up and a nesting period for new parents.


Growth, community & gear - Professional development opportunities, Arc'teryx Academies (outdoor skill-building events), Employee Belonging Councils, and access to employee discounts and Pro Deals (exclusive discounted pricing on gear). 











 *Eligibility for certain programs may vary by role. 








Equal Opportunity




 




Arc’teryx is committed to actively creating and fostering a culture of inclusivity where voices are heard, people are seen, and values are respected. We care about the uniqueness of our applicants, employees, and guests, and we do so in a safe space fueled by curiosity and acceptance. 




 




Creating an inclusive workplace is connected to our core value Do Right while also having the added benefit of helping make our business better. We believe inclusion helps us to create a healthier, happier workplace, drive creativity and innovation, and reflect the communities where we operate.  




 




All applicants, employees, and guests can expect equality of opportunity and fair treatment in alignment with our values.




 




Leave it Better




We believe that the mountains transform us, that how we adventure matters, and that there’s always a better way. 




Join us in creating positive change in ourselves, our communities, and the world. 




 




Live it. Get out there - the mountains make us better 




Disruptive evolution. In pursuit of better. Always. 




Commit. We set bold objectives and see them through.

Title: Data Analyst II, Fraud
Description:
Job Description:

At Remitly, we believe everyone deserves the freedom to access, move, and manage their money wherever life takes them. Since 2011, we've tirelessly delivered on our promise to customers sending money globally, providing secure, simple, and reliable ways to manage their money, ensuring true peace of mind.

Whether it's supporting loved ones back home, growing a business across continents, or pursuing new opportunities abroad, we're not just here to move money— we're here to move our global customers forward.

We're looking for builders, reimaginers, and global thinkers who want to work at the intersection of technology, trust, and transformation. If that's you and you're ready to do the most meaningful work of your career—we invite you to join over 2,800 passionate Remitlians worldwide who are united by our vision to transform lives with trusted financial services that transcend borders

About the Role:

As a Data Analyst, Fraud, you will be part of Remitly's Identity & Trust Analytics Team. Your mission is to leverage analytical methods to identify, prevent, and mitigate fraud impacting our customers globally. You will develop a deep understanding of Remitly's fraud detection systems, regional risk profiles, and operational processes to improve fraud defenses while maintaining a seamless experience for legitimate customers. You'll partner with Product, Engineering, Business Management, and Compliance to customize and optimize fraud models and workflows, integrating data from legacy and new platforms. This hybrid role requires 2–3 days per week in the Vancouver office.

You Will:


Become an expert on Remitly's fraud data (e.g. chargebacks, promo abuse, scams, onboarding risk).
Build data-driven models and dashboards to analyze and monitor fraud exposure, balancing customer experience with operational efficiency.
Collect, normalize, and analyze data from legacy prebriefs, transaction logs, and onboarding systems to identify fraud trends and behavior anomalies.
Partner with Business Management and Product to continuously improve customer verification, KYC, KYB and compliance workflows.
Design and evaluate experiments and A/B tests to measure fraud control effectiveness and minimize false positives.
Develop and track risk metrics that quantify fraud exposure, policy compliance, and system performance.
Provide actionable insights and influence the development of fraud detection strategies, tools, and automation.


You Have:


3+ years in an analytics or fraud/risk analysis role within fintech, e-commerce, or digital payments.
Experience with fraud or trust analytics.
Bachelor's degree in mathematics, engineering, economics, or another quantitative discipline (or equivalent experience).
Proficiency with SQL and hands-on experience analyzing large datasets (Python, R, or statistical modeling experience preferred).
Strong understanding of fraud taxonomies (chargebacks, identity theft, scams, onboarding risk, and promo abuse).
Demonstrated ability to distill business problems, develop hypotheses, perform analysis, and communicate clear recommendations.
Excellent data storytelling and communication skills; ability to translate complex findings into business decisions.


Compensation Details. The starting base salary range for this position is $100,000 - $125000. In Canada, Remitly employees are shareholders in our Company and equity is part of our total compensation plan. Your recruiter can share more information about medical benefits offered, as well as other financial benefits and total compensation components offered with this role.

Our Benefits


Four weeks vacation
Health Benefits
Mental Health & Family Forming Benefits
RRSP plan with company match
Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)
Life Insurance & Disability
Continuing education and travel benefits


Our Connected Work Culture: Driving Innovation, Together

At Remitly, we believe that true innovation sparks when we come together. Our Connected Work Culture fosters dynamic in-person collaboration, where ideas ignite and challenging problems find solutions faster. For corporate team members, we have an in-office expectation of at least 50% of the time monthly, typically achieved by coming in three days a week. This creates a consistent, meaningful overlap that supports team norms and business needs. Managers also have the flexibility to set higher expectations based on their team's specific needs. These intentional in-office moments are vital for deepening relationships, fueling creativity, and ensuring your impact is felt where it matters most.

Remitly is an E-Verify Employer

At Remitly, we are dedicated to ensuring that our workplace offers equal employment opportunities to all employees and candidates, in full compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

Remitly is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Title: Enterprise Data Analyst
Description:
INTRODUCTION

At Specialty Program Group Canada (SPG Canada), we are insurance entrepreneurs united by a bold vision: to aggregate strengths and deliver unmatched value as Canada’s largest Delegated Underwriting Authority Enterprise (DUAE).




We exist to satisfy insurance needs in our target markets faster, better, and even where others might not. We do this by uniting leading underwriting talent and specialty insurance brands under one coast-to-coast platform, delivering speed, service, and expertise at a national scale.




But what truly sets us apart is our people. At SPG Canada, you will be part of a collaborative, growing team that values innovation, professional development, and shared success. Our culture is built on entrepreneurial thinking, open communication, and a strong sense of purpose. We believe in empowering our employees to take ownership, contribute ideas, and grow their careers within a supportive and dynamic environment.




Our legacy MGA brands include Cansure, i3 Underwriting, Totten Insurance Group, Beacon, Anderson McTague & Associates, Mode, and Avro. Our value to our partners is further enhanced through our in-house claims management team, Specialty Claims Canada.




Together, we are building more than a market leader — we are building a place where talented professionals can thrive.




SPGCanada.ca




OVERVIEW

We are looking for an Enterprise Data Analyst to join our Data & Intelligence team.




Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.




The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.




This role sits within the Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.

The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.




This role sits within the Data & Intelligence team, which is responsible for the data used by products delivered in partnership to the field. The Analyst will work cross-functionally to drive improvements in our Data-as-a-Product solutions and will support data testing, data quality, and data governance projects as directed.




This is a hybrid position based in our Vancouver, BC office.




RESPONSIBILITIES

In this position you will:

Support various data warehouse and platform enhancement initiatives — new subject areas, improved data quality, faster data availability, internal compliance, and simplified usage — contributing to the regular, iterative release of data within the SPGC ecosystem.
Liaise with IT and Operations teams to ensure technical and business solutions fully meet stakeholder needs for BI reporting, ad-hoc extracts, data science projects, and customer relationship management.
Support business, contractual, and compliance data needs by creating and maintaining reporting pipelines and delivering one-off reports to internal and external partners.
Perform data profiling; plan, execute, and review user acceptance test (UAT) cases. Establish minimum viable product and acceptance criteria for development sprints and undertake UAT for data-related projects.
Execute data projects assigned by your line manager, ensuring business requirements are properly understood and user stories are well crafted to enable efficient, accurate ETL/ELT development.
Develop and prioritize development stories, participate in Agile ceremonies, and contribute to after-action reviews for data projects.
Establish programmatic processes to proactively measure and manage data quality (ideally automated) using SQL and related languages; coordinate remediation of data defects and data quality problems.
Collaborate with the Data & Intelligence team to implement common data definitions and metadata documentation. Serve as an EDW and data-source subject matter expert.




QUALIFICATIONS AND CHARACTERISTICS

You’re a good fit if you have:

Bachelor’s degree in a related quantitative field, or 5 years of related insurance industry data experience.
Strong, hands-on SQL proficiency — query authoring, performance tuning, and data validation.

INTRODUCTION

At Specialty Program Group Canada (SPG Canada), we are insurance entrepreneurs united by a bold vision: to aggregate strengths and deliver unmatched value as Canada’s largest Delegated Underwriting Authority Enterprise (DUAE).


We exist to satisfy insurance needs in our target markets faster, better, and even where others might not. We do this by uniting leading underwriting talent and specialty insurance brands under one coast-to-coast platform, delivering speed, service, and expertise at a national scale.


But what truly sets us apart is our people. At SPG Canada, you will be part of a collaborative, growing team that values innovation, professional development, and shared success. Our culture is built on entrepreneurial thinking, open communication, and a strong sense of purpose. We believe in empowering our employees to take ownership, contribute ideas, and grow their careers within a supportive and dynamic environment.


Our legacy MGA brands include Cansure, i3 Underwriting, Totten Insurance Group, Beacon, Anderson McTague & Associates, Mode, and Avro. Our value to our partners is further enhanced through our in-house claims management team, Specialty Claims Canada.


Together, we are building more than a market leader — we are building a place where talented professionals can thrive.


SPGCanada.ca


OVERVIEW

We are looking for an Enterprise Data Analyst to join our Data & Intelligence team.


Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.


The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.


This role sits within the Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.

The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.


This role sits within the Data & Intelligence team, which is responsible for the data used by products delivered in partnership to the field. The Analyst will work cross-functionally to drive improvements in our Data-as-a-Product solutions and will support data testing, data quality, and data governance projects as directed.


This is a hybrid position based in our Vancouver, BC office.


RESPONSIBILITIES

In this position you will:

  • Support various data warehouse and platform enhancement initiatives — new subject areas, improved data quality, faster data availability, internal compliance, and simplified usage — contributing to the regular, iterative release of data within the SPGC ecosystem.
  • Liaise with IT and Operations teams to ensure technical and business solutions fully meet stakeholder needs for BI reporting, ad-hoc extracts, data science projects, and customer relationship management.
  • Support business, contractual, and compliance data needs by creating and maintaining reporting pipelines and delivering one-off reports to internal and external partners.
  • Perform data profiling; plan, execute, and review user acceptance test (UAT) cases. Establish minimum viable product and acceptance criteria for development sprints and undertake UAT for data-related projects.
  • Execute data projects assigned by your line manager, ensuring business requirements are properly understood and user stories are well crafted to enable efficient, accurate ETL/ELT development.
  • Develop and prioritize development stories, participate in Agile ceremonies, and contribute to after-action reviews for data projects.
  • Establish programmatic processes to proactively measure and manage data quality (ideally automated) using SQL and related languages; coordinate remediation of data defects and data quality problems.
  • Collaborate with the Data & Intelligence team to implement common data definitions and metadata documentation. Serve as an EDW and data-source subject matter expert.


QUALIFICATIONS AND CHARACTERISTICS

You’re a good fit if you have:

  • Bachelor’s degree in a related quantitative field, or 5 years of related insurance industry data experience.
  • Strong, hands-on SQL proficiency — query authoring, performance tuning, and data validation.
  • Microsoft Fabric experience (Google Cloud data architecture such as BigQuery accepted as an alternative platform background).
  • Strong, hands-on SQL and Python proficiency — query authoring, performance tuning, data validation, and transformation/automation work in Microsoft Fabric notebooks.
  • Power BI and DAX for reporting and dashboarding.
  • Practical exposure to data warehousing, business intelligence, data modeling, and master data management concepts.
  • Demonstrated SCRUM/Agile experience.
  • Understanding of data governance, data integration, and master data management best practices.
  • A consultative style that leverages both business and technical resources.

Strong assets (nice to haves):

  • Familiarity with the EPIC broker management system.
  • Exposure to NetSuite / NetSuite Analytics Warehouse.

By joining Specialty Program Group Canada, you’ll become part of a rapidly growing company focused on providing innovative insurance solutions to support independent brokers in Canada. As a member of our team, you’ll benefit from:

  • Dental care

Title: Decision Science Analyst
Description:
Come join Pattison Food Group Ltd., a Jim Pattison business, and Canada’s largest Western-based provider of food and health products.

Pattison Food Group Ltd. is a certified Great Place to Work® and consists of Save-On-Foods and other well-known grocery banners. With nearly 300 retail locations, we proudly employ nearly 30,000 team members.

We have an exciting opportunity for a full-time Decision Science Analyst to join our More Rewards team in Langley, BC.

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Customer Data & Analytics, you will be responsible for leveraging all aspects of data and analytics to support large-scale projects, experimentation, and continuous improvement initiatives that guide decision-making and deliver high-quality insights. In this role, you will support the development and management of customer data and insights to support the More Rewards Loyalty Program, Marketing, Retail Operations and Merchandising.

You will be responsible for:


Collaborating with stakeholders to understand business questions, processes and KPIs.
Formulating testable hypotheses aligned with business questions and conducting statistical experiments in a live environment.
Writing SQL scripts to automate data pipelines and workflows.
Using Python scripts in a Jupyter Notebook environment for exploratory data analysis, data wrangling and statistical testing.
Developing and maintaining dashboards and visualizations in Power BI to convey insights, report on KPIs and test results.
Learning cloud-based analytics environments and big data tools to build analytics workflows on large datasets.
Discovering unique and creative insights to identify new opportunities for driving business value.
Becoming an expert in the relevant datasets within your analytics domain.
Supporting the Data & Analytics team with key projects.



You have:


A degree in a STEM discipline with 3+ years of relevant experience in data and analytics.
Advanced Python coding skills and understanding of SQL.
Experience with additional programming languages is an asset.
Experience using experimental design principles such as A/B and multivariate testing and working with business stakeholders to formulate testable hypotheses.
An understanding of advanced analytics and data science methods, such as anomaly detection, forecasting, and supervised & unsupervised learning.
Working knowledge of cloud environments, such as Azure, GCP or AWS. Experience with big data tools such as Spark and Databricks is an asset.



You are:


A confident communicator, both verbally and written, with strong interpersonal skills and the ability to communicate a story with data and insights to non-technical stakeholders.
A problem solver with strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
Effective in task management and delivering high-quality work.
Capable of multitasking and thriving in a fast-paced environment.
Self-motivated with the ability to work independently and collaboratively.



What we offer:

In addition to a highly competitive salary, we offer a great range of benefits, a company pension plan, free parking, exclusive team member offers, and opportunities for development and career progression.

If you’re looking to join a team that puts people first, you’re in the right place. Apply online today!

Compensation Details:

$83,394.00 - $104,243.00

The compensation offered for this position will take into consideration location, education, skills, experience and other factors.

Title: Data Analyst 1
Description:
Come join Pattison Food Group Ltd., a Jim Pattison business, and Canada’s largest Western-based provider of food and health products.

Pattison Food Group Ltd. is a certified Great Place to Work® and consists of Save-On-Foods and other well-known grocery banners. With nearly 300 retail locations, we proudly employ nearly 30,000 team members.

We have an exciting opportunity for a full-time Data Analyst 1 to join our Data & Analytics team in Langley, BC.

Reporting to the Lead Data Engineer, the Data Analyst 1 role supports business decision-making through data analysis, reporting, and insight generation. This position works closely with business stakeholders to understand key questions, translate them into analytical approaches, and deliver clear, actionable insights.

This role contributes to a range of initiatives across areas such as customer analytics, loyalty, marketing, retail operations, and merchandising. Using large and complex datasets, the Data Analyst 1 helps identify trends, measure outcomes, support experimentation, and uncover opportunities to drive business value.

You will be responsible for: (Responsibilities)


Partnering with business stakeholders to understand priorities, business questions, processes, and KPIs.
Analyzing data to generate insights that support decision-making and continuous improvement.
Building reports, dashboards, and visualizations that communicate findings clearly and effectively to stakeholders.
Maintaining dashboards, reports, and other data assets in production to support their ongoing accuracy, reliability, and performance.
Writing SQL queries to extract, manipulate, and prepare data for analysis and reporting.
Using Python and notebook-based tools for exploratory analysis, data wrangling, and statistical work where needed.
Supporting the design, execution, and interpretation of experiments such as A/B testing and other hypothesis-driven analyses.
Validating, troubleshooting, and improving data used in reporting and analysis.
Working with large datasets in cloud-based or modern analytics environments.
Supporting the governance, administration, and cost optimization of data visualization and reporting tools.
Collaborating with data and cross-functional partners to support reporting, analysis, and business needs.
Identifying trends, opportunities, and actionable recommendations through thoughtful analysis.
Building knowledge of core datasets, reporting logic, and business context within the assigned analytics domain.
Supporting broader Data & Analytics team priorities and cross-functional projects as required.



You have: (Requirements)


A degree in a relevant field such as statistics, mathematics, computer science, engineering, economics, or another quantitative discipline.
3+ years of relevant background in data analytics, business analytics, reporting, or a related field.
Working knowledge of SQL for querying and transforming data.
Familiarity with Power BI, Databricks AI/BI, Tableau, or similar visualization and reporting tools.
Ability to build dashboards or reports that support business decision-making.
Exposure to large or complex datasets.
Working knowledge of Python for data analysis is preferred.
Exposure to experimentation, test design, or statistical analysis is an asset.
Familiarity with data modeling or data quality validation is an asset.
Exposure to cloud data environments such as Azure, AWS, or GCP is an asset.
Experience working with Databricks is an asset.



You are: (Competencies)


Able to translate business needs into practical data solutions.
Able to translate data into clear, practical insights for non-technical stakeholders.
An effective written and verbal communicator.
Detail-oriented and committed to quality.
Able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Self-motivated and comfortable working both independently and collaboratively.
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn new tools, datasets, and business contexts.



What we offer:

In addition to a highly competitive salary, we offer a great range of benefits, a company pension plan, free parking, exclusive team member offers, and opportunities for development and career progression.

If you’re looking to join a team that puts people first, you’re in the right place. Apply online today!
